meta.generated_by: Synthesis of three independent LLM conformance analyses with direct source re-verification
meta.method: Three independent analyses (git branches pr/main/mpi-conformance-analysis-1/-2/-3, referenced as b1/b2/b3) were compared finding-by-finding. Where all three agreed, the result was accepted. Every conflict, and every finding unique to one reviewer, was re-investigated directly against the Open MPI source at the baseline commit and against the MPI-5.0 specification PDF / machine-readable API list. Symbol presence was recomputed directly from ompi/include/mpi.h.in and the Fortran sources rather than inherited.

status_legend.SUPPORTED: Available in the default build/runtime (a non-default --disable-* flag may remove it; see gate).
status_legend.CONDITIONAL: Available only via a non-default optional dependency (optional library, explicitly-enabled configure flag, or optional compiler feature); see gate.
status_legend.PARTIAL: Present but incomplete or not fully standard-conformant.
status_legend.UNSUPPORTED: Required behavior genuinely absent, with no known in-flight work.
status_legend.MISSING_INFLIGHT: Absent on main but in-flight via a known PR/branch (ABI PR #13280; OMPIO MPI_Info branch, issue #13367).
status_legend.NA: Not applicable (advice-to-implementors text, or an explicitly optional feature).

agreement_legend.unanimous: All three reviewers that addressed the item agree (after vocabulary normalization).
agreement_legend.conflict: Reviewers disagreed; resolved here by direct source investigation.
agreement_legend.unique: Only one reviewer raised it; validated/​corrected here.
agreement_legend.partial-overlap: Two reviewers overlapped; the third was silent or scoped it elsewhere.

headline: Open MPI main implements the large majority of MPI-5.0, but **self-reports as MPI 4.1** (``MPI_VERSION``/``MPI_SUBVERSION`` and ``MPI_Get_version`` return 4/1; VERSION:23-24), so it does not yet advertise MPI-5.0 conformance.

**C API surface:** 537 of 567 MPI-5.0 procedures are declared in the normal ``mpi.h``. The 28 absent are exactly the MPI ABI surface, in-flight via PR #13280; ``MPI_F_sync_reg``/``MPI_Sizeof`` are Fortran-only.
**mpi_f08 gap:** the six ``MPI_Status_{get,set}_{source,tag,error}`` procedures have C bindings but no ``mpi_f08`` binding.
**Application Binary Interface (Chapter 20):** entirely absent on main; in-flight via PR #13280.
**Behavioral requirements:** 399 reconciled across all chapters - 327 supported (83.6% of applicable), 12 conditional, 23 partial, 4 unsupported, 25 missing-but-in-flight, 8 N/A.

**Regression flagged:** ``MPI_Testall`` with a real ``statuses[]`` array returns ``MPI_ERR_IN_STATUS`` even on full success - an inverted condition introduced 2025-10-15 by commit 38a7fbb837 (req_test.c:252). ``MPI_Waitall`` is unaffected.

c_procedures.note: The 28 absent C procedures are exactly the MPI ABI surface (6 MPI_Abi_* + 22 *_toint/*_fromint), in-flight via PR #13280. MPI_F_sync_reg and MPI_Sizeof are Fortran-only and correctly absent from C.

f08_procedures.note: The 6 MPI_Status_{get,set}_{source,tag,error} procedures have C bindings but no mpi_f08 binding (verified: no source under ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/). Confirmed by reviewers b2 and b3.

constants.note: All MPI-5.0 datatype, op, error-class, comparison, and info-related named constants are present. Only the two listed MPI_T error classes are genuinely absent (verified absent from mpi.h.in; all three reviewers concur). Extended-precision Fortran KIND constants (MPI_REAL16, MPI_COMPLEX32, MPI_INTEGER16, ...) are present only when the Fortran compiler supports those kinds (CONDITIONAL).

chapters.title: Introduction to MPI
chapters.tldr: Chapter 1 is descriptive (it inventories what the Standard includes). Open MPI implements the large majority of the listed components. Two substantive caveats: (1) the build self-reports MPI 4.1 via MPI_VERSION/MPI_SUBVERSION and MPI_Get_version (VERSION:23-24), so it does not yet *advertise* MPI-5.0 even though most MPI-5.0 features are present; (2) the MPI Application Binary Interface (the single largest MPI-5.0 addition, listed in 1.13) is absent on main and in-flight via PR #13280.

findings.topic: Implementation advertises its supported MPI standard level (MPI_VERSION / MPI_SUBVERSION macros + MPI_Get_version)
findings.evidence: VERSION:23-24 (mpi_standard_version=4, mpi_standard_subversion=1); config/autogen_found_items.m4:12-13 (MPI_VERSION_NUM=4, MPI_SUBVERSION_NUM=1); ompi/include/mpi.h.in:211-212 templated from these; ompi/mpi/c/get_version.c.in returns those macros
findings.adjudication_note: The macros and MPI_Get_version work correctly and are thread-safe, but the configured values are (4,1), not (5,0). So a strict standards check (e.g. #if MPI_VERSION>=5) fails and the library advertises MPI-4.1 conformance. b1's PARTIAL is correct; b2's SUPPORTED only checked that the mechanism exists. This is the single most visible 'not yet MPI-5.0' signal in the tree.

findings.topic: MPI Application Binary Interface listed as an included Standard component (1.13)
findings.gate: in-flight: PR #13280
findings.evidence: No MPI_Abi_*, MPI_ABI_VERSION/SUBVERSION, MPI_ERR_ABI, or *_toint/*_fromint converters in ompi/include/mpi.h.in (see Chapter 20)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 marked UNSUPPORTED only because its rubric lacked an in-flight bucket (it still tagged PR #13280). Per the in-flight tiebreaker the whole ABI surface is MISSING_INFLIGHT, not UNSUPPORTED.

findings.topic: Component inventory of 1.13 (point-to-point, collective, RMA, I/O, dynamic processes, datatypes, topologies, tools, etc.) maps to subsystems present in the tree
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/{pml,coll,osc,io,topo,part}, ompi/mpi/{c,fortran}, ompi/mpi/tool — every listed component has a corresponding subsystem
findings.adjudication_note: Descriptive statement; all listed major components are present by default (details in the per-chapter pages).

findings.topic: Canonical data representation (external32) for MPI_Pack_external/Unpack_external and MPI I/O interoperability is included (1.15)
findings.evidence: opal/datatype/opal_datatype_external32.* ; ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_external32.c:106 (fixed big-endian IEEE arch id); pack_external/unpack_external bindings present
findings.adjudication_note: external32 is genuinely implemented (see Chapter 5). The MPI I/O 'external32' datarep is also accepted (see Chapter 14), though the related 'internal' datarep is rejected.

findings.topic: Backward compatibility: any valid MPI-4.1 program is a valid MPI-5.0 program
findings.evidence: MPI-4.x symbol surface fully present; deprecated interfaces retained (see Chapter 16)
findings.adjudication_note: Valid unique observation from b2; the MPI-4.1 API surface is preserved (deprecated-not-removed), so source compatibility holds.

findings.topic: Conformance to base Standard absent explicit side-document opt-ins (1.14)
findings.evidence: No side-document deviations enabled by default
findings.adjudication_note: Advice/meta statement; nothing in the default build opts into non-base-standard behavior.

chapters.title: MPI Terms and Conventions
chapters.tldr: Chapter 2 is definitional/conventional and carries no assigned MPI procedures or constants of its own; conformance is assessed semantically against cross-cutting requirements. Open MPI satisfies almost every Chapter-2 requirement by default: opaque-object handle types (C typedefs + USE mpi_f08 BIND(C) derived types with a single INTEGER MPI_VAL and overloaded .EQ./.NE./==//=), invalid-handle (*_NULL) and reserved-sentinel constants, MPI_Aint/MPI_Offset/MPI_Count C types with matching Fortran KIND parameters, the large-count _c procedure family, the AINT_ADD/DIFF-only macro rule, default ERRORS_ARE_FATAL on comms/wins with ERRORS_RETURN on files, the three predefined error handlers, the weak-progress guarantee via in-call opal_progress() polling, MPI_BOTTOM/MPI_IN_PLACE/MPI_STATUS_IGNORE/MPI_PROC_NULL buffer-and-status conventions, the MPI_THREAD_SINGLE..MULTIPLE compliance model, COMM_DUP info non-propagation, and the signal-usage documentation obligation. Two requirements are CONDITIONAL: (1) the MPI-1 constructs Table 2.1 marks 'Removed since MPI-3.0' are compiled out of mpi.h by default and source-usable only with the non-default --enable-mpi1-compatibility (the standard permits omitting them, so this is optional); (2) the MPI-4/5 rule that handle-less errors raise on MPI_COMM_SELF is implemented but NOT the shipped default -- Open MPI ships ompi_mpi_compat_mpi3=true, raising such errors on MPI_COMM_WORLD (MPI-3 behavior) unless the user sets the non-default MCA var mpi_compat_mpi3=0. Strong/asynchronous progress is explicitly not required by the standard (NA). No Chapter-2 behavior is missing or in-flight; the two in-flight PRs (ABI ch.20, ompio-info ch.13) do not bear on this chapter.

findings.topic: Opaque objects accessed via typed handles; distinct C handle type per category; assignment and equality comparison
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:449-468 (per-category C typedefs e.g. MPI_Comm/MPI_Datatype/MPI_Request); ompi/communicator/comm.c:248,275,288 (OBJ_RETAIN/OBJ_RELEASE reference counting realizes 'as if copied' shared-reference semantics)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. C handles are pointers, so assignment and == comparison are inherent; opaque objects are reference-counted OPAL base objects deallocated via OBJ_RELEASE with pending ops completing before free.

findings.topic: USE mpi_f08 handles are BIND(C) derived types with a single INTEGER MPI_VAL; .EQ./.NE./==//= overloaded for handle comparison
findings.gate: --disable-mpi-fortran removes f08 bindings (default ON)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi/mpi-types.F90.in:45-212 (BIND(C) derived types with INTEGER :: MPI_VAL and OPERATOR(.EQ.)/(.NE.)/(==)/(/=) overloads)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 agree; b3 did not raise it separately. f08 derived-type handles with overloaded equality are present and generated by default; mpi_f08 is built by default (gate note only).

findings.topic: Invalid-handle (MPI_*_NULL) constants and predefined static handles per object type; predefined objects must not be user-freed
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:984-999 (MPI_*_NULL); :986-987,1305 (deallocate sets handle to invalid value); OMPI_PREDEFINED_GLOBAL macro for predefined static handles
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. All *_NULL invalid-handle constants and predefined static handles are defined; free operations reset the user's handle to the predefined NULL value.

findings.topic: Named-constant / reserved-sentinel conventions (MPI_BOTTOM, MPI_IN_PLACE, MPI_UNWEIGHTED, MPI_WEIGHTS_EMPTY, MPI_BUFFER_AUTOMATIC, MPI_ERRCODES_IGNORE, MPI_ANY_TAG/SOURCE, MPI_PROC_NULL, MPI_UNDEFINED) as distinct out-of-range addresses/integers
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:574-584 (MPI_UNWEIGHTED=(int*)2, MPI_WEIGHTS_EMPTY=(int*)3, MPI_BOTTOM=(void*)0, MPI_IN_PLACE=(void*)1, MPI_BUFFER_AUTOMATIC=(void*)4, MPI_ERRCODES_IGNORE=(int*)0; negative-int constants for ANY_TAG/ANY_SOURCE/PROC_NULL/UNDEFINED)
findings.adjudication_note: b3-unique, valid. Reserved sentinels are distinct out-of-range addresses/integers usable in initializers as required (integer-constant-expression constants are #defines).

findings.topic: Special Fortran constants that cannot be PARAMETERs (MPI_BOTTOM, MPI_IN_PLACE, MPI_STATUS_IGNORE, etc.) implemented as predefined static/COMMON-block variables; all other named constants are PARAMETERs
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpif-f08-types.h:11-20 (predefined BIND(C) variables); ompi/mpi/fortran/base/constants.h:71-78 (COMMON-block alias for special constants); ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/mod/mpi-f08-types.F90:35-67 (PARAMETER handles)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 agree (b2 verified representatively for STATUS_IGNORE, MPI_BOTTOM, MPI_BUFFER_AUTOMATIC). Handle constants are PARAMETER; the ten special constants use the address-of-predefined-variable mechanism the standard's advice to implementors prescribes.

findings.topic: MPI_Aint / MPI_Offset / MPI_Count C types and matching Fortran INTEGER KIND parameters with identical width across languages; MPI_Count wide enough for int/MPI_Aint/MPI_Offset
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:449-451,565 (MPI_Aint/MPI_Offset/MPI_Count typedefs; MPI_Count = OMPI_MPI_COUNT_TYPE wide integer); ompi/include/mpif-config.h.in:43-48 (MPI_ADDRESS_KIND/MPI_OFFSET_KIND/MPI_COUNT_KIND PARAMETERs derived from the same configure-time sizes)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 agree. C typedefs and Fortran KIND PARAMETERs are derived from the same configure-time sizes, guaranteeing matching width; MPI_Count is a wide integer comparable to the plain-int MPI_UNDEFINED (-32766).

findings.topic: Large-count support via '_c'-suffixed C procedures (MPI_Send_c, MPI_Recv_c, MPI_Get_count_c, MPI_Type_contiguous_c, ...) and polymorphic mpi_f08 interfaces
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in (MPI_Send_c, MPI_Recv_c, MPI_Get_count_c, MPI_Type_contiguous_c and PMPI_* variants declared); f08 large-count interfaces generated under use-mpi-f08
findings.adjudication_note: b2-unique, valid. The full set of _c large-count C entry points is declared and the f08 large-count interfaces are generated; this is the chapter's large-count convention surface.

findings.topic: Only MPI_AINT_ADD/PMPI_AINT_ADD/MPI_AINT_DIFF/PMPI_AINT_DIFF may be implemented as C macros; no other MPI routine is a macro
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:1443-1446 (exactly the four permitted symbols defined as macros; real functions also exist for the profiling interface)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 agree. Exactly the four permitted symbols are macros; other function-like #defines (mpi.h.in:4161-4169) are compile-time sentinels for MPI-3.0-removed routines, not callable MPI functions.

findings.topic: MPI-1 constructs Table 2.1 marks 'Removed since MPI-3.0' (MPI_UB/LB, MPI_Address, MPI_Type_hvector/hindexed/struct/extent, MPI_Errhandler_create/get/set, *_INTEGER combiners) may be provided for backward compatibility but are not required
findings.gate: configure flag --enable-mpi1-compatibility (default disabled; OMPI_ENABLE_MPI1_COMPAT / OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS)
findings.evidence: config/ompi_configure_options.m4:179-192 (--enable-mpi1-compatibility default disabled -> ompi_mpi1_support=0); ompi/include/mpi.h.in:407-408 (OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS = !(OMPI_ENABLE_MPI1_COMPAT||OMPI_BUILDING)); :809-846,1259-1274,4080-4091 (combiners/MPI_UB/MPI_LB/removed typedefs gated, else removed-symbol static assert)
findings.adjudication_note: b2-unique, verified and confirmed. By default these removed names are compiled out of mpi.h (or expand to a removed-symbol static_assert on C11+); --enable-mpi1-compatibility restores them. The standard permits omitting them (may-be-provided-not-required), so default-omitted-with-optional-flag = CONDITIONAL. The .c impls remain linked for ABI but are undeclared by default, so this does not make them source-usable. Not in-flight (PR #13280 adds only ABI MPI_Abi/toint/fromint symbols).

findings.topic: Deprecated-but-not-removed constructs (MPI_KEYVAL_*, MPI_ATTR_*, MPI_INFO_GET, MPI_SIZEOF, MPI_TYPE_SIZE_X, mpif.h) remain part of the standard and must be present
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:3943-3957,3986-3987 (present with __mpi_interface_deprecated__ warnings, NOT gated by the MPI1-compat flag)
findings.adjudication_note: b2-unique, valid. Deprecated (not removed) constructs are present by default with deprecation warnings, distinct from the removed-symbol set; required to be present and they are.

findings.topic: By default a detected error aborts the computation, EXCEPT file operations (comms/windows default ERRORS_ARE_FATAL; files default ERRORS_RETURN); three predefined handlers ARE_FATAL/RETURN/ABORT exist
findings.evidence: ompi/communicator/comm_init.c:467 (comm default ERRORS_ARE_FATAL); ompi/win/win.c:443 (win default fatal); ompi/file/file.c:261 (file default ERRORS_RETURN); ompi/errhandler/errhandler_predefined.c:68-126 (ARE_FATAL->abort), :128-186 (ABORT), :218-251 (RETURN no-op)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. Default comm/win handler is fatal-abort; default file handler is return, matching the standard's file carve-out. All three predefined handlers exist with comm/file/win/instance variants and are routed via ompi_errhandler_invoke.

findings.topic: Errors not attributable to a specific MPI object are raised on MPI_COMM_SELF (World Model, MPI-4/5 behavior)
findings.gate: MCA var mpi_compat_mpi3=0 (default is true -> MPI-3 MPI_COMM_WORLD behavior)
findings.evidence: ompi/errhandler/errhandler_invoke.c:54 (comm = ompi_mpi_compat_mpi3 ? &ompi_mpi_comm_world.comm : &ompi_mpi_comm_self.comm); ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_params.c:86,366-372 (ompi_mpi_compat_mpi3 = true by default; settable via mpi_compat_mpi3)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT resolved in b3's favor with source proof. b1 and b2 both claimed COMM_SELF is the default (both cited the COMM_SELF behavior as supported/default), but the shipped default is ompi_mpi_compat_mpi3=true, which routes handle-less errors to MPI_COMM_WORLD (MPI-3 behavior). The MPI-5-conformant MPI_COMM_SELF behavior is reachable ONLY by setting the non-default MCA var mpi_compat_mpi3=0. This is default-OFF-enableable (inverse of default-on-removable), directly parallel to --enable-mpi1-compatibility, so CONDITIONAL per the framework's b3-normalization rule. Not missing/in-flight; the code path exists.

findings.topic: Errors before MPI_INIT / after MPI_FINALIZE / Sessions-only (no associated object, COMM_SELF uninitialized) routed to the launch-time initial error handler
findings.evidence: ompi/errhandler/errhandler_invoke.c:66-75 (else-branch: lazily inits and invokes ompi_initial_error_handler when MPI state is outside INIT_COMPLETED..FINALIZE_PAST_COMM_SELF); ompi/errhandler/errhandler.c:102-136 / errhandler.h:171-190 (ompi_initial_error_handler populated from launch keys)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 agree; this is the genuinely-conformant sub-path distinct from the post-init COMM_SELF/COMM_WORLD default above. Before MPI_INIT/after MPI_FINALIZE/Sessions-only, errors go to the initial error handler selected during launch. Split out as its own SUPPORTED topic to avoid contradicting the CONDITIONAL World-Model row.

findings.topic: Weak-progress guarantee: decoupled MPI activities eventually execute during blocked procedures and repeated flag=false test calls
findings.evidence: opal/runtime/opal_progress.c:58-65 (opal_progress() callback engine pumped from blocking/test paths; opal_progress_event_flag = OPAL_EVLOOP_ONCE|OPAL_EVLOOP_NONBLOCK); driven by pml/ob1, btl, etc.
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. The mandated eventual-execution (weak progress) is provided by default via in-call opal_progress() polling inside MPI calls and test loops. This is the binding 2.9 requirement and it is met.

findings.topic: Strong / asynchronous progress (dedicated progress thread or NIC offload)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/mtl/ofi/mtl_ofi_component.c:183,198 (control/data_progress default MTL_OFI_PROG_UNSPEC -> FI_PROGRESS_AUTO NOT requested by default); opal/runtime/opal_progress_threads.c:139 (progress-thread machinery; sole non-default caller is btl/usnic)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT (b2 CONDITIONAL vs b3 NA), resolved to NA. The standard EXPLICITLY does not require strong/asynchronous progress (2.9), so per the taxonomy 'optional feature MPI explicitly permits omitting' = NA. b2's CONDITIONAL over-classifies an above-baseline optional capability as a gated requirement. The capability does exist only via non-default transport/build/MCA-param choices, but since the standard does not mandate it, its absence-by-default is not a conformance matter.

findings.topic: Implementation documents which signals it uses; strongly recommended (soft) to avoid SIGALRM/SIGFPE/SIGIO; may prohibit MPI calls in signal handlers
findings.evidence: opal/runtime/opal_params_core.c:137-176 (default intercept list SIGABRT,SIGBUS,SIGFPE,SIGSEGV exposed as the user-settable opal_signal MCA var with full help text; handler installed only if none already present); SIGALRM and SIGIO are NOT intercepted
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT (b1 PARTIAL vs b2 SUPPORTED), resolved to SUPPORTED. The BINDING requirement of 2.10.2 is 'document which signals it uses' -- met via the opal_signal MCA var and its help text. The SIGALRM/SIGFPE/SIGIO avoidance is explicitly 'strongly recommended' (soft, non-binding). Open MPI does intercept SIGFPE by default, deviating only from non-binding advice, and even that is mitigated: installed only when no handler exists, fully documented, and user-overridable. SIGALRM/SIGIO are not touched. Penalizing a soft recommendation as if it were a 'shall' (b1's PARTIAL) over-states the gap; the mandatory obligation is satisfied.

findings.topic: MPI_BOTTOM and MPI_IN_PLACE buffer conventions honored where buffer args are expected
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/coll/base/coll_base_allreduce.c:72,154,167 (MPI_IN_PLACE substitutes rbuf for sbuf); MPI_BOTTOM=(void*)0 used as base address with absolute-address datatypes built via MPI_Get_address
findings.adjudication_note: b3-unique, valid. MPI_IN_PLACE is honored at the collective/binding layer across coll components and reduce/scan/gather/scatter; MPI_BOTTOM is the base for absolute-address datatypes. Both deliver the 2.3 rule that these 'constants' may be passed where buffer args are expected.

findings.topic: MPI_STATUS_IGNORE / MPI_STATUSES_IGNORE / MPI_ERRCODES_IGNORE NULL-status convention (skip status writeback)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:1002-1006 (MPI_STATUS(ES)_IGNORE NULL pointers); ompi/mpi/c/recv.c.in:74,83 (status writeback skipped when status == MPI_STATUS_IGNORE); array form handled in wait/req_wait paths; MPI_ERRCODES_IGNORE=(int*)0 recognized by spawn paths
findings.adjudication_note: b3-unique, valid. Completion paths test against the IGNORE sentinels and skip status writeback, delivering the 2.5.2 NULL-status convention.

findings.topic: MPI_PROC_NULL communication semantics: sends/recvs to/from PROC_NULL are no-ops returning MPI_SUCCESS with empty-request status on receive
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/recv.c.in:82-93 (MPI_PROC_NULL source short-circuits; OMPI_COPY_STATUS fills empty-request status source=PROC_NULL/tag=ANY_TAG/count=0); matching PROC_NULL short-circuits in send/isend/irecv
findings.adjudication_note: b3-unique, valid. PROC_NULL communication is a no-op with the standard empty-request status on receive.

findings.topic: Thread-compliance model: MPI_THREAD_SINGLE..FUNNELED..SERIALIZED..MULTIPLE monotonic levels; MPI_Init_thread returns the provided level
findings.evidence: ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_init.c:309-329 (ompi_mpi_thread_level() returns provided level; sets ompi_mpi_thread_multiple for MPI_THREAD_MULTIPLE), :278-301 (env override); opal/mca/threads/thread_usage.h:69, condition.h:63 (opal_using_threads()/OPAL_THREAD_LOCK gating; pthreads/argobots/qthreads backends)
findings.adjudication_note: b3-unique, valid. All four monotonic levels exist; MPI_THREAD_MULTIPLE support is real in a default (pthreads) build. Per-transport correctness is a QoS matter, not a Chapter-2 convention gap.

findings.topic: MPI_COMM_DUP / MPI_COMM_IDUP no longer propagate input-comm info hints (§18.1.1 semantic change)
findings.evidence: ompi/communicator/comm.c:1597-1600 (ompi_comm_dup() calls ompi_comm_dup_with_info(comm, NULL, ...) so no info is copied from the source comm; info applied only when explicitly passed via *_with_info)
findings.adjudication_note: b3-unique, valid and unconditional. COMM_DUP/IDUP do not propagate source-comm info, matching the MPI-4/5 change; conformant in any build (distinct from the config-gated handle-less-error change tracked above).

findings.topic: Basic runtime routines (printf, malloc, write) operate independently after MPI_INIT and before MPI_FINALIZE; per-process programs complete regardless of COMM_WORLD size
findings.evidence: ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_init.c (no MPI-specific serialization/collective coupling of libc I/O or allocation)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 agree. Satisfied by construction: Open MPI imposes no collective coupling on basic libc runtime routines; this independence is inherent, not a disableable feature.

chapters.title: Point-to-Point Communication
chapters.tldr: Open MPI's main branch conforms broadly to MPI-5.0 Chapter 3. All four send modes (standard/buffered/synchronous/ready), blocking and nonblocking, persistent requests, Isendrecv(_replace), wildcards (MPI_ANY_SOURCE/MPI_ANY_TAG), MPI_PROC_NULL no-op semantics, probe/matched-probe (Mprobe/Improbe/Mrecv/Imrecv + MPI_MESSAGE_NO_PROC), cancellation, multiple-completion, and the full MPI-5.0 buffer-policy overhaul (per-process/per-comm/per-session Bsend buffers with comm>session>process precedence, MPI_BUFFER_AUTOMATIC, and the new {Buffer,Comm,Session}_{attach,detach,flush,iflush}_buffer procedures) are present in C and f08 by default. Three genuine deviations were verified: (1) the MPI-4.0 status accessor procedures MPI_Status_{get,set}_{source,tag,error} have C bindings but NO mpi_f08 binding (six procedures); (2) MPI_Get_count_c (the big-count variant) inherits the int-path INT_MAX overflow check from the shared template and wrongly returns MPI_UNDEFINED for counts above INT_MAX that fit in MPI_Count; (3) a regression on main (commit 38a7fbb837) inverts the success/error condition in MPI_Testall (only) in its non-MPI_STATUSES_IGNORE branch, so a successful MPI_Testall with a real statuses[] array returns MPI_ERR_IN_STATUS and a failed one is masked; MPI_Waitall and MPI_Testsome are unaffected. Heterogeneous representation conversion (3.3.2) is CONDITIONAL on --enable-heterogeneous (default off). Send-cancel-as-no-op and synchronous iflush are conformant QoI choices, not gaps.

findings.topic: Blocking standard-mode send/recv (MPI_Send/MPI_Recv): envelope matching on (source,tag,comm), MPI_ANY_SOURCE/MPI_ANY_TAG wildcards, count/datatype semantics, truncation detection
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/send.c.in; ompi/mpi/c/recv.c.in (MCA_PML_CALL send/recv into ob1); ompi/mca/pml/ob1 matching engine
findings.adjudication_note: Core p2p; unanimous and verified via the C wrappers and ob1 PML default path.

findings.topic: Three additional send modes: buffered (Bsend), synchronous (Ssend, nonlocal rendezvous), ready (Rsend), blocking and nonblocking
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/bsend.c.in (SEND_BUFFERED); ssend.c.in (SEND_SYNCHRONOUS); rsend.c.in (SEND_READY); plus isend/ibsend/issend/irsend.c.in. ob1 honors all four modes (pml_ob1_isend.c).
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous; all four modes present in C and f08.

findings.topic: MPI_TAG_UB >= 32767 requirement
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/pml/ob1/pml_ob1.c:96 (.pml_max_tag = INT_MAX)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; verified. ob1 advertises pml_max_tag = INT_MAX, far exceeding the 32767 floor.

findings.topic: Return status semantics: MPI_SOURCE/MPI_TAG/MPI_ERROR fields and MPI_Get_count decode (MPI_UNDEFINED on non-divisible/overflow; 0 on zero-size datatype)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:478-480 (status fields); ompi/mpi/c/get_count.c.in:61-73 (size==0->0; non-divisible/overflow->MPI_UNDEFINED; else int)
findings.adjudication_note: Base int-count MPI_Get_count is fully conformant (b1 and b3 agree). The _c big-count variant has a separate deviation tracked as its own topic below. The C bindings and behavior are present and conformant; however the six MPI_Status_{get,set}_{source,tag,error} procedures have NO mpi_f08 binding (a genuine gap counted once in Chapter 19, Language Bindings).

findings.topic: MPI_Get_count_c (big-count variant) overflow handling: must return true MPI_Count up to count-type max, not cap at INT_MAX
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/get_count_generated.c:93-131 (MPI_Get_count_c, OUT param MPI_Count*), specifically lines 122-126: still checks 'internal_count > ((size_t) INT_MAX) -> MPI_UNDEFINED' and casts '(int)internal_count'. Inherited verbatim from the single template get_count.c.in:66-70 via the bigcount generator.
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b3; VERIFIED in generated source. For a received element count above INT_MAX (~2.1e9) that fits in MPI_Count, MPI_Get_count_c wrongly returns MPI_UNDEFINED instead of the true count, and the (int) cast truncates. The _c variant exists precisely to support such counts, so this is a real conformance deviation. Narrow edge case (>2.1G-element single receive); one-line fix (use count-type max on the _c path). Present-but-wrong, not in-flight -> PARTIAL.

findings.topic: MPI_STATUS_IGNORE / MPI_STATUSES_IGNORE accepted wherever status is an OUT argument
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in (sentinel constants); recv.c.in:83 and req_test.c:235 branch on MPI_STATUS(ES)_IGNORE
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; verified the IGNORE sentinels are honored in recv and multiple-completion paths.

findings.topic: Blocking send-receive (MPI_Sendrecv) and in-place replace (MPI_Sendrecv_replace)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/sendrecv.c.in; ompi/mpi/c/sendrecv_replace.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: Agreed by b1/b2; present in C and f08.

findings.topic: Type-matching rules: identical-name match, MPI_BYTE matches any byte, MPI_PACKED matches any type (homogeneous case)
findings.evidence: opal/datatype type matching (homogeneous); ompi/mpi/c/send.c.in OMPI_CHECK_DATATYPE_FOR_SEND
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; type matching is a runtime property of the opal datatype engine, conformant in the default homogeneous build.

findings.topic: Data/representation conversion across heterogeneous environments (different binary representations / endianness)
findings.gate: --enable-heterogeneous (OPAL_ENABLE_HETEROGENEOUS_SUPPORT, default 0)
findings.evidence: config/opal_configure_options.m4:360-374 (default disabled); ompi/proc/proc.c:267-274 (OMPI_ARCH published to modex only under #if OPAL_ENABLE_HETEROGENEOUS_SUPPORT) and proc.c:781-795 (differing peer arch -> OMPI_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED + 'heterogeneous-support-unavailable' in non-hetero build); opal/datatype/opal_convertor.c (byte-swap/conversion engaged only when remoteArch != opal_local_arch)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous CONDITIONAL; this is the framework's named example. Conversion behavior is genuinely gated behind the non-default --enable-heterogeneous flag. Default homogeneous build is conformant (no conversion needed) but does not provide cross-representation conversion; a true arch mismatch is refused rather than mis-converted.

findings.topic: Ordering guarantee: messages are non-overtaking (single-threaded determinism), progress, and (no) fairness
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/pml/ob1 matching engine (FIFO matching per source/tag/comm)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; the unconditional single-threaded non-overtaking guarantee holds in the default build.

findings.topic: Multithreaded ordering relaxation / concurrent send-recv from distinct threads under MPI_THREAD_MULTIPLE
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1, which marked it CONDITIONAL on MPI_THREAD_MULTIPLE. Corrected to SUPPORTED: requesting a thread level via MPI_Init_thread is the standard RUNTIME mechanism, not a build/library gate. There is no non-default configure flag gating thread support (grep of config/ for an enable-thread-multiple option returns nothing; the legacy --enable-mpi-thread-multiple was removed and THREAD_MULTIPLE is compiled in by default). The framework's CONDITIONAL is reserved for configure-flag/optional-library/compiler-feature gates, which this is not.
findings.evidence: config/ has no --enable-mpi-thread-multiple / thread-level configure gate (grep returns no enable-thread option); thread level negotiated at MPI_Init_thread; ompi/mca/pml/ob1 thread-safe paths compiled in by default

findings.topic: Per-process buffered-mode buffer (MPI_Buffer_attach/detach) with BSEND_OVERHEAD accounting and circular allocation
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/buffer_attach.c.in; buffer_detach.c.in; ompi/mca/pml/base/pml_base_bsend.c (mca_pml_base_bsend_attach/detach, BASE_BSEND_BUF)
findings.adjudication_note: Agreed by b1/b2; classic per-process Bsend buffering present.

findings.topic: MPI-5.0 buffer-policy overhaul: per-communicator and per-session Bsend buffers ({Comm,Session}_{attach,detach}_buffer) with precedence comm > session > process
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/comm_attach_buffer.c.in (COMM_BSEND_BUF); session_attach_buffer.c.in (SESSION_BSEND_BUF); ompi/mca/pml/base/pml_base_bsend.c:87+ (mca_pml_bsend_buffer_get implements comm-then-session-then-process precedence per MPI-5 §3.6); pml_base_bsend.h (BASE/COMM/SESSION enum)
findings.adjudication_note: Agreed by b1/b2; the new MPI-5.0 attach/detach buffer procedures and the comm>session>process precedence are implemented in C and f08. (b3 noted a stale uncompiled comm_buffer_attach.c.in with an obsolete 2-arg call -- dead code, not referenced in Makefile.am, no runtime effect; excluded as NA.)

findings.topic: MPI_BUFFER_AUTOMATIC: library auto-sizes buffering for buffered-mode sends at any level (C and Fortran sentinel)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:578 (MPI_BUFFER_AUTOMATIC); ompi/include/mpif-f08-types.h / mpif-sentinels.h (Fortran sentinels); pml_base_bsend.c self-growing allocator path
findings.adjudication_note: Agreed by b1/b2; sentinel present in C and both Fortran headers with an auto-growing allocator path.

findings.topic: Blocking flush of buffered-mode buffers (MPI_Buffer_flush, MPI_Comm_flush_buffer, MPI_Session_flush_buffer): nonlocal, drains before return
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/buffer_flush.c.in; comm_flush_buffer.c.in; ompi/mca/pml/base/pml_base_bsend.c:470-473 (mca_pml_base_bsend_flush: opal_condition_wait + opal_progress until bsend_count==0)
findings.adjudication_note: Agreed by b1/b2; flush blocks until the buffer drains, as required. Drain semantics are a runtime property but the wait loop is in source.

findings.topic: Nonblocking flush (MPI_Buffer_iflush, MPI_Comm_iflush_buffer, MPI_Session_iflush_buffer): start a flush whose returned request completes once buffered messages are transmitted
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/pml/base/pml_base_bsend.c:479-534 (mca_pml_base_bsend_iflush): TODO 'right now treating this as blocking' at :480; blocks in opal_condition_wait/opal_progress until bsend_count==0 (:526-529) then returns *req = MPI_REQUEST_NULL (:532)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT (b1 PARTIAL vs b2/b3 SUPPORTED). Resolved to SUPPORTED. The standard mandates only that the returned request completes once all buffered messages are transmitted; it does not mandate communication/computation overlap. Implementing iflush synchronously and returning a completed request (MPI_REQUEST_NULL) satisfies the required semantics -- waiting on MPI_REQUEST_NULL is legal and the buffer is drained. The lack of true asynchrony is a quality-of-implementation gap, not a conformance gap (note the TODO). 'Any valid code path = supported' applies.

findings.topic: Nonblocking communication: Isend/Ibsend/Issend/Irsend (per-mode), Irecv, Isendrecv/Isendrecv_replace start with separate completion
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/isend.c.in; ibsend.c.in; issend.c.in; irsend.c.in; irecv.c.in; isendrecv.c.in; isendrecv_replace.c.in (MCA_PML_CALL isend/irecv)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous; all nonblocking modes plus MPI-4 nonblocking sendrecv present in C and f08.

findings.topic: Single completion: MPI_Wait / MPI_Test and MPI_Request_free for active/persistent requests
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/wait.c.in; test.c.in; request_free.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; verified present.

findings.topic: Multiple completion: MPI_Testall returns spurious MPI_ERR_IN_STATUS on success (regression); Waitany/Waitall/Waitsome/Testany/Testsome correct
findings.evidence: ompi/request/req_test.c:252 (MPI_Testall / ompi_request_default_test_all, non-MPI_STATUSES_IGNORE branch): 'if (MPI_SUCCESS == request->req_status.MPI_ERROR) { rc = MPI_ERR_IN_STATUS; }' is INVERTED -- it should be '!='. The MPI_STATUSES_IGNORE branch at req_test.c:286 is correct ('!='). VERIFIED CORRECT (unaffected): MPI_Waitall at ompi/request/req_wait.c:373 ('if (statuses[i].MPI_ERROR != OMPI_SUCCESS)') and MPI_Testsome at ompi/request/req_test.c:390 ('!='). Procedures all present (waitall.c.in, testall.c.in, etc.).
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT (b1/b2 SUPPORTED vs b3 non_conformant). Resolved to PARTIAL. b3's regression is VERIFIED by reading source and git history: introduced by commit 38a7fbb837 ('Fix for issue #13432', 2025-10-15), which refactored both req_test.c and req_wait.c. The bug is ISOLATED to MPI_Testall (ompi_request_default_test_all): in the real-array_of_statuses branch the condition is inverted, so (a) a fully successful MPI_Testall returns MPI_ERR_IN_STATUS (firing the error handler) instead of MPI_SUCCESS, and (b) a genuinely failed request does NOT set MPI_ERR_IN_STATUS (the error is masked) and the nested FT branch is dead code. MPI_Waitall (req_wait.c:373) and MPI_Testsome (req_test.c:390) keep the correct '!=' condition and are NOT affected; the MPI_STATUSES_IGNORE path of Testall is also correct. Present-but-wrong, one-character fix ('==' -> '!='), not on the in-flight list -> PARTIAL, not MISSING_INFLIGHT. This is a correctness regression on main worth fixing promptly. Filed as open-mpi/ompi issue #13967.

findings.topic: Non-destructive status test: MPI_Request_get_status and _any/_all/_some
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/request_get_status{,_any,_all,_some}.c.in and matching ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/request_get_status*.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: Agreed by b1/b2; all four present in C and f08.

findings.topic: Probe operations: MPI_Probe (blocking) and MPI_Iprobe (nonblocking) match without receiving
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/probe.c.in; iprobe.c.in (MCA_PML_CALL probe/iprobe)
findings.adjudication_note: Agreed by b1/b3; present.

findings.topic: Matched probe/receive: MPI_Mprobe/MPI_Improbe yield an MPI_Message; MPI_Mrecv/MPI_Imrecv consume it (thread-safe matched receive; MPI_MESSAGE_NO_PROC for MPI_PROC_NULL)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/mprobe.c.in; improbe.c.in; mrecv.c.in; imrecv.c.in (MCA_PML_CALL); ompi/message/message.c; MPI_MESSAGE_NULL/NO_PROC in mpi.h.in; PROC_NULL probe yields MPI_MESSAGE_NO_PROC (ompi/request/request.c)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous; full matched-probe family in C and f08, thread-safe under THREAD_MULTIPLE.

findings.topic: MPI_Cancel of a pending (unmatched) receive request and MPI_Test_cancelled reporting
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/pml/ob1/pml_ob1_recvreq.c:97 (cancel granted only when unmatched, denied if matched); ompi/mpi/c/test_cancelled.c.in (*flag = status->_cancelled). Also MTL path: ompi/mca/pml/cm/pml_cm_cancel.c + mtl/ofi fi_cancel.
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous; receive cancellation works in default ob1 (and best-effort under MTL/UCX, which the standard permits).

findings.topic: MPI_Cancel of a send request (deprecated, best-effort)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/pml/ob1/pml_ob1_sendreq.c:173-174 ('we dont cancel send requests by now'; returns OMPI_SUCCESS without setting _cancelled, so the communication completes)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT (b1 PARTIAL vs b2/b3 SUPPORTED). Resolved to SUPPORTED. MPI-5.0 deprecates send cancellation and permits a best-effort outcome: 'either the cancellation succeeds, or the communication succeeds, but not both.' ob1 always lets the communication succeed (cancel is a no-op), which is exactly one of the two explicitly permitted outcomes. MPI_Test_cancelled then correctly reports not-cancelled. Conformant -> SUPPORTED, not PARTIAL.

findings.topic: Persistent communication requests: per-mode init (Send/Bsend/Ssend/Rsend_init, Recv_init), MPI_Start, MPI_Startall
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/send_init.c.in; bsend_init.c.in; ssend_init.c.in; rsend_init.c.in; recv_init.c.in; start.c.in; startall.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: Agreed by b1/b2; full persistent-request set present, including persistent-request inactivation in req_test/req_wait.

findings.topic: Null-process semantics: send/recv to/from MPI_PROC_NULL succeed immediately; recv status returns source=PROC_NULL, tag=ANY_TAG, count=0
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/recv.c.in:82-92 (PROC_NULL -> copies ompi_request_empty.req_status, returns MPI_SUCCESS immediately); send.c.in:81; irecv.c.in:66; ompi/request/request.c:175-205 (empty-request status carries SOURCE=PROC_NULL, TAG=ANY_TAG, count=0)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous; handled at the C-binding layer before reaching the PML, with the prescribed empty-status values.

findings.topic: Chapter-3 error classes exist and are returned: MPI_ERR_TRUNCATE, MPI_ERR_IN_STATUS, MPI_ERR_PENDING, MPI_ERR_BUFFER, MPI_ERR_{REQUEST,TYPE,COUNT,TAG,RANK}
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:685-703 (all defined); ompi/mca/pml/ob1/pml_ob1_recvreq.h:200 (MPI_ERR_TRUNCATE); ompi/request/req_wait.c (MPI_ERR_IN_STATUS aggregation); OMPI_ERR_BUFFER on bsend attach path in pml_base_bsend.c
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2; all chapter-relevant error classes are defined and exercised in truncation, multiple-completion, and bsend-attach paths. (Note: the MPI_ERR_IN_STATUS array-of-statuses *return* in Testall is buggy -- tracked under the multiple-completion topic -- but the class itself exists and is used.)

findings.topic: Point-to-point delivered by the default transport stack (no optional library required); big-count (_c) entry points present
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/pml/ob1/pml_ob1.c:81-94 (ob1 always-available default over self/sm/tcp BTLs); _c variants generated from the same .c.in templates (e.g. isend.c.in) via the bigcount generator. Optional alternates cm (MTL) and ucx are not required.
findings.adjudication_note: Agreed by b1/b3. ob1 over default BTLs delivers the full p2p set without any optional library; UCX/OFI/PSM2 are alternative high-performance PMLs, not prerequisites. All _c big-count entry points are present (symbol baseline; behavior identical to int path with MPI_Count widening, with the single get_count_c exception tracked separately).

chapters.title: Partitioned Point-to-Point Communication
chapters.tldr: Open MPI on this checkout (HEAD 71d6dcf7f4) implements MPI-5.0 Chapter 4 in the default build. All six procedures (MPI_Psend_init, MPI_Precv_init, MPI_Pready, MPI_Pready_range, MPI_Pready_list, MPI_Parrived) are present in both C and mpi_f08 bindings, backed by the always-built, non-optional 'part' framework whose sole 'persist' component is hard-selected with priority 1 (no configure flag, MCA, or external-library gate; works over any PML). Init calls are local/non-blocking with lazy negotiation; Start/complete reuses the Chapter 3 persistent-request machinery; Pready_range/Pready_list reduce to repeated Pready; the persist component handles differing send/recv partition counts via fractional aggregation. The one genuine deviation is MPI_Parrived on a null or freshly-inactive (never-started) request: the standard (p.119) mandates flag=true, but the default PARAM_CHECK build returns MPI_ERR_REQUEST for a null request (parrived.c.in:48) and flag=false for a freshly-initialized precv request (req->flags==NULL, part_persist.h:377->570). The core probing path is fully conformant and completed-then-reset inactive requests do return flag=true, so MPI_Parrived is PARTIAL, not absent. Neither in-flight item (ABI PR #13280, OMPIO MPI_File info) touches this chapter. The part.h header's 'blocking in the RMA component' note and the referenced-but-absent rma backend are vestigial and do not describe the shipped persist module.

findings.topic: Six partitioned procedures present in C and mpi_f08 bindings (Psend_init, Precv_init, Pready, Pready_range, Pready_list, Parrived)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/{psend_init,precv_init,pready,pready_range,pready_list,parrived}.c.in (+ *_generated.c); ompi/mpi/c/{psend_init,precv_init}_ts.c.in; b3 maps mpi_f08 bindings present (for-claude/work/symbol_baseline.json); not in the f08 genuine-gap list (Status_{get,set}_* / Sizeof).
findings.adjudication_note: All three agree (b1/b2 explicit; b3 via 'Fortran mpi_f08 bindings: fully_conformant' caveat). Verified all six C .in sources exist; PMPI profiling generated. No _c (large-count) variant exists for these, which is correct — MPI-5.0 partitioned procs are not in the _c embiggening set.

findings.topic: Partitioned framework/component built and selected by default (no optional library or configure-flag gate)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/part/configure.m4:12-18 (MCA_CONFIGURE_FRAMEWORK priority 1 + MCA_SETUP_DIRECT_CALL, no library gate); ompi/mca/part/base/part_base_frame.c:141 (hard-adds 'persist'); ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist_component.c (priority 1); ompi/mca/part/ has only persist/ (no rma/).
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous (b3 via 'Component maturity / single backend' caveat: config_gated but always-built/always-selected, non-optional direct-call framework -> SUPPORTED per framework rule). Single 'persist' component, no third-party dependency, no --enable/--disable gate. Confirmed always-on. The post_configure.sh DIRECT_CALL_HEADER points at a non-existent rma backend and part.h:45-54 'blocking in the RMA component' comment is stale/vestigial — the built module is persist.

findings.topic: Partitioned init calls (Psend_init/Precv_init) are local/non-blocking; matching may be deferred (lazy negotiation)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist.h:340-405 (precv_init posts non-blocking irecv of setup at :379, sets lazy flags, returns); :407-491 (psend_init posts non-blocking isend at :462, returns); progress in mca_part_persist_progress (:158-338).
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 agree SUPPORTED; b3 silent on this sub-topic (not disagreement). Verified init posts non-blocking PML ops and returns immediately; negotiation deferred to the progress engine, consistent with the §4.1 rationale permitting late matching. Setup/matching uses the user communicator (:379); bulk transfer is routed over a dup of MPI_COMM_WORLD using translated world ranks (architectural restriction, but conformant for the addressed cases).

findings.topic: Start/Startall activates the operation and resets partitions; Wait/Test complete via Chapter 3 request machinery
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist.h:493-532 (mca_part_persist_start resets done_count/flags, sets req_state ACTIVE, req_complete PENDING); :137-151 (mca_part_persist_complete via ompi_request_complete); :297-320 (progress completes when done_count==real_parts).
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 SUPPORTED; b3 silent. Verified PART requests dispatch through the generic ompi_request start path into part_start, and completion goes through standard request completion. Precv completion indicates the whole buffer was received.

findings.topic: MPI_Pready marks a send-side partition ready; valid only on a partitioned (send) request
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/pready.c.in:48 (rejects NULL / req_type != OMPI_REQUEST_PART -> MPI_ERR_REQUEST); ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist.h:534-557 (mca_part_persist_pready marks flags for the range).
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 SUPPORTED; b3 silent on Pready specifically (but its erroneous-input caveat notes the binding only checks the handle). Verified: rejects non-PART requests; does not validate partition-in-range or send-vs-recv — standard labels those user errors, detection not required.

findings.topic: MPI_Pready_range and MPI_Pready_list are equivalent to repeated MPI_Pready over the indicated partitions
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/pready_range.c.in:54 (single mca_part.part_pready(low,high,request)); ompi/mpi/c/pready_list.c.in:54-56 (loops per listed element); ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist.h:534-557 (pready core loops min..max).
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 SUPPORTED; b3 silent. Range maps to one low/high call that loops the range; list loops element-by-element — both reduce to the same single-partition pready core, semantically equivalent to repeated MPI_Pready as required.

findings.topic: MPI_Parrived probes partial receive completion (per-partition); does not complete the request; repeatable; aggregates across differing send/recv partition counts
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/parrived.c.in:54; ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist.h:559-591 (tests per-partition flags; fractional remap convert=real_parts/req_parts with floor/ceil at :577-582 for mismatched counts; opal_progress() when not arrived at :586-588; does not mark request complete).
findings.adjudication_note: Core probing behavior is unanimously conformant (b1/b2 explicit; b3 'core probing behavior is conformant'). This row covers the conformant core; the null/inactive deviation is the separate PARTIAL topic below. Differing send-side vs receive-side partition counts handled via fractional aggregation (b2's ch04-differing-partition-counts merged here).

findings.topic: MPI_Parrived with a null or inactive request must return flag=true
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/parrived.c.in:48 (NULL request or non-PART req_type -> MPI_ERR_REQUEST in the default PARAM_CHECK build; MPI_REQUEST_NULL has req_type OMPI_REQUEST_NULL, not OMPI_REQUEST_PART); ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist.h:377 (precv_init sets req->flags = NULL) -> :570 (mca_part_persist_parrived gates on '0 != req->flags', returns _flag=false at :567/589 for a freshly-init'd request).
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict resolved to PARTIAL. MPI-5.0 p.119 requires flag=true for BOTH a null and an inactive request argument. Verified: default build returns MPI_ERR_REQUEST for null and flag=false for a freshly-initialized (never-started) precv (flags==NULL). However the procedure is PRESENT and its core is fully conformant, and a completed-then-reset inactive request DOES return flag=true (its flags array is retained) — so this is 'present but not fully standard-conformant' = PARTIAL, not UNSUPPORTED (b2's missing_gap normalizes to UNSUPPORTED but the behavior is present, not absent) and not SUPPORTED (no default path satisfies the MUST for the null / freshly-inactive cases). Not in-flight (in-flight list is only ABI ch20 + OMPIO MPI_File info). b1/b3 land on PARTIAL; adopted.

findings.topic: count/datatype describe one partition; partitions equal-sized, contiguous, no padding; total data = parts*count*datatype
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist.h:386 (req_bytes = parts*count*dt_size); :243-252 (per-partition send base = req_addr + bytes*i where bytes = real_count*dt_size, then sends real_count elements of req_datatype); :264-283 (recv side mirrors; falls back to MPI_BYTE when dt sizes differ).
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; kept SUPPORTED after investigation. b1 correctly observed the per-partition base is offset by count*SIZE (opal_datatype_type_size), not count*EXTENT, while the PML send reads count*EXTENT bytes of req_datatype — so for datatypes with extent>size (resized/strided/with-holes) consecutive partitions' regions overlap/mislocate. This is a real quality-of-implementation limitation, but §4.2.3 specifies partitions are 'contiguous in memory, no padding between them', which arguably blesses tight size-based packing for the common (extent==size) case. Defensible as SUPPORTED with the layout caveat recorded here, not PARTIAL.

findings.topic: Absolute displacements / MPI_BOTTOM not supported for partitioned communication
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist.h:251,278 (per-partition buffer = req_addr + bytes*i, assumes a concrete base address); standard explicitly disallows MPI_BOTTOM for partitioned ops.
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 marked SUPPORTED, but the requirement is that MPI_BOTTOM/absolute displacements are NOT supported here — i.e. the standard itself permits the implementation to omit them. This is non-binding on the implementation, so NA is the precise taxonomy value (no required behavior to implement). Open MPI simply assumes a real buffer base and neither special-cases nor must reject MPI_BOTTOM. Status difference is purely vocabulary, not a substantive disagreement.

findings.topic: Per-operation info argument accepted on Psend_init/Precv_init (implementation-defined keys)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/psend_init.c.in / precv_init.c.in pass info to mca_part; ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist.h:348,415 (info parameter present, never inspected).
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 SUPPORTED; b3 silent. The info handle is accepted as a channel but no keys are recognized. Recognizing zero info keys is always conformant (info hints are advisory). Unrelated to the OMPIO MPI_File info in-flight work.

findings.topic: Erroneous-usage cases (partitions<=0, partition>=count, Pready on active partition, Pready on recv / Parrived on send request, free/cancel of an active request) need not be detected
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/pready.c.in:48 / parrived.c.in:48 (check only handle non-NULL + req_type==OMPI_REQUEST_PART); ompi/mca/part/persist/part_persist_sendreq.c:32 & part_persist_recvreq.c:31 (req_cancel = NULL, consistent with cancel of a partitioned request being erroneous).
findings.adjudication_note: b2 (ch04-erroneous-conditions, default) + b3 (erroneous-input caveat, fully_conformant) agree these are user errors the standard does not require the implementation to detect; b1 noted minimal validation in passing. NA is the precise value: the standard imposes no requirement for erroneous program input, so there is no binding behavior to implement. Verified req_cancel is wired to NULL for both send and recv part requests.

chapters.title: Datatypes
chapters.tldr: Open MPI's Chapter 5 (Datatypes) is overwhelmingly SUPPORTED by default: the full derived-datatype constructor family (contiguous/vector/hvector/indexed/hindexed/indexed_block/hindexed_block/struct/subarray/darray/resized) and their large-count _c variants, address arithmetic (Get_address, Aint_add/diff), extent/true-extent (incl _x), commit/free/dup, Get_elements/_x, full combiner decoding via Type_get_envelope/get_contents, and canonical external32 pack/unpack are all delivered unconditionally by the always-built opal/datatype + ompi/datatype engine. Three genuine, narrow deviations exist: (1) the large-count MPI_Pack_c/MPI_Unpack_c/MPI_Pack_size_c variants truncate their 64-bit size through (unsigned int)/(int) casts, so the >2-4 GiB case the _c API exists to serve is mishandled (PARTIAL); (2) MPI_Type_get_value_index only returns the predefined NAMED pair types and yields MPI_DATATYPE_NULL for any other value/index pair instead of constructing an unnamed datatype, so the MPI_COMBINER_VALUE_INDEX combiner is unreachable (PARTIAL). The optional Fortran KIND datatypes (INTEGER16, REAL16, COMPLEX32, etc.) and the MPI_TYPE_CREATE_F90_* achievable precision set are CONDITIONAL on configure-time Fortran-compiler KIND autodetection (standard-permitted optionality). The base int-typed Pack/Unpack/Pack_size and everything else are conformant.

findings.topic: Derived datatype constructors (contiguous/vector/hvector/indexed/hindexed/indexed_block/hindexed_block/struct) incl big-count _c forms
findings.evidence: ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_create_{contiguous,vector,indexed,struct}.c (all present); ompi/mpi/c/type_{contiguous,vector,create_hvector,indexed,create_hindexed,create_indexed_block,create_hindexed_block,create_struct}.c.in; big-count _c entry points generated by BIGCOUNT template (e.g. type_contiguous_generated.c)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported. All constructor source files present; PROTOTYPE COUNT template generates both int and _c entry points. b3 covered this under the 'decoding fidelity' / build-configurability caveats as fully_conformant.

findings.topic: Subarray constructor (n-D subarray with lb/ub markers, correct extent)
findings.evidence: ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_create_subarray.c (present); ompi/mpi/c/type_create_subarray.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported; constructor file verified present.

findings.topic: Distributed-array (DARRAY) constructor with BLOCK/CYCLIC/NONE distributions and ORDER_C/FORTRAN
findings.evidence: ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_create_darray.c (present); ompi/mpi/c/type_create_darray.c.in; DARRAY decode case ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_args.c:217,969
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported; file present and a DARRAY combiner decode case exists.

findings.topic: MPI_Type_create_resized + lb/ub-marker (resized) extent semantics
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/type_create_resized.c.in; ompi/mpi/c/type_get_extent{,_x}.c.in, type_get_true_extent{,_x}.c.in; RESIZED decode case ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_args.c:240,1019
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported; resized + extent/true-extent in both int and _x forms present.

findings.topic: Address/size queries: MPI_Get_address, MPI_Aint_add, MPI_Aint_diff (portable address arithmetic)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/get_address.c.in; ompi/include/mpi.h.in:1443-1446 (MPI_Aint_add/diff macros); ompi/mpi/fortran/mpif-h/aint_add_f.c, aint_diff_f.c
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported. C macro form for Aint_add/diff is standard-permitted; real Fortran symbols also provided.

findings.topic: MPI_Type_size / _c return type size; MPI_UNDEFINED when value does not fit OUT param
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/type_size.c.in, type_size_x.c.in; ompi/include/mpi.h.in (MPI_Type_size{,_c,_x})
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported; int, _x and _c forms present.

findings.topic: MPI_Type_commit (idempotent), MPI_Type_free (sets handle to NULL, in-flight comms/derived types unaffected)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/type_commit.c.in, type_free.c.in; reference-counted ompi_datatype engine; opal_datatype_is_committed (ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype.h)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported; commit state tracked, types reference-counted.

findings.topic: MPI_Type_dup copies attributes via key copy callbacks; preserves committed state/bounds
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/type_dup.c.in (attribute copy via ompi_attr_copy_all at MPI level); ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_create.c:114-117 (engine-level dup intentionally sets d_keyhash=NULL, attr copy done at top level)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported. b1's caveat is correct: the engine dup path does NOT copy attributes (by design); the MPI wrapper type_dup.c.in performs the attribute copy. Behavior is conformant; evidence corrected to the wrapper.

findings.topic: General datatypes usable in send/recv with count>1 (concatenation/type-signature matching), MPI_BOTTOM + absolute-address buffers
findings.gate: Cross-endian/heterogeneous data conversion on this send/recv path requires the non-default --enable-heterogeneous build; homogeneous (same-arch) communication is default SUPPORTED
findings.evidence: ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_sndrcv.c; opal/datatype convertors used by PML; ompi/include/mpi.h.in:576 (MPI_BOTTOM); convertor base-address handling in opal/datatype
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported; convertor-based concatenation and MPI_BOTTOM handling present. Per task instruction: the only place cross-endian conversion actually bites is heterogeneous send/recv, which is gated on the non-default --enable-heterogeneous flag (CONDITIONAL for that sub-case); the type-map/concatenation behavior itself is default SUPPORTED.

findings.topic: MPI_Get_elements / _c / _x return basic element count, MPI_UNDEFINED on non-integral or >INT_MAX
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/get_elements.c.in:69-77 (MPI_UNDEFINED translation for non-integral via OMPI_ERR_VALUE_OUT_OF_BOUNDS and for >INT_MAX overflow); get_elements_x.c.in; engine ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_get_elements.c
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported. b1's caveat correct: the engine returns OMPI_ERR_VALUE_OUT_OF_BOUNDS; the MPI_UNDEFINED translation and >INT_MAX case are in the wrapper get_elements.c.in (verified lines 69-77).

findings.topic: Datatype decoding: MPI_Type_get_envelope (combiner + arg counts), MPI_Type_get_contents (reconstruct ctor args); predefined => MPI_COMBINER_NAMED; deprecated *_INTEGER aliases decoded
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/type_get_envelope.c.in; ompi/mpi/c/type_get_contents.c.in; ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_args.c:140-243 (combiner table: DARRAY:217, RESIZED:240, HINDEXED_BLOCK:243), :380 (NAMED for predefined)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported (b3 fully_conformant). Full combiner table including DARRAY/SUBARRAY/RESIZED/HINDEXED_BLOCK/F90_*/NAMED/DUP and legacy *_INTEGER aliases.

findings.topic: Big-count decode: non-_c get_envelope/get_contents raise MPI_ERR_TYPE when num_large_counts>0; _c variants return large-count arrays
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/type_get_envelope.c.in:69-72 (returns MPI_ERR_TYPE when cl>0 or any count>INT_MAX); ompi_datatype_args.c tracks num_large_counts; _c envelope/contents return large arrays
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported; verified MPI_ERR_TYPE path for large-count on non-_c envelope (cl>0 guard).

findings.topic: MPI_Pack / MPI_Unpack / MPI_Pack_size (incremental position, MPI_PACKED relaxed matching) - base int-typed variants
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/pack.c.in, unpack.c.in, pack_size.c.in; generated int-typed entry points MPI_Pack/MPI_Unpack/MPI_Pack_size; opal convertor pack/unpack
findings.adjudication_note: The base int-typed Pack/Unpack/Pack_size are conformant (all 3 reviewers agree, b3 explicitly states base variants are fully conformant). The large-count _c variants are a separate PARTIAL topic below.

findings.topic: Big-count MPI_Pack_c / MPI_Unpack_c / MPI_Pack_size_c (>2-4 GiB size handling)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/pack.c.in:93 -> pack_generated.c:134,188 (MPI_Pack_c has MPI_Count outsize but truncation check casts '(unsigned int)outsize'); ompi/mpi/c/unpack.c.in:100 -> unpack_generated.c:138,198 (MPI_Unpack_c, MPI_Count insize, '(unsigned int)insize'); ompi/mpi/c/pack_size.c.in:63 -> pack_size_generated.c:84,112 (MPI_Pack_size_c has MPI_Count* size but '*size = (int)length'). Correct pattern at ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_external.c:56 uses (size_t).
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT resolved in favor of b3 (verified in generated source). The shared PROTOTYPE COUNT template is correct for the int-typed base bindings but, for the _c bindings where outsize/insize are 64-bit MPI_Count and size is MPI_Count*, the truncation check casts through (unsigned int) and pack_size stores via (int). Sizes/positions above ~2-4 GiB - exactly the case the _c API exists to support - yield spurious or missed MPI_ERR_TRUNCATE and a truncated pack size. b1 and b2 assessed the family at the API-presence level (symbols present, position semantics OK) and missed the _c truncation. Narrow but genuine non-conformance => PARTIAL.

findings.topic: Canonical external32 pack/unpack (MPI_Pack_external / Unpack_external / Pack_external_size) in big-endian IEEE external32 datarep
findings.evidence: ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_external32.c:106 (fixed arch_id OPAL_ARCH_ISBIGENDIAN|LONGDOUBLEIS128|LDEXPSIZEIS15|LDMANTDIGIS113|BOOLIS8|LOGICALIS8); ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_external.c:33-134; ompi/mpi/c/pack_external.c.in, unpack_external.c.in, pack_external_size.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous-supported (b3 fully_conformant). Dedicated ompi_mpi_external32_convertor implements the standard big-endian IEEE byte-aligned format; only 'external32' is the valid datarep. b1 notes the datarep string is not validated against MPI_ERR_ARG, but since 'external32' is the only standard-valid value this is a harmless permissiveness, not a conformance gap.

findings.topic: MPI_Type_get_value_index / MPI_COMBINER_VALUE_INDEX (MPI-5.0)
findings.evidence: ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_create.c:128-180 (ompi_datatype_get_value_index returns predefined NAMED pair types only; any other value/index pair => MPI_DATATYPE_NULL; code comment 'not a complete implementation ... doesn't support possible unnamed datatypes'); ompi/mpi/c/type_get_value_index.c.in (binding present); MPI_COMBINER_VALUE_INDEX defined ompi/include/mpi.h.in:836 but NO VALUE_INDEX decode case in ompi_datatype_args.c and no constructor producing it
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT resolved to PARTIAL. The MPI_Type_get_value_index symbol/binding exists and works for the standard predefined pair types (FLOAT_INT, DOUBLE_INT, 2INT, LONG_INT, etc.), but for any other value_type/index_type pair it returns MPI_DATATYPE_NULL instead of constructing an unnamed pair datatype, and the code self-documents this as incomplete. Consequently MPI_COMBINER_VALUE_INDEX is unreachable (defined as a constant only, no decode/construct path). b3 marked it SUPPORTED only on the basis that the constant '=19' is defined - that conflates symbol/constant presence with behavioral completeness. b1's PARTIAL is correct. (Merged b1's two findings - get_value_index PARTIAL and the VALUE_INDEX combiner UNSUPPORTED - into one topic, since they are one underlying gap: the combiner is unreachable precisely because no unnamed pair type is ever constructed. PARTIAL rather than UNSUPPORTED because the predefined-pair path works.)

findings.topic: MPI_TYPE_CREATE_F90_REAL/_COMPLEX/_INTEGER (precision/range mapping, cached consistent handles)
findings.gate: Fortran build enabled (--enable-mpi-fortran, default on) AND the requested precision/range is achievable by a configure-detected Fortran KIND (e.g. OMPI_HAVE_FORTRAN_REAL2); MPI_DATATYPE_NULL returned when unsatisfiable
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/type_create_f90_real.c.in:57-113 (MPI_UNDEFINED/null handling + opal_hash_table per-(p,r) cache); type_create_f90_{complex,integer}.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT. The mapping logic and per-(p,r) caching (consistency requirement) are fully implemented and built unconditionally in C (b2's point). However the *achievable set of precisions* depends on which Fortran KIND types configure detected, so the function may return MPI_DATATYPE_NULL for precisions the compiler cannot provide (b1's point). Marking CONDITIONAL to capture that the practically-reachable precision range is gated on configure-time Fortran KIND autodetect; the procedure itself is present and correct.

findings.topic: MPI_TYPE_MATCH_SIZE returns matching named type for (typeclass,size)
findings.evidence: ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_match_size.c:50-53 (returns MPI_DATATYPE_NULL when no predefined type of requested typeclass/size exists); ompi/mpi/c/type_match_size.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: Unique (b1). Valid and correctly SUPPORTED; returning DATATYPE_NULL for an unavailable size is standard-conformant behavior (caller then gets an error).

findings.topic: Optional Fortran KIND datatypes (MPI_INTEGER1/2/4/8/16, MPI_REAL2/4/8/16, MPI_COMPLEX4/8/16/32, MPI_LOGICAL1..16)
findings.gate: Per-KIND configure-time Fortran-compiler autodetect (OMPI_HAVE_FORTRAN_<KIND>); --disable-mpi-fortran removes Fortran exposure
findings.evidence: ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_module.c:216-286 (per-KIND #if OMPI_HAVE_FORTRAN_* ... #else OMPI_DATATYPE_INIT_UNAVAILABLE); config/ompi_fortran_check.m4:52,74-78,156-158,180-182 (detection gated, size/KIND-value mismatch disables a KIND); ompi/include/mpi.h.in:1356-1395 (handles declared)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique (b2), CONDITIONAL confirmed. The standard marks these datatypes optional; OMPI populates each only when configure autodetects the Fortran compiler actually supports that KIND, else the handle maps to UNAVAILABLE. Genuinely per-KIND configure-dependent. Not in-flight (PR #13280 / ompio-info touch other chapters).

findings.topic: Fortran-2008 bindings for all Chapter 5 procedures/constants
findings.gate: --disable-mpi-fortran removes the Fortran/f08 surface (default-on, removable); C bindings unaffected
findings.evidence: config/ompi_configure_options.m4:125-130 (default sets OMPI_TRY_FORTRAN_BINDINGS to USEMPIF08, highest level); ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/mod/mpi-f08-interfaces.h.in (Type_commit/free/get_extent etc.); ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/type_* f08 bodies
findings.adjudication_note: Unique (b2). b2 first labeled CONDITIONAL then self-refuted to 'default'. Per framework, a feature that is default-ON and only removed by a --disable-* flag is SUPPORTED with a gate note - matching b2's corrected verdict and the FRAMEWORK pre-resolved fact that mpi_f08 is default-on => SUPPORTED.

findings.topic: Datatype error classes (MPI_ERR_TYPE, MPI_ERR_ARG, MPI_ERR_COUNT) for invalid datatype/argument usage
findings.gate: Argument validation via MPI_PARAM_CHECK (default on; --disable-mpi-param-check removes the explicit checks)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:687 (MPI_ERR_TYPE), :697 (MPI_ERR_ARG); MPI_ERR_COUNT defined; binding templates validate handles/args under MPI_PARAM_CHECK (e.g. pack.c.in:54-69 returns MPI_ERR_COMM/ARG/COUNT)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique (b2), SUPPORTED. Error classes defined as default C macros; bindings return them under MPI_PARAM_CHECK (default on, removable).

findings.topic: MPI_LB / MPI_UB deprecation and *_INTEGER deprecated combiner back-compat
findings.gate: --enable-mpi1-compatibility controls exposure of MPI_LB/MPI_UB and the *_INTEGER combiner aliases (OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS); deprecated markers/combiners themselves are handled
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:817-826 (MPI_COMBINER_HINDEXED_INTEGER/STRUCT_INTEGER guarded by !OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS); ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_args.c decodes deprecated *_INTEGER aliases to canonical combiners
findings.adjudication_note: b2/b3 both note deprecated-combiner/back-compat handling. Resized supersedes MPI_LB/MPI_UB; the deprecated combiner aliases are decoded for back-compat (default build), removable via the MPI-1 compat configure flag. SUPPORTED with gate note for the legacy MPI_LB/MPI_UB symbol exposure.

chapters.title: Collective Communication
chapters.tldr: Open MPI's main branch implements MPI-5.0 Chapter 6 by default. All blocking collectives (barrier, bcast, gather[v], scatter[v], allgather[v], alltoall[v][w], reduce, allreduce, reduce_scatter[_block], scan, exscan) plus neighbor collectives are served by the unconditionally-built basic component (intra+inter paths) and accelerated by tuned/han/inter; all nonblocking (I*) and persistent (*_init) variants are served by libnbc, which has no configure.m4 and is always built. Predefined and user-defined ops, MPI_Reduce_local, MPI_IN_PLACE, big-count _c variants, op/datatype validity checks (MPI_ERR_OP), and inter-communicator two-group semantics are all default-supported. b1 and b2 cover the full chapter; b3 scoped itself to blocking collectives and explicitly deferred nonblocking/persistent to other agents (silent there, not disagreement). Two real edge findings: (1) MPI_Type_get_value_index implements only the predefined NAMED value-index pairs (FLOAT_INT, 2INT, Fortran 2INTEGER/2REAL, etc.) and returns MPI_DATATYPE_NULL for unnamed pairs (Example 6.17's MPI_DOUBLE+MPI_UINT64_T); MPI_COMBINER_VALUE_INDEX is defined as a constant but never produced by the envelope path -> PARTIAL. (2) MPI_Exscan does not reject an inter-communicator argument (MPI_Scan does) -> minor permissiveness, the required intra-comm behavior is fully supported. Predefined-op reductions over REAL16/16-byte long double are gated on optional compiler support (CONDITIONAL). Optional accelerated providers (UCC/Portals4/accelerator/acoll) are non-required layers (CONDITIONAL), never the sole provider of any required behavior.

findings.topic: Blocking barrier (intra + inter), MPI_Barrier
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/coll/basic/coll_basic_module.c:115 (barrier inter), :128/:130 (barrier intra basic/log); ompi/mca/coll/base/coll_base_barrier.c
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. Always-built basic installs barrier for both intra and inter communicators; verified BASIC_INSTALL_COLL_API barrier entries.

findings.topic: Blocking data-movement collectives over intra-communicators (bcast, gather[v], scatter[v], allgather[v], alltoall[v][w]) with type-matching / rank-order semantics
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/coll/basic/coll_basic_module.c:129-167 (intra install of allgather[v]/alltoall[v][w]/gather[v]/scatter[v]/bcast); tuned default priority via ompi/mca/coll/tuned/
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. Verified basic installs every blocking data-movement collective for intra communicators; tuned accelerates by priority but is not required.

findings.topic: Blocking collectives over inter-communicators (MPI_ROOT/MPI_PROC_NULL root convention, two-group all-to-all semantics)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/coll/basic/coll_basic_module.c:109-123 (inter install of all data-movement + reduction collectives); ompi/mca/coll/inter/coll_inter_module.c; MPI_ROOT present in mpi.h.in
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. basic installs *_inter variants for every collective that admits inter-comm semantics; inter component adds optimized paths.

findings.topic: Reduction family (reduce, allreduce, reduce_scatter[_block], scan, exscan) with predefined ops
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/coll/basic/coll_basic_module.c:121-141 (reduce/reduce_scatter[_block] inter+intra, scan/exscan intra); ompi/mca/op/base/op_base_functions.c
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. All reduction collectives installed by basic; scan/exscan are intra-only per standard (installed only on intra path).

findings.topic: Predefined-op x datatype coverage incl. MAXLOC/MINLOC over the standard type groups
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/op/base/op_base_functions.c (MAX/MIN/SUM/PROD/LAND/LOR/LXOR/BAND/BOR/BXOR/MAXLOC/MINLOC/REPLACE/NO_OP over standard type groups); ompi/op/op.c
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 agree SUPPORTED; b3 silent (scoped out ops). Base op component always provides correct scalar reductions; SIMD backends (avx/aarch64/riscv64) are optional accelerators.

findings.topic: Predefined-op reductions over MPI_REAL16 / 16-byte long double
findings.gate: compiler support: OMPI_HAVE_FORTRAN_REAL16 && OMPI_REAL16_MATCHES_C (optional compiler feature: matching 16-byte real KIND)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/op/base/op_base_functions.c:210,276,339,411 (REAL16/c_long_double_complex functions guarded by #if OMPI_HAVE_FORTRAN_REAL16 && OMPI_REAL16_MATCHES_C)
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict b1(conditional) vs b2(default). b1 is correct under the framework: REAL16 reductions are compiled only when the compiler provides a matching 16-byte real KIND — an optional compiler feature, which the framework lists as CONDITIONAL. Scalar long double reductions work regardless; only the Fortran REAL16 path is gated. Not a conformance defect, a compiler limitation.

findings.topic: User-defined reduction ops (MPI_Op_create/_c, MPI_Op_free, MPI_Op_commutative; commute flag honored; C + Fortran callbacks)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/op_create.c.in, op_free.c.in, op_commutative.c.in; MPI_Op_create_c at ompi/include/mpi.h.in:2142; MPI_User_function + MPI_User_function_c declared in mpi.h.in; f08 abstract interface in mpi-f08-interfaces-callbacks.F90
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 agree SUPPORTED; b3 silent. All three bindings present; both C callback typedefs declared; f08 callback interface present (the chapter map's MPI_User_function f08_present=false is a map error, the interface is public).

findings.topic: Op/datatype validity check: reject invalid op/datatype combos and MPI_OP_NULL with MPI_ERR_OP
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/scan.c.in:75 (MPI_OP_NULL -> MPI_ERR_OP), :78 (ompi_op_is_valid -> MPI_ERR_OP); ompi/mpi/c/exscan.c.in:68-70 (same)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2; valid. Verified ompi_op_is_valid enforces datatype/op compatibility and MPI_OP_NULL is rejected with MPI_ERR_OP across reduction bindings.

findings.topic: MPI_Reduce_local (process-local reduction, no communicator)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/reduce_local.c.in:75 (coll_reduce_local dispatch); ompi/mca/coll/basic/coll_basic_module.c install reduce_local=mca_coll_base_reduce_local; MPI_Reduce_local_c at ompi/include/mpi.h.in:2214
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 agree SUPPORTED; b3 silent. Present, wired to op framework, big-count _c variant declared.

findings.topic: MPI_IN_PLACE semantics for collectives (blocking/nonblocking/persistent)
findings.evidence: MPI_IN_PLACE in ompi/include/mpi.h.in; in-place branches handled per-collective in ompi/mca/coll/base/ (e.g. coll_base_alltoall.c in-place branch)
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 agree SUPPORTED; b3 silent. MPI_IN_PLACE defined and handled across coll components for all collectives that define it.

findings.topic: Nonblocking collectives (all MPI_I* variants) with separate completion and independent progress
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/coll/libnbc/ (no configure.m4 -> unconditionally built); nbc_i*.c files for every I-variant (iallgather[v], ialltoall[v][w], ibarrier, ibcast, ireduce[_scatter[_block]], iscan, iexscan, iscatter[v], igather[v]); coll_libnbc_component.c wires intra+inter I-variants
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 agree SUPPORTED; b3 silent (explicitly deferred nonblocking to another agent). libnbc has no configure.m4, confirmed always built; every I-variant source file present.

findings.topic: MPI_Ibarrier is the one synchronizing nonblocking collective
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/coll/libnbc/nbc_ibarrier.c; coll_libnbc_component.c installs coll_ibarrier for intra and inter communicators
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2; valid. libnbc ibarrier present for intra and inter; synchronizing-completion property is a schedule semantic provided by the barrier schedule.

findings.topic: Persistent collectives (all MPI_*_init variants) producing reusable inactive requests started by MPI_Start/Startall, with info argument
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/coll/libnbc/coll_libnbc_component.c:382-404 (intra coll_*_init), :340-356 (inter coll_*_init); nbc.c:688,696,711,720 (persistent flag, noop request, req_persistent); ompi/mpi/c/*_init.c.in bindings forward MPI_Info
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 agree SUPPORTED; b3 silent (deferred persistent to another agent). Verified every _init variant wired in libnbc for intra (lines 382-404) and inter (340-356). scan_init/exscan_init NULL only on the inter path (scan/exscan undefined on intercomm) — conformant. Info argument accepted and threaded; libnbc may ignore hints (valid).

findings.topic: MPI_Info hints accepted on persistent collective init calls
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/bcast_init.c.in, reduce_init.c.in, neighbor_allgather_init.c.in (MPI_Info param present and forwarded); ompi/mca/coll/libnbc/nbc_ireduce_scatter.c (info param threaded)

This is over-permissiveness on an erroneous argument, not a missing required behavior — the required intra-comm exscan is fully supported. Classed PARTIAL on this narrow validation point; does not break conforming programs.

findings.topic: MPI_Type_get_value_index: predefined NAMED value-index pair types (FLOAT_INT, DOUBLE_INT, 2INT, Fortran 2INTEGER/2REAL...), NULL-with-SUCCESS for no match
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/type_get_value_index.c.in (delegates to ompi_datatype_get_value_index); ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_create.c:139-177 (named pairs FLOAT_INT/DOUBLE_INT/LONG_INT/SHORT_INT/2INT/LONGDBL_INT + Fortran 2INTEGER/2REAL/2DBLPREC/2CPLEX/2DBLCPLEX); :136 inits *pair_type=&ompi_mpi_datatype_null then returns OMPI_SUCCESS
