Confirmed in source: unnamed value-index pairs return MPI_DATATYPE_NULL instead of a usable type, and MPI_COMBINER_VALUE_INDEX is defined but never emitted by the get_envelope path. Core libmpi code compiled unconditionally (gate none), not touched by any in-flight PR (#13280=ABI, ompio-info=File hints). Classed PARTIAL rather than UNSUPPORTED: MPI_Type_get_value_index exists and returns the predefined named pairs correctly; only the unnamed-pair subset and its VALUE_INDEX envelope are unimplemented — present-but-incomplete = PARTIAL per taxonomy.

findings.topic: Mixing nonblocking/persistent collective requests with other requests; MPI_CANCEL / active MPI_REQUEST_FREE on collective requests erroneous
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/coll/libnbc/nbc.c (request-based completion via ompi_request_t); coll_libnbc_component.c:156 (opal_free_list request pool); standard ompi_request_t usable in MPI_WAITALL/TESTANY

findings.topic: Optional hardware-accelerated / offloaded collective providers (UCC, Portals4, accelerator/GPU, AMD acoll, han/adapt/xhc)
findings.gate: coll/ucc (--with-ucc / UCC lib), coll/portals4 (Portals4), coll/accelerator (CUDA/ROCm via OMPI_HAVE_ACCELERATOR_SUPPORT), coll/acoll; han/adapt/xhc/tuned are default-on accelerators

findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. These are additional algorithm providers layered above the always-built basic/tuned/libnbc stack; never the sole provider of a required behavior, so disabling them does not affect conformance. UCC/Portals4/accelerator require optional external deps -> CONDITIONAL; han/adapt/tuned/xhc are default-on (their removal is gate-only).

chapters.title: Groups, Contexts, Communicators, and Caching
chapters.tldr: Open MPI's main checkout (HEAD 71d6dcf7f4) conforms strongly to MPI-5.0 Chapter 7. All chapter procedures are present in C and mpi_f08, group constructors/accessors, communicator dup/idup/create/split/free, inter-communicator ops, the session-model create-from-group / intercomm-create-from-groups constructors, the full attribute-caching engine (comm/win/type keyvals, copy/delete callbacks, predefined callbacks and predefined attributes), object naming, and comm set_info/get_info round-trip are all SUPPORTED by default. MPI_Comm_split_type SHARED/HW_GUIDED/HW_UNGUIDED/RESOURCE_GUIDED are implemented in libmpi using opal's bundled hwloc (default-built) and PMIX_LOCALITY locality, so they are SUPPORTED (not CONDITIONAL); b3's optional_lib_gated downgrade is rejected because hwloc is bundled and the graceful MPI_COMM_NULL/MPI_COMM_SELF degradation is itself spec-conformant. The session-model create-from-group constructors are pure libmpi (operate on an already-resolved group) so they are SUPPORTED with a runtime gate note for pset resolution (not CONDITIONAL). The single real gap is the mpi_pset_name sub-path of MPI_COMM_TYPE_RESOURCE_GUIDED (a normative MPI-5.0 addition, verified in mpi50-report.pdf p.331-332): the key is recognized at the binding layer but never resolved to a split, so a pset-only call yields MPI_COMM_NULL. Because returning MPI_COMM_NULL when the implementation does not recognize/support the mpi_pset_name value is itself explicitly spec-sanctioned (mpi50-report.pdf p.332), this is PARTIAL (present-but-incomplete, conformant-but-degraded), not UNSUPPORTED.

The Sessions model (ompi/instance) is built by default. Symbol and code path present and complete.

findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/comm_split.c.in; ompi/communicator/comm.c (ompi_comm_split; rankkeycompare)

findings.topic: MPI_Comm_split_type MPI_COMM_TYPE_SHARED groups shared-memory domain
findings.gate: hwloc (bundled in 3rd-party/, built by default) provides node-local detection; --disable would only affect finer splits, not SHARED node-level.
findings.evidence: ompi/communicator/comm.c:829 (case MPI_COMM_TYPE_SHARED -> OPAL_PROC_ON_LOCAL_NODE(locality)); ompi/communicator/comm.c:1452 (ompi_comm_split_type)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous SUPPORTED. SHARED case is node-level locality via OPAL_PROC_ON_LOCAL_NODE; reliable by default. Verified case label at comm.c:829.

findings.topic: MPI_Comm_split_type MPI_COMM_TYPE_HW_GUIDED via mpi_hw_resource_type; MPI_COMM_NULL on missing/unknown value or MPI_INFO_NULL
findings.gate: hwloc (bundled in 3rd-party/, built by default) + PMIX_LOCALITY for sub-node locality. Without hwloc topology, finer splits degrade gracefully to MPI_COMM_SELF/MPI_COMM_NULL (spec-conformant), not an error.
findings.evidence: ompi/communicator/comm.c:79-93 (support table); comm.c:1470-1505 (key lookup, strncasecmp match against support table, MPI_COMM_NULL on unsupported); ompi/mpi/c/comm_split_type.c.in:118-122 (MPI_INFO_NULL/missing-key -> MPI_COMM_NULL)
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict: b3 optional_lib_gated (hwloc/PMIx). Rejected: hwloc is bundled in 3rd-party/ and built by default (framework treats bundled-default like OMPIO/RMA -> SUPPORTED with gate note). The graceful degradation b3 describes is itself spec-conformant for these split types, so it does not pull below SUPPORTED. Verified support table and MPI_COMM_NULL escape at comm.c:1473-1504.

findings.topic: MPI_Comm_split_type MPI_COMM_TYPE_HW_UNGUIDED produces strict subset by iterating topology classes; MPI_COMM_NULL when no finer split
findings.gate: hwloc (bundled, default-built) + PMIX_LOCALITY; size-1 / no-finer-split short-circuits to MPI_COMM_NULL.
findings.evidence: ompi/communicator/comm.c:1355 (ompi_comm_split_unguided, real recursive impl over support table; size-1 -> MPI_COMM_NULL at :1367-1384); dispatched at comm.c:1577-1582
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict resolved as HW_GUIDED above. Real recursive implementation (not a stub): iterates use_for_unguided entries largest->smallest, sets resulting mpi_hw_resource_type in info. b3 optional_lib_gated downgrade rejected (bundled hwloc = SUPPORTED with gate note).

findings.topic: MPI_Comm_split_type MPI_COMM_TYPE_RESOURCE_GUIDED: mpi_pset_name sub-path not resolved to a split (yields MPI_COMM_NULL); mpi_hw_resource_type sub-path works
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/comm_split_type.c.in:118-131 (binding accepts mpi_pset_name to pass the gate when flag_res set); ompi/communicator/comm.c:1471-1481 (ompi_comm_split_type reads ONLY mpi_hw_resource_type via opal_info_get; flag==0 when pset-only -> MPI_COMM_NULL at :1478-1480). Negative evidence: mpi_pset_name appears only in comm_split_type.c.in/_generated.c, nowhere in comm.c or any MCA component. Standard normative text verified directly: MPI-5.0 (for-claude/mpi50-report.pdf p.331-332) defines MPI_COMM_TYPE_RESOURCE_GUIDED with the 'mpi_pset_name' info key (logical resource / process-set), and explicitly lists 'MPI_COMM_NULL is also returned ... The MPI implementation does not recognize the value associated with the info key "mpi_hw_resource_type" or "mpi_pset_name"' as a sanctioned response.
findings.adjudication_note: 3-way conflict. b3 generically marks split_type SUPPORTED; b1 PARTIAL; b2 missing_gap (UNSUPPORTED). Verified the requirement against the standard itself (not just reviewer claims): MPI_COMM_TYPE_RESOURCE_GUIDED + the mpi_pset_name info key ARE normative MPI-5.0 (mpi50-report.pdf p.331-332; added in MPI-5.0 per the change-log), NOT an OMPI extension -- so there is a real required behavior, ruling out SUPPORTED/NA for this sub-path. Resolved to PARTIAL: the mpi_hw_resource_type RESOURCE_GUIDED path is fully implemented, AND the mpi_pset_name key IS recognized at the binding layer (present) but never wired through to a pset-based split (incomplete) -> present-but-incomplete = PARTIAL. b2's UNSUPPORTED is rejected: the standard explicitly sanctions returning MPI_COMM_NULL when the implementation does not recognize/support the mpi_pset_name value (mpi50-report.pdf p.332 bullet list), so OMPI's MPI_COMM_NULL is conformant-but-degraded, not 'genuinely required behavior absent.' The pset-to-group machinery exists (ompi_group_from_pset, instance.c:1424) but is not wired into ompi_comm_split_type. Not covered by any in-flight PR (PR #13280 = ABI/Ch.20; ompio-info branch = File/IO).

findings.topic: mpi_hw_resource_type value form: bare keys only; strongly-recommended hwloc:// URI form not matched
findings.evidence: ompi/communicator/comm.c:79-93 (bare-key table); comm.c:1491-1499 (strncasecmp prefix match -> 'hwloc://NUMANode' never matches, yields MPI_COMM_NULL)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2 (its caveats list it conditional, but its own enriched verification adjusted it to default). The hwloc:// URI form is 'strongly recommended' advice-to-implementors, not a 'shall'; non-support is not a conformance violation. No configure flag/component/library gates URI parsing -> not CONDITIONAL. Default build fully provides the split types via bare keys. Portability/usability note only; classified SUPPORTED.

findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; valid. Binding present; attribute delete callbacks driven from c_keyhash teardown. Kept as distinct requirement.

This is the COMM info mechanism (opal_infosubscribe) and is distinct from the File/OMPIO info-round-trip in-flight item -- that bleed does NOT apply here. SUPPORTED.

chapters.title: Virtual Topologies for MPI Processes
chapters.tldr: Chapter 8 is fully SUPPORTED in the default Open MPI build. All topology construction (Cartesian, deprecated graph, distributed graph) and every local query function live in the always-built ompi/mca/topo/base; topo_base_comm_select fill_null_pointers wires every cart/graph/dist_graph entry point from mca_topo_base_* regardless of which topo component is selected, so behavior is complete even with only the basic component (priority 0). Neighborhood collectives are served by default-built components: blocking by coll/basic, nonblocking and persistent _init by coll/libnbc, with correct Section 8.6 buffer ordering and MPI_PROC_NULL handling. Large-count (_c / MPI_Count) variants of the neighborhood family are present in mpi.h.in. MPI_UNWEIGHTED/MPI_WEIGHTS_EMPTY sentinels and MPI_CART/MPI_GRAPH/MPI_DIST_GRAPH constants are defined. The only nuance is rank reordering: the REQUIRED semantics (reorder=false keeps ranks; reorder=true is a permission the standard says may be a no-op) hold on a plain base build; the optional hardware-aware reorder *quality* for MPI_Dist_graph_create comes from the topo/treematch component, which is bundled in-tree (3rd-party/treematch) and built by default (with_treematch=yes). Disabling treematch only loses the optimization, falling back to the conformant no-reorder path -> SUPPORTED with a gate note, not CONDITIONAL. Neither in-flight PR (ABI #13280, ompio-info) touches topology.

findings.topic: Topology construction: MPI_Cart_create / MPI_Graph_create / MPI_Dist_graph_create / MPI_Dist_graph_create_adjacent collectively create communicators carrying cached topology
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/topo/base/topo_base_cart_create.c; topo_base_graph_create.c; topo_base_dist_graph_create.c; topo_base_dist_graph_create_adjacent.c; C bindings ompi/mpi/c/{cart_create,graph_create,dist_graph_create,dist_graph_create_adjacent}.c.in (all present); dispatch via mca_topo_base_comm_select. topo_base_comm_select.c:282-289 fill_null_pointers wires module->topo.cart.cart_create = mca_topo_base_cart_create etc.
findings.adjudication_note: All three reviewers agree. Verified all four constructor C bindings exist and core logic is in always-built topo/base, compiled into libmpi; available regardless of selected topo component.

findings.topic: Rank reordering: reorder=true MAY remap ranks to improve communication; reorder=false keeps identical ranks (hardware-aware remap via topo/treematch for dist_graph)
findings.gate: Hardware-aware reorder *quality* for MPI_Dist_graph_create via topo/treematch component (bundled 3rd-party/treematch, with_treematch=yes default, needs hwloc). Disabling it falls back to the conformant no-reorder path.
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/topo/base/topo_base_cart_create.c:122 ('Open MPI currently ignores the reorder flag'), :177 stores reorder without remapping; topo_base_cart_map.c / topo_base_graph_map.c return calling rank. ompi/mca/topo/basic/topo_basic_component.c:80 priority=0 accepts ANY topo type (conformant no-reorder fallback for cart/graph/dist_graph). ompi/mca/topo/treematch/topo_treematch_component.c:87 priority=42; topo_treematch_dist_graph_create.c:114 reorder=false path keeps comm_old ranks, reorder=true uses hwloc remap. config/opal_config_treematch.m4:27 with_treematch=yes default, :26 external build no longer supported (bundled).
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT: b1 and b3 marked the reorder OPTIMIZATION as conditional/optional_lib_gated; b2 correctly identifies the REQUIRED semantics as default-supported. Per framework taxonomy (default-on-removable = SUPPORTED) and 'any code path = supported': the only MUSTs (reorder=false keeps ranks; reorder=true is a mere permission the standard explicitly allows to be a no-op) are satisfied on a plain base build. treematch is bundled in-tree and default-built, not an external library, so even the hardware-aware reorder is available out of the box. Resolved SUPPORTED; the treematch dependency is carried as a gate note, not a conditionality.

chapters.title: MPI Environmental Management
chapters.tldr: Chapter 9 is broadly implemented and conformant in Open MPI HEAD (71d6dcf7f4) by default, in C and use-mpi-f08: init/init_thread/finalize/initialized/finalized, the Sessions model (session_init/finalize/get_info/get_num_psets/get_nth_pset/get_pset_info, comm_create_from_group), MPI_Abort, the full error-handling surface (create/set/get/call/free errhandler for comm/win/file/session, all standard error classes/codes/strings, Add/Remove_error_*), Get_processor_name, Get_version/library_version, Wtime/Wtick, Aint_add/diff (macros), Alloc_mem/Free_mem (mpi_minimum_memory_alignment + MPI_ERR_NO_MEM), and the predefined attributes (TAG_UB, IO, WTIME_IS_GLOBAL, LASTUSEDCODE). Three genuine quality gaps remain, all on default builds with no gate: (1) the tree advertises MPI 4.1 (MPI_VERSION=4/MPI_SUBVERSION=1, Get_version returns (4,1)), not the 5.0 being audited (PARTIAL value gap); (2) MPI_Get_hw_resource_info is a stub returning an empty MPI_Info with no hwloc://-style resource keys, so the HW/RESOURCE_GUIDED discovery workflow finds nothing (PARTIAL); (3) MPI_ERRORS_ABORT is fully wired for comm/win/file but the session/instance variant is missing (eh_instance_fn left NULL) and the invoke path has no NULL guard, so ERRORS_ABORT on a session NULL-derefs/crashes instead of aborting the local process (PARTIAL). MPI_ERR_ABI is the only absent standard error class and is in-flight (PR #13280, ABI chapter).

findings.topic: MPI_Get_version returns (version,subversion); thread-safe; callable before init / after finalize
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/get_version.c.in:66-67 (*version=MPI_VERSION; *subversion=MPI_SUBVERSION); VERSION:23-24 (mpi_standard_version=4, mpi_standard_subversion=1); config/autogen_found_items.m4:12-13 (MPI_VERSION_NUM=4, MPI_SUBVERSION_NUM=1)
findings.adjudication_note: Procedure is correct, thread-safe (reads compile-time macros, no global state), callable any time. The defect is the VALUE: the tree hardcodes MPI 4.1, so Get_version returns (4,1) and the MPI_VERSION/MPI_SUBVERSION C macros + Fortran params are 4/1, not the (5,0) MPI-5.0 §9.1.1 mandates. No configure flag/MCA/library sets version=5; not in-flight (PR #13280 only adds the distinct MPI_ABI_VERSION). b2 called this missing_gap, but the symbol is present and works — it is a value non-conformance, i.e. PARTIAL (present but not fully standard-conformant), not UNSUPPORTED.

findings.topic: MPI_Get_library_version returns null-terminated impl string <= MPI_MAX_LIBRARY_VERSION_STRING-1; callable any time; thread-safe
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/get_library_version.c.in (snprintf into MPI_MAX_LIBRARY_VERSION_STRING buffer); MPI_MAX_LIBRARY_VERSION_STRING=256 ompi/include/mpi.h.in:564; f08 get_library_version_f08.F90
findings.adjudication_note: Present in C and f08; 256-char buffer satisfies the standard minimum.

findings.topic: MPI_Init / MPI_Init_thread / MPI_Finalize / MPI_Initialized / MPI_Finalized (World Model bootstrap)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/{init,init_thread,finalize,initialized,finalized}.c.in all present; f08 bindings present
findings.adjudication_note: b3 deferred these on scope grounds via a chapter-split file (for-claude/work/chapter_functions.json) that no longer exists in the tree -> treated as silent. The task scope assigns init/finalize to Chapter 9; b1/b2 did not enumerate them. All five symbols exist and are wired in C and f08; the World Model bootstrap is the default runtime path — SUPPORTED.

findings.topic: MPI_Abort terminates the job / processes of the communicator's group
findings.gate: PRRTE/PMIx runtime (default, ships with mpirun)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/abort.c.in:47-52 (param check, show_help, ompi_mpi_abort(comm, errorcode)); ompi/errhandler/errhandler_predefined.c:524 (backend_abort -> ompi_mpi_abort)

findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/session_{init,finalize,get_info,get_num_psets,get_nth_pset,get_pset_info}.c.in present; ompi/mpi/c/comm_create_from_group.c.in present
findings.adjudication_note: Part of the chapter scope; reviewers focused on the error/inquiry subset. All Sessions-model entry points and Comm_create_from_group exist in C (and f08) — SUPPORTED by default.

findings.topic: MPI_Get_processor_name returns a unique node specifier, null-terminated, sets resultlen <= MPI_MAX_PROCESSOR_NAME-1
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/get_processor_name.c.in:58-62 (gethostname, forced null-termination, resultlen=strlen); MPI_MAX_PROCESSOR_NAME=256
findings.adjudication_note: Correctly null-terminates and bounds resultlen; 256-char buffer satisfies the standard. C and f08 present.

findings.topic: MPI_Get_hw_resource_info returns MPI_Info of URI-form hardware-resource (key,value) pairs (e.g. hwloc://NUMANode) usable for HW/RESOURCE_GUIDED splitting
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/get_hw_resource_info.c.in:46-64 (validates info!=NULL -> MPI_ERR_INFO; *info=ompi_info_allocate(); MPI_ERR_NO_MEM on alloc fail; return MPI_SUCCESS with ZERO info_set calls; dev comment lines 53-56 "No resources currently being specified so just return empty info object")
findings.adjudication_note: Symbol present in C and f08, validates args, returns a valid freeable empty MPI_Info — it never crashes or violates the signature, and an empty info is a defensible degenerate-but-legal 'no restrictions' return. But no hwloc://-style resource-type keys are ever populated (even though hwloc is available), so the Example 9.1 HW/RESOURCE_GUIDED discovery workflow finds nothing on every system. Present but the substantive feature is absent = PARTIAL/stub. Not UNSUPPORTED (b2): the symbol and a legal success path exist. Not CONDITIONAL: behavior is identical on every build, no gate. Neither in-flight PR touches this file.

findings.topic: Predefined attributes MPI_TAG_UB (>=32767), MPI_IO, MPI_WTIME_IS_GLOBAL on MPI_COMM_WORLD; constant init->finalize; not user-modifiable
findings.evidence: ompi/attribute/attribute_predefined.c:133-138 (create_comm TAG_UB/HOST/IO/WTIME_IS_GLOBAL/APPNUM/LASTUSEDCODE/UNIVERSE_SIZE), :165-175 (set_f: TAG_UB=mca_pml.pml_max_tag, IO=MPI_ANY_SOURCE, WTIME_IS_GLOBAL=0); MPI_HOST set to MPI_PROC_NULL (deprecated since 4.1, correct)

findings.adjudication_note: All four object types have full create/set/get/free in C and f08 with type validation (e.g. session_set rejects non-INSTANCE/non-PREDEFINED handlers).

findings.adjudication_note: Win and File defaults match the standard and are set independently of the initial handler.

findings.topic: MPI_ERRORS_ARE_FATAL aborts connected processes (comm/win/file/session variants)
findings.evidence: ompi/errhandler/errhandler_predefined.c:68-126 (fatal comm/file/win handlers -> backend_abort fatal=true), :188-216 (fatal instance handler -> ompi_mpi_abort)
findings.adjudication_note: All four predefined fatal handlers present (including instance) and wired in errhandler.c:175-178.

findings.topic: MPI_ERRORS_RETURN returns the error code without aborting (comm/win/file/session)
findings.evidence: ompi/errhandler/errhandler_predefined.c:218-263 (no-op comm/file/win/instance return handlers); errhandler.c:189-193 (all four set incl. eh_instance_fn)
findings.adjudication_note: All four return-handler variants present, including the instance variant (eh_instance_fn set at errhandler.c:192).

findings.topic: MPI_ERRORS_ABORT (new in 4.0): comm/win/file abort the group; session aborts only the local process
findings.evidence: ompi/errhandler/errhandler.c:197-208 (ompi_mpi_errors_abort sets eh_comm_fn/eh_file_fn/eh_win_fn but NOT eh_instance_fn -> NULL via OBJ_CONSTRUCT zero-init); ompi/errhandler/errhandler_predefined.c:128-186 (abort comm/file/win handlers exist; NO ompi_mpi_errors_abort_instance_handler defined); ompi/errhandler/errhandler_invoke.c:129-134 (TYPE_INSTANCE case calls errhandler->eh_instance_fn with no NULL guard); ompi/mpi/c/session_set_errhandler.c.in:44-48 (accepts any PREDEFINED handler incl. ERRORS_ABORT)
findings.adjudication_note: Investigated the conflict in source. comm/win/file ERRORS_ABORT are correct (backend_abort fatal=false). But the session/instance variant is genuinely broken: ompi_mpi_errors_abort.eh_instance_fn is never assigned (contrast fatal at errhandler.c:178 and return at :192 which both set it), there is no ompi_mpi_errors_abort_instance_handler function at all, and the invoke path dereferences eh_instance_fn with no NULL check. MPI_Session_set_errhandler accepts ERRORS_ABORT (it is PREDEFINED), so a subsequent session error NULL-derefs and crashes instead of aborting only the local process. b2's 'default' overlooked the missing instance wiring. PARTIAL: three of four object variants conformant, the session variant is a crash bug.

findings.topic: MPI_Errhandler_free marks handler for dealloc, sets handle to MPI_ERRHANDLER_NULL
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/errhandler_free.c.in; ompi/errhandler/errhandler.c
findings.adjudication_note: Present; standard free semantics.

findings.adjudication_note: Pre-init/post-finalize callable via instance retain; thread-safe registry.

chapters.title: The Info Object
chapters.tldr: Open MPI fully and unconditionally supports MPI-5.0 Chapter 10 in this main checkout. All nine info procedures (MPI_Info_create, _create_env, _delete, _dup, _free, _get_nkeys, _get_nthkey, _get_string, _set) plus the two deprecated-but-retained queries (MPI_Info_get, MPI_Info_get_valuelen) are present in C, and all nine f08 procedures are present (default-on Fortran build). Constants MPI_MAX_INFO_KEY (36, in [33,256]), MPI_MAX_INFO_VAL (256), MPI_INFO_NULL, MPI_INFO_ENV are defined. The implementation lives in the always-compiled opal/util/info.c and ompi/info/info.c; every chapter requirement is default-SUPPORTED with no conditional, in-flight, or genuine-gap item. MPI_INFO_ENV is populated with the standard keys it can determine (command, argv, maxprocs, soft, host, arch, wdir, thread_level, mpi_initial_errhandler); the 'file' key is legitimately absent because Open MPI does not launch via the appfile mechanism the standard ties that key to, which is conformant per the standard's per-key availability wording. Reviewers were unanimous on substance; the only normalization issues were b2's self-corrected f08 caveat (adjusted to default-supported) and the b3-only deprecated-symbol note (NA, correctly).

findings.topic: Info object stores an unordered set of (key,value) string pairs; a key maps to exactly one value (set overrides existing)
findings.evidence: opal/util/info.c:162-176 (opal_info_set_nolock: info_find_key locates existing key and replaces ie_value; else OBJ_NEW entry appended at :143-153)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: set-replaces-existing and append-if-new semantics in opal_info_set_nolock. Single value per key guaranteed by find-then-replace.

findings.topic: Info caches arbitrary (key,value) pairs with no key whitelist; key recognition is not required for storage
findings.evidence: opal/util/info.c:157-176 (opal_info_set_nolock stores any key verbatim into opal_list_t super; no validation against a known-key set)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: no key whitelist anywhere in the set path; any string key/value is stored.

findings.topic: get_nkeys / get_nthkey / get_string retain all (key,value) pairs (layered-functionality cache)
findings.evidence: opal/util/info.c:348-367 (get_nthkey iterates the full list positionally); opal/util/info.c:283 (ompi_info_get_nkeys -> opal_info_get_nkeys counts whole list); no pruning of stored pairs
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: enumeration walks the complete opal_list_t. b3's caveat that get_nkeys also counts library-internal keys is not user-observable for plain user-created Info objects (no internal keys present), so no deviation; noted but does not lower status.

findings.topic: Key/value max length implementation-defined: MPI_MAX_INFO_KEY in [33,256], MPI_MAX_INFO_VAL defined
findings.evidence: opal/include/opal_config.h:2015 (OPAL_MAX_INFO_KEY 36) and :2018 (OPAL_MAX_INFO_VAL 256); ompi/include/mpi.h.in:580-581 map MPI_MAX_INFO_* to OPAL_MAX_INFO_*; config/opal_configure_options.m4:522,525 (defaults 36/256, configurable)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: MPI_MAX_INFO_KEY=36 is within [33,256]; conformant. C value 36 differs from the Fortran usable length by one (standard's KEY bound applies to the C constant). b2 forward-references PR #13280 which would fix ABI values (256/1024) but that is out-of-scope for current-main conformance and does not change the SUPPORTED verdict.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_create creates a new empty info object
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/info_create_generated.c (ompi_info_allocate); opal/util/info.c info_constructor:370+ constructs empty opal_list_t
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; verified valid. ompi_info_allocate yields an info with an empty entry list.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_set adds/overrides a pair and raises MPI_ERR_INFO_KEY / MPI_ERR_INFO_VALUE on over-length key/value
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/info_set.c.in:81-82 (key_length >= MPI_MAX_INFO_KEY -> MPI_ERR_INFO_KEY), :88-89 (value_length >= MPI_MAX_INFO_VAL -> MPI_ERR_INFO_VALUE), :99 (ompi_info_set)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: exact mandated error classes under MPI_PARAM_CHECK; set delegates to opal_info_set.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_delete removes a pair and raises MPI_ERR_INFO_NOKEY when key not defined
findings.evidence: opal/util/info.c:308 (opal_info_delete returns OPAL_ERR_NOT_FOUND when key absent); ompi/mpi/c/info_delete.c.in:77-78 (OPAL_ERR_NOT_FOUND -> MPI_ERR_INFO_NOKEY)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: explicit mapping of not-found to MPI_ERR_INFO_NOKEY in the C wrapper.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_get_string: key present -> flag=true and value copied; absent -> flag=false and value unchanged
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/info_get_string_generated.c:109-114 (flag set by ompi_info_get; value copied only when *flag true)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: value untouched when flag is false.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_get_string buflen protocol: buflen==0 returns required size (value+1 for C NUL) without modifying value; too-small buffer truncates+NUL-terminates and sets buflen to required size
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/info_get_string_generated.c:103-107 (buflen==0 -> valuelen, +1 for NUL when flag), :110-112 (else opal_string_copy(value,...,*buflen) truncates+NUL-terminates, then *buflen = length+1)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: matches the standard's required-size/truncation/NUL-inclusion protocol exactly.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_get_string (and deprecated get/get_valuelen) treat over-MPI_MAX_INFO_KEY key as erroneous
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/info_get_string.c.in (key_length >= MPI_MAX_INFO_KEY -> MPI_ERR_INFO_KEY); ompi/mpi/c/info_get.c.in:81-84 and info_get_valuelen.c.in:73-76 same check
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2; verified valid. Over-length key rejected with MPI_ERR_INFO_KEY under MPI_PARAM_CHECK across the get-family.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_get_nkeys returns the number of currently defined keys
findings.evidence: ompi/info/info.c:283-285 (ompi_info_get_nkeys -> opal_info_get_nkeys returns list length)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; verified valid.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_get_nthkey returns the nth key with stable 0..N-1 numbering while info unmodified
findings.evidence: opal/util/info.c:348-367 (positional list walk; OPAL_ERR_BAD_PARAM when n past end)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; verified valid. Stable ordering follows the insertion-ordered opal_list_t.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_dup duplicates info into an independent object with the same (key,value) pairs and same key ordering
findings.evidence: ompi/info/info.c:246-247 (ompi_info_dup -> opal_info_dup_public); opal/util/info.c:72-95 (opal_info_dup_impl walks list with OPAL_LIST_FOREACH preserving order, deep-copies entries)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: insertion order preserved; produces an independent object. dup_public filters library-internal and never-referenced entries (info.c:79), but for user Info objects every user-set key is referenced, so all user keys are duplicated -- conformant (b3 noted this is not user-observable).

findings.topic: MPI_Info_free frees info and sets the handle to MPI_INFO_NULL
findings.evidence: ompi/info/info.c:433-443 (ompi_info_free OBJ_RELEASE then *info = MPI_INFO_NULL); ompi/mpi/c/info_free.c.in:71 calls ompi_info_free; ompi/include/mpi.h.in:991 (MPI_INFO_NULL predefined global)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: handle nulled to MPI_INFO_NULL after release.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_create_env produces an info consistent with MPI_INFO_ENV; user frees via MPI_Info_free; ISO C binding accepts argc/argv (0/NULL allowed), Fortran omits them
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/info_create_env.c.in:47 (PROTOTYPE INT argc, STRING_ARRAY argv, INFO_OUT info), :60-67 (ompi_info_allocate then ompi_mpiinfo_init_env); ompi/info/info.c:130-136 (NULL argv falls back to ompi_process_info.command); ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/info_create_env_f08.F90
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: same init helper (ompi_mpiinfo_init_env) builds both MPI_INFO_ENV and create_env results; NULL argv handled defensively.

findings.topic: MPI_Info_create_env is thread-safe and callable before MPI init / after finalize (local); object may be incompletely populated when values unavailable
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/info_create_env.c.in:47-69 (no MPI-init-state guard); ompi/info/info.c:125-238 reads ompi_process_info.* fields with NULL checks; per-object locking opal/util/info.c info_constructor (i_lock = OBJ_NEW(opal_mutex_t))
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: no init/finalize gate in wrapper or helper; defensive NULL-checks permit partial population, exactly as the standard's advice-to-users allows.

findings.topic: MPI_INFO_ENV exposes the §11.2.1 reserved keys (command, argv, maxprocs, soft, host, arch, wdir, file, thread_level)
findings.evidence: ompi/info/info.c:125-238 (ompi_mpiinfo_init_env sets command:140, argv:154, maxprocs:162, soft:164, mpi_initial_errhandler:170, host:174, arch:181 under HAVE_SYS_UTSNAME_H, wdir:187 if initial_wdir, thread_level:195-204); ompi/include/mpi.h.in:999 (MPI_INFO_ENV); docs/man-openmpi/man3/MPI_Info_env.3.rst:25,60 (per-key 'If available' wording; 'file' documented)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: all standard keys Open MPI can determine are populated. Open MPI's own MPI_INFO_ENV man page (MPI_Info_env.3.rst:25 'If available, the value will be set to...') documents these reserved keys with availability-dependent population, and lists 'file' (line 60) as a recognized key. The 'file' key is absent at runtime because Open MPI does not use the appfile launch mechanism the standard associates with it; population is per-key conditional, so its absence is conformant. 'soft' is set equal to maxprocs (Open MPI lacks soft-spawn). Extra OMPI-specific keys (ompi_num_apps, ompi_first_rank, ompi_np, ompi_positioned_file_dir) are permitted. SUPPORTED.

findings.topic: Info argument is interpreted before the procedure returns; caller may read/modify/free it immediately afterward (all procedures)
findings.evidence: opal/util/info.c:72-95 (consuming paths dup entries into private opal_info_t); ompi/info/info.c:246 (ompi_info_dup copies); set/get copy by value, no retained reference to the user handle
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: consumers copy required info into their own state at call time; the caller's handle is independent on return.

findings.topic: Portable value-string representations: booleans 'true'/'false' and decimal integers within a standard integer type
findings.evidence: opal/class/opal_cstring.c (opal_str_to_bool accepts true/false plus permissive yes/no/digits; opal_cstring_to_int via strtol with overflow check); opal/util/info.c (opal_info_get_bool)
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: mandated 'true'/'false' accepted; integer parsing has overflow handling. Per-key interpretation at point-of-use is permitted by the standard.

findings.topic: Both key and value are case sensitive
findings.evidence: opal/util/info.c:431-444 (info_find_key matches keys with strcmp, not strcasecmp); values stored verbatim
findings.adjudication_note: Verified: case-sensitive key matching via strcmp; values copied verbatim.

findings.topic: Info accessors are thread-safe (per-object serialization)
findings.evidence: opal/util/info.c OPAL_THREAD_LOCK/UNLOCK around get/set/delete/valuelen/nthkey (e.g. get_nthkey :355, get_nkeys/get/delete paths); per-object i_lock created in info_constructor
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; verified valid. Each opal_info_t carries its own mutex; accessor operations lock it.

findings.topic: Fortran mpi_f08 bindings for the nine standard info procedures
findings.gate: removable via --disable-mpi-fortran (default ON; requires a Fortran compiler)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/info_{create,create_env,delete,dup,free,get_nkeys,get_nthkey,get_string,set}_f08.F90 all present; config/ompi_configure_options.m4 default-ON Fortran gate
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2 (which self-corrected its initial 'conditional' to default-supported). Per the framework, mpi_f08 is built by default and --disable-mpi-fortran is a removable default-on flag -> SUPPORTED with a gate note, not CONDITIONAL. All nine f08 info files exist with real implementations.

findings.topic: Deprecated MPI_Info_get / MPI_Info_get_valuelen (removed from MPI-4.1 standard body) retained as working back-compat bindings
findings.standard_ref: MPI-4.0 deprecation; removed in MPI-4.1 / absent from MPI-5.0 §10 body
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/info_get.c.in:57-100 (validates handle/valuelen/key/value/flag, copies via opal_string_copy(value,...,valuelen+1)); ompi/mpi/c/info_get_valuelen.c.in:54-86 (-> ompi_info_get_valuelen, opal/util/info.c:325)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b3; correctly NA. Neither routine is part of the MPI-5.0 Chapter 10 procedure set (MPI_Info_get_string replaces them), so they impose no MPI-5.0 requirement. Open MPI retains correct, functional bindings for backward compatibility -- not required, hence NA, not a gap.

findings.topic: Fortran leading/trailing space stripping on info key/value (Fortran-binding-only rule)
findings.gate: removable via --disable-mpi-fortran (default ON)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/info_set_f08.F90 -> ompi/mpi/fortran/mpif-h/info_set_f.c:86-87 (calls ompi_fortran_string_f2c on key and value); ompi/mpi/fortran/base/strings.c (ompi_fortran_string_f2c strips leading spaces at :10 loop ' '==*fstr, and trailing spaces at :17 reverse loop ' '==*end); C bindings correctly do not strip
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b3 (marked 'assumed'); now VERIFIED. The Fortran info_set path converts key/value via ompi_fortran_string_f2c, which strips both leading and trailing blanks (strings.c:10,17) before calling PMPI_Info_set -- exactly the Fortran-only space-handling the standard requires. C bindings correctly leave C strings untouched. SUPPORTED with the standard Fortran-disable gate note; not in-flight.

chapters.title: Process Initialization, Creation, and Management
chapters.tldr: Open MPI on main (HEAD 71d6dcf7f4) provides a default, conforming implementation of essentially all of Chapter 11: the World Model (Init/Init_thread/Finalize, four thread levels incl. MPI_THREAD_MULTIPLE, MPI_INFO_ENV, Initialized/Finalized, Abort), the Sessions Model (Session_init/finalize, pset enumeration/query, Comm/Intercomm_create_from_group), and full dynamic process management (Comm_spawn[_multiple], Comm_get_parent, Open/Close_port, Comm_accept/connect/disconnect, Comm_join, Publish/Lookup/Unpublish_name). The central reconciliation question is b3's labeling of all 13 dynamic/session-pset routines as 'optional_lib_gated': per the framework's pre-resolved fact (and b3's OWN caveat at ch11.json:652-655 stating PMIx is a MANDATORY build dependency so every routine compiles unconditionally), these are SUPPORTED by default with a runtime gate note 'requires PRRTE/PMIx runtime (default, ships with Open MPI)', NOT CONDITIONAL. The b3 status is a labeling difference about RUNTIME capability, not a real conformance conflict. The only genuine non-default-path item is the FT-MPI/ULFM subset-abort resilience advice-to-implementors behavior; the only genuine gap is the two Table-11.1 ABI routines (MISSING_INFLIGHT via PR #13280). 'soft' spawning and other uninterpreted reserved keys are expressly permitted by the standard (NA). The mpi_memory_alloc_kinds round-trip on MPI_File is in-flight (#13367).

findings.topic: MPI_Init / MPI_Init_thread bootstrap the World Model; accept NULL argc/argv; exactly-once; COMM_WORLD/SELF valid after
findings.evidence: ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_init.c:340 (ompi_mpi_init -> ompi_mpi_instance_init); ompi/mpi/c/init.c.in, init_thread.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: World-model bootstrap via the instance layer; NULL argc/argv accepted. b1+b2 agree; b3 only flagged dynamic/session-pset symbols (silent here).

findings.topic: Four thread levels granted incl. MPI_THREAD_MULTIPLE; provided>=requested; thread-compliant operation
findings.evidence: ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_init.c:131-329 (ompi_mpi_thread_level / ompi_mpi_thread_multiple); no enable_mpi_thread_multiple configure flag
findings.adjudication_note: All four levels grantable in the default 5.x build; MPI_THREAD_MULTIPLE has no build-time gate. Runtime perf may vary (not a conformance issue).

findings.topic: MPI_Query_thread / MPI_Is_thread_main report negotiated provided level / identify main thread
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/query_thread.c.in, is_thread_main.c.in; ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_init.c:320,328 (ompi_mpi_main_thread / thread_provided)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous; light verification confirms both routines return the negotiated level/main-thread identity.

findings.topic: MPI_Initialized / MPI_Finalized always callable before init and after finalize; thread-safe (Table 11.1 always-available subset)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/initialized.c.in; ompi/mpi/c/finalized.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: Always-available subset; both return false for Sessions-only apps as required.

findings.topic: MPI_Finalize cleans up World-Model state; collective over connected procs; COMM_SELF freed first triggering attribute delete callbacks in reverse insertion order
findings.evidence: ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_finalize.c:141-195; ompi/attribute/attribute.c:1237 (reverse-order delete)
findings.adjudication_note: COMM_SELF freed first; attribute callbacks run in reverse insertion order. Does not free Sessions/parent objects.

findings.topic: mpi_initial_errhandler info key selects initial error handler (errors_are_fatal / errors_abort / errors_return) before/during init
findings.evidence: ompi/info/info.c:170; ompi/errhandler/errhandler_predefined.c (MPI_ERRORS_ABORT handler); ompi/runtime/ompi_rte.c
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2; verified. MPI_ERRORS_ABORT is a real predefined handler and the info key is consumed at init. Valid -> kept.

findings.topic: MPI_INFO_ENV exposes predefined keys (command, argv, maxprocs, mpi_initial_errhandler, soft, host, arch, wdir, thread_level)
findings.evidence: ompi/info/info.c:125-204 (ompi_mpiinfo_init_env); ompi/instance/instance.c:492
findings.adjudication_note: Required §11.2.1 keys populated; the precise set is implementation-dependent per standard. 'file' / 'mpi_memory_alloc_kinds' not in INFO_ENV (permitted). Note: info.c:163 states the read-only INFO_ENV 'soft' is set to maxprocs; this is the INFO_ENV object, distinct from spawn-key interpretation.

findings.topic: MPI_Session_init local & thread-safe; honors thread_level and mpi_memory_alloc_kinds info keys; multiple sessions per process
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/session_init.c.in:20-58 (thread_level parsed at :44-52); ompi/instance/instance.c:851
findings.adjudication_note: Sessions Model present via instance layer. Default session thread level is SINGLE (advice-to-implementors recommends otherwise; non-normative).

findings.topic: MPI_Session_get_info returns the session's hints incl. the provided thread_level
findings.evidence: ompi/instance/instance.c:902 (ONLY mpi_memory_alloc_kinds subscribed into session info via opal_infosubscribe_subscribe); ompi/mpi/c/session_get_info.c.in (dups session->super.s_info into info_used — no thread_level added); session_init.c.in:45 (thread_level parsed, stored as int i_thread_level instance.h:31, never re-published as a key); info.c:195-204 sets 'thread_level' only on the WORLD-model MPI_INFO_ENV object, NOT the session info
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict resolved to PARTIAL (b1 correct, b2 wrong). The MPI-5.0 standard text is explicit and normative: §11.3.1 (p.490, PDF) states 'The thread support level actually provided by the MPI implementation can be determined via a subsequent call to MPI_SESSION_GET_INFO to return the info object associated with the Session', and §11.3.4 requires GET_INFO to return 'all hints that are supported by the implementation and have default values specified; any user-supplied hints that were not ignored by the implementation'. thread_level is a supported, non-ignored predefined hint (parsed and acted on at session_init.c.in:45), yet Open MPI never adds it to session->super.s_info — only mpi_memory_alloc_kinds is subscribed (instance.c:902). So MPI_Session_get_info cannot report the provided thread level as the standard designates. Present but not fully conformant -> PARTIAL. (The thread_level key IS set on the separate MPI_INFO_ENV object at info.c:195-204, which does not satisfy this requirement.)

findings.topic: MPI_Session_finalize cleans up session state, sets handle to MPI_SESSION_NULL, may synchronize on derived groups
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/session_finalize.c.in; ompi/instance/instance.c (ompi_mpi_instance_finalize)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; verified present. Symbol and instance teardown path exist.

findings.topic: MPI_ERRORS_ABORT predefined initial error handler and MPI_ERR_PROC_ABORTED error class exist
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:759 (MPI_ERR_PROC_ABORTED=74), :1434 (MPI_ERRORS_ABORT handler); ompi/errhandler/errhandler_predefined.c:343-345,486-492
findings.adjudication_note: The constant/handler EXISTENCE is default-present (distinct from the runtime continued-communication behavior below).

findings.topic: Subset-abort resilience: non-aborted processes keep communicating and receive MPI_ERR_PROC_ABORTED when touching aborted peers
findings.evidence: MPI-5.0 standard §11.4.2 MPI_Abort, 'Advice to implementors' box (for-claude/mpi50-report.pdf, verified text): 'After aborting a subset of processes, a high-quality implementation should be able to provide error handling... the remaining processes ... should be able to continue communicating ... and receive an appropriate error code ... (e.g., an error of class MPI_ERR_PROC_ABORTED). ... (End of advice to implementors.)'; ompi/errhandler/errhandler.c:416 (sole runtime delivery of MPI_ERR_PROC_ABORTED) inside #if OPAL_ENABLE_FT_MPI (block 314-452); errhandler.c:521-525 default fall-through aborts; runtime gate ompi_ftmpi_enabled default false (ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_params.c:100-126)
findings.adjudication_note: Resolved to NA, now grounded in the verified standard text. The MPI-5.0 §11.4.2 surviving-process continued-communication / MPI_ERR_PROC_ABORTED-delivery behavior is explicitly enclosed in an 'Advice to implementors' box ('a high-quality implementation should ...', ending '(End of advice to implementors.)') — non-binding, not a normative requirement. Per the taxonomy, advice-to-implementors that does not bind an implementation = NA. b2 (with detailed verification) is correct on the SOURCE facts (delivery exists only under --with-ft=mpi / OPAL_ENABLE_FT_MPI plus runtime ompi_ftmpi_enabled, not in a default build), so its CONDITIONAL would be right IF the behavior were normative; but because the standard frames it as advice, NA is the correct taxonomy value. The genuinely required behavior — MPI_Abort aborting the comm group, plus the existence of MPI_ERRORS_ABORT / MPI_ERR_PROC_ABORTED — is SUPPORTED (separate topics above).

findings.topic: MPI_Comm_spawn / MPI_Comm_spawn_multiple start maxprocs children, return intercomm, MPI_ERR_SPAWN on failure; MPI_ARGV(S)_NULL, MPI_ERRCODES_IGNORE honored; ordered child ranks
findings.gate: requires PRRTE/PMIx runtime supporting PMIx_Spawn (default, ships with Open MPI's mpirun); also runtime-disableable via MCA mpi_dynamics_enabled (default true)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/comm_spawn.c.in:103 (gate ompi_mpi_dynamics_is_enabled, default true), :44; comm_spawn_multiple.c.in:44; ompi/dpm/dpm.c:829 (ompi_dpm_spawn), :1639 (PMIx_Spawn); ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_params.c:84,330 (mpi_dynamics_enabled=true); array_of_errcodes filled comm_spawn.c.in:170-172
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict resolved to SUPPORTED. Code compiles unconditionally (PMIx is a mandatory build dep — b3's own caveat ch11.json:652-655). b3's optional_lib_gated refers to RUNTIME capability of the active launcher; the DEFAULT launcher is PRRTE/mpirun, which supports PMIx_Spawn -> SUPPORTED with gate note. mpi_dynamics_enabled defaults true (default-on-removable = SUPPORTED). spawn_multiple enforces a single child COMM_WORLD with ordered ranks. b1's separate runtime/dynamics 'conditional_on' note is the same gate, not a build gate.

findings.topic: Reserved spawn info keys (host, wdir, path, arch, file, soft) interpreted per §11.8.4 if the implementation chooses to interpret them
findings.evidence: ompi/dpm/dpm.c:966 ('host' -> PMIX_HOST), :995 ('wdir' -> PMIX_WDIR) interpreted unconditionally in the default spawn loop; dpm.c:1036 comment 'path, arch, file, soft -- to be implemented' (permissibly uninterpreted); dpm.c compiled unconditionally into libmpi (ompi/Makefile.am:170, not an MCA component)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2 (whose own verification 'adjusted' the original label to default). Resolved to SUPPORTED. MPI-5.0 §11.8.4 states an implementation is NOT required to interpret reserved keys, but if it does, it must provide the described functionality. Open MPI interprets host/wdir correctly (mapped to PMIX_HOST/PMIX_WDIR) on every default build, and interprets no key incorrectly. The uninterpreted path/arch/file/soft keys are expressly permitted (a completeness observation, not a gap). No configure flag / component / external lib gate -> not CONDITIONAL.

findings.topic: 'soft' spawning: returning a partial process count instead of MPI_ERR_SPAWN
findings.evidence: ompi/dpm/dpm.c:1036 ('soft -- to be implemented'); ompi/dpm/dpm.c:863 (comment referencing 'soft' MPI-2 semantics)
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict resolved to NA. Both b1 (UNSUPPORTED) and b2 (PARTIAL) are wrong on the taxonomy: the 'soft' reserved key is one the implementation NEED NOT interpret (§11.8.4), and the standard explicitly does NOT require soft spawning. An optional feature MPI permits omitting = NA. Open MPI does not interpret 'soft' (dpm.c:1036), which is conformant. b1's own caveat already acknowledges 'standard explicitly does not require soft spawning'.

chapters.title: One-Sided Communications (RMA)
chapters.tldr: Open MPI's MPI-5.0 Chapter 12 (RMA) support is overwhelmingly SUPPORTED by default. The osc framework ships a built-in, ungated default path: osc/rdma (priority 20, builds over any BTL with a software lock-based atomic fallback) for general windows, and osc/sm (priority 100, no configure.m4) for shared-memory windows. All RMA procedures (window create/allocate/allocate_shared/create_dynamic, attach/detach, Put/Get/Accumulate/Get_accumulate/Fetch_and_op/Compare_and_swap, the R-variants, Fence/PSCW/Lock(_all)/Flush(_local)(_all)/Sync) are present in both C and use-mpi-f08 bindings, and all twelve RMA error classes are defined. User-defined ops are rejected with MPI_ERR_OP at the C binding (accumulate.c.in:67-68); REPLACE/NO_OP are special-cased. Memory model reports MPI_WIN_UNIFIED (fully conformant; SEPARATE is not required) and osc/portals4 can report SEPARATE. UCX (osc/ucx), Portals4 (osc/portals4), and UBCL (osc/ubcl) are OPTIONAL accelerators gated on external libraries, not required for conformance. The single genuine deviation is PARTIAL: MPI_Win_get_info on the default rdma path does not synthesize default values for honored-but-unsubscribed hints (same_size, same_disp_unit, accumulate_ops); only no_locks is subscribed and round-tripped (TODO acknowledged at osc_rdma_component.c:1518). User-supplied non-ignored hints DO round-trip. Two ignorable info hints have no effect on the default path: accumulate_ordering is parsed under the misspelled key 'accumulate_order' with zero consumers in osc/rdma, and mpi_accumulate_granularity is recognized by no osc component; both are permitted to be ignored (Sec 12.2.7) and the conformant strict default ordering is always enforced. Nothing in this chapter is in-flight (PR #13280 ABI and the OMPIO MPI_File-info branch do not touch ch12).

findings.topic: RMA procedures present (C + mpi_f08 bindings) for all window/comm/sync ops
findings.gate: mpi_f08 bindings removable via --disable-mpi-fortran (default ON)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/win_*.c.in, put.c.in, get.c.in, accumulate.c.in, get_accumulate.c.in, fetch_and_op.c.in, compare_and_swap.c.in, rput.c.in, rget.c.in, raccumulate.c.in, rget_accumulate.c.in; ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/ win_*/accumulate/rput/rget files
findings.adjudication_note: Both reviewers that addressed it agree all RMA procedures exist in C and f08. C templates emit large-count _c variants per c_variant_present flags. SUPPORTED by default; f08 default-on but removable (gate note).

findings.topic: osc framework provides a default RMA code path (osc/rdma + osc/sm, ungated)
findings.gate: whole RMA area removable via --disable osc (configure-gated, not a conformance failure)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/osc/rdma/configure.m4 (no build gating, 'We can always build, unless explicitly disabled'); osc_rdma_component.c:237 (priority 20); ompi/mca/osc/sm (no configure.m4, priority 100, osc_sm_component.c:131)
findings.adjudication_note: Framework fact: osc/rdma + osc/sm are default built-in. rdma builds over any BTL (incl. sm/tcp/self) with a software lock-based atomic fallback (ompi_osc_rdma_lock_acquire_exclusive + cas_local), so atomics are conformant without an accelerated BTL. SUPPORTED by default.

findings.topic: MPI_WIN_CREATE over user memory (FLAVOR_CREATE), collective over comm
findings.evidence: ompi/win/win.c:261 (create flavor routes through alloc_window); ompi/mca/osc/rdma/osc_rdma_component.c (allocate_module flavor handling)
findings.adjudication_note: Standard window creation over a user buffer. Unanimous, verified.

findings.topic: MPI_WIN_ALLOCATE returns MPI-allocated (symmetric) memory (FLAVOR_ALLOCATE)
findings.evidence: ompi/win/win.c:292 (allocate flavor); ompi/mca/osc/rdma/osc_rdma_component.c (same_size/same_disp symmetric alloc path)
findings.adjudication_note: ALLOCATE flavor handled by rdma; symmetric-memory optimization keyed on same_size/same_disp_unit. Unanimous, verified.

findings.topic: MPI_WIN_ALLOCATE_SHARED + MPI_WIN_SHARED_QUERY (FLAVOR_SHARED), alloc_shared_noncontig
findings.gate: shareable single-node memory; raises MPI_ERR_RMA_SHARED if not shareable
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/osc/sm/osc_sm_component.c:173-208 (SHARED/ALLOCATE handling, MPI_ERR_RMA_SHARED at :208), :271-278 (alloc_shared_noncontig), :486 (shared_query); osc_rdma_component.c:1647 (shared_query, ALLOCATE flavor only)

findings.topic: MPI_WIN_CREATE_DYNAMIC + MPI_WIN_ATTACH / MPI_WIN_DETACH (dynamic windows)
findings.gate: attaching a GPU/accelerator buffer needs a BTL able to register it, else OMPI_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/osc/rdma/osc_rdma_component.c:120-121 (.osc_win_attach/.osc_win_detach); osc_rdma_dynamic.c (attach/detach, GPU-register check :193); ompi/win/win.c:354 (create_dynamic flavor)
findings.adjudication_note: Dynamic-window flavor with attach/detach supported by rdma (and registered by sm/ucx/portals4). target_disp = absolute address, MPI_BOTTOM base. Unanimous, verified.

findings.topic: MPI_WIN_FREE collective destruction (delays for passive-target completion unless no_locks)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/osc/rdma/osc_rdma_component.c:122 (.osc_free = ompi_osc_rdma_free)
findings.adjudication_note: UNIQUE (b1 only). Valid: window free is wired in the rdma module template; collective destruction. SUPPORTED.

findings.topic: Window attributes BASE/SIZE/DISP_UNIT/CREATE_FLAVOR/MODEL cached and queryable
findings.evidence: ompi/win/win.c:214-243 (config_window sets all five attrs); win.c:229 (DISP_UNIT stored as (int)disp_unit per Table 12.1)

Conformant; comment at win.c:223-228 documents this. SUPPORTED.

Promoted to a consolidated SUPPORTED row covering the actual user-keyval PROCEDURES (create_keyval/free_keyval/set_attr/get_attr/delete_attr), a real §12.2.6 requirement that was otherwise only partially captured. All present in C bindings. SUPPORTED.

findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/accumulate.c.in:67-68 (returns MPI_ERR_OP when !ompi_op_is_intrinsic(op)); osc_rdma_accumulate.c (op mapping, software fallback via gacc_local)

SUPPORTED.

findings.adjudication_note: REPLACE/NO_OP special-cased; atomic vs bounded-lock path chosen per acc_single_intrinsic and accumulate_ops hint. Conformant via software fallback when BTL lacks hardware atomics. SUPPORTED.

findings.topic: Request-based RMA (MPI_RPUT/RGET/RACCUMULATE/RGET_ACCUMULATE) completed via MPI test/wait
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/osc/rdma/osc_rdma_component.c:131-134 (.osc_r* function pointers); osc_rdma_request.c; ompi/mpi/c/rput.c.in, rget.c.in, raccumulate.c.in, rget_accumulate.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: R-variants return MPI_Request completed by standard test/wait; valid in passive-target epoch. Unanimous, verified.

findings.topic: Memory model: window reports MPI_WIN_UNIFIED or MPI_WIN_SEPARATE via MPI_WIN_MODEL
findings.evidence: osc_rdma_component.c:1584 (MPI_WIN_UNIFIED); osc_sm_component.c:469 (UNIFIED); osc_ucx_component.c:668 (UNIFIED); osc_portals4_component.c:597-599 (UNIFIED or SEPARATE)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT resolved to SUPPORTED. b1 marked PARTIAL because the default rdma path always reports UNIFIED and never SEPARATE. Overruled: the standard does not require offering SEPARATE; UNIFIED is the stronger model and is always permitted. Reporting UNIFIED unconditionally is fully conformant. portals4 can report SEPARATE anyway. b2/b3 (SUPPORTED) are correct.

findings.topic: Active-target sync: MPI_WIN_FENCE
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/osc/rdma/osc_rdma_component.c:136 (.osc_fence); osc_rdma_active_target.c

findings.topic: MPI_WIN_SET_INFO / MPI_WIN_GET_INFO procedures present and functional
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/win_get_info.c.in:56 (opal_info_dup_public of win->super.s_info); win_set_info.c.in; osc_rdma_component.c:1515 (infosubscribe)
findings.adjudication_note: The set_info/get_info procedures themselves work and round-trip user-supplied non-ignored hints. Listed separately from the defaulted-hint synthesis gap below.

findings.topic: MPI_WIN_GET_INFO returns all supported hints WITH DEFAULT VALUES (default-value synthesis)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/osc/rdma/osc_rdma_component.c:1515 (subscribes only 'no_locks'), :1517-1520 (TODO: same_size/same_disp_unit not subscribed); opal/util/info.c:79 (opal_info_dup_public skips keys with ie_referenced==0); osc_rdma_component.c:1416-1417 (rdma reads same_size/same_disp_unit via check_config_value_bool -> opal_info_get, which refs only USER-supplied keys, info.c:124)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT resolved to PARTIAL, siding with b3 (medium-confidence minority). The standard requires get_info to return all hints 'supported by the implementation and have default values specified.' On the default rdma path, only no_locks is subscribed and thus injected with a default into s_info, so it round-trips. same_size/same_disp_unit/accumulate_ops are honored internally but have NO default injected, so their DEFAULTS are not reported by MPI_Win_get_info (developer TODO at :1518 acknowledges this). User-supplied non-ignored hints DO round-trip (check_config_value_bool/enum-get call opal_info_get which increments ie_referenced). Scope is narrow: procedures work, user hints round-trip; the gap is only default-value synthesis for honored-but-unsubscribed hints. Does not affect RMA data-movement correctness. b1/b2 (SUPPORTED) overweighted the working procedure path; the TODO + dup_public ie_referenced==0 skip make PARTIAL the honest call. Not in-flight (inflight.json covers only ABI #13280 and OMPIO MPI_File-info).

findings.topic: accumulate_ordering info hint (relax RMA accumulate ordering at target)
findings.evidence: ompi/win/win.c:140 (enum created under misspelled 'accumulate_order'), :185-194 (read under 'accumulate_order'); w_acc_order has ZERO consumers in osc/rdma (grep); standard spelling used only by non-default osc/portals4 (osc_portals4_component.c:570,574); default strict ordering rar|war|raw|waw enforced (win.c:186-187)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT resolved to SUPPORTED-with-caveat. The standard-spelled key 'accumulate_ordering' is registered/read under the misspelled name 'accumulate_order' (no 'ing'), and the parsed w_acc_order value has no consumer in osc/rdma, so the relaxation hint is inert on the default path. b2's UNSUPPORTED (missing_gap) is overruled: recognizing an optional info hint is NOT required behavior. The standard explicitly permits ignoring hints (12.2.7), and the conformant strict default ordering is always enforced, so window correctness holds. b1's PARTIAL is also overruled: the chapter requirement (correct accumulate ordering) is met; this is a usability/spelling defect in an ignorable optimization, not incompleteness of required behavior. Real but non-conformance-blocking; caveat noted in tldr.

findings.topic: mpi_accumulate_granularity info hint

UNSUPPORTED is reserved for genuinely-required absent behavior; recognizing an optional hint is not required. The window works; the optional hint has no effect. Caveat noted in tldr.

chapters.title: External Interfaces
chapters.tldr: Open MPI's main branch implements MPI-5.0 Chapter 13 by default in C: generalized requests (MPI_Grequest_start/complete plus the three query/free/cancel callbacks with correct deferred-free, cancel-complete-flag, and error-code propagation semantics), all status setters (MPI_Status_set_elements/_x, set_cancelled, set_source/tag/error), the six status conversion routines (f2c/c2f/c2f08/f082c/f2f08/f082f), MPI_Request_get_status{,_any,_some,_all}, datatype decoding (MPI_Type_get_envelope/contents +_c), and thread support (Init_thread/Query_thread/Is_thread_main with provided=requested up to MPI_THREAD_MULTIPLE, ungated). All of this is unconditional default code; --enable-grequest-extensions gates only the non-standard MPIX extension, not standard grequests. The one genuine gap is the Fortran (mpi_f08/mpi-module/mpif.h) binding for all six MPI_Status_{get,set}_{source,tag,error} routines: only C bindings exist on main, and no in-flight PR adds them. The underlying C behavior is reachable, but the standard-mandated Fortran bindings are absent (UNSUPPORTED), so b1's PARTIAL framing dissolves into a SUPPORTED C-behavior topic plus an UNSUPPORTED f08-binding topic.

findings.adjudication_note: Verified. Matches spec exactly; C/Fortran dispatch on greq_funcs_are_c. SUPPORTED.

The C bindings and behavior are present and conformant; however the six MPI_Status_{get,set}_{source,tag,error} procedures have NO mpi_f08 binding (a genuine gap counted once in Chapter 19, Language Bindings).

findings.topic: Thread support: MPI_Init_thread provided level, MPI_Query_thread, MPI_Is_thread_main, the four THREAD_* levels
findings.evidence: ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_init.c:322 (ompi_mpi_thread_level: provided=requested for any requested level up to MPI_THREAD_MULTIPLE), 328 (thread_multiple flag), 133 (static init MPI_THREAD_SINGLE); ompi/mpi/c/init.c.in:41 (plain MPI_Init requests MPI_THREAD_SINGLE); query_thread.c.in:41 (*provided=ompi_mpi_thread_provided); is_thread_main.c.in:43 (opal_thread_self_compare(ompi_mpi_main_thread)); init_thread.c.in present

Plain MPI_Init defaults to MPI_THREAD_SINGLE; MPI_Init_thread(MPI_THREAD_MULTIPLE) yields MULTIPLE. Query_thread/Is_thread_main correctly report state. SUPPORTED.

chapters.title: I/O
chapters.tldr: Open MPI's main branch broadly conforms to MPI-5.0 Chapter 14. OMPIO is the sole MPI-IO provider (ROMIO is absent from the tree) at default priority 30, built and selected automatically on a plain ./configure && make with no special flag, no non-default MCA selection, and no external library (default fs/ufs + fbtl/posix cover generic filesystems). The whole MPI_File_* surface -- open/close/delete/set_size/preallocate/get_size/get_group/get_amode, set_view/get_view, all three data-access positioning families (explicit-offset, individual pointer, shared pointer), blocking/nonblocking, split-collective begin/end, nonblocking-collective iread/iwrite_*_all, atomicity/sync, type-extent, and the per-File default-MPI_ERRORS_RETURN error handler -- is therefore SUPPORTED by default; the only build-time caveat is removability via --disable-io-ompio, which strips the only io component with no fallback (gate note, not a conformance gap). Required data representations native and external32 are SUPPORTED (external32 clones ompi_mpi_external32_convertor and the read/write engine performs the byte conversion). Three genuine deviations stand out: the required 'internal' data representation is rejected with MPI_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_DATAREP (set_view's gate accepts only native/external32) -> UNSUPPORTED; user-defined data representations via MPI_Register_datarep are non-functional (OMPIO's register_datarep is a hard 'return OMPI_ERROR' stub) -> UNSUPPORTED; and two narrow status-reporting bugs (get_type_extent ignores f_datarep so external32 views get the native extent, and collective reads set status._ucount to the requested rather than the actual byte count, breaking EOF-by-short-read detection) -> PARTIAL. Full per-File MPI_Info hint round-trip (so MPI_File_get_info reports all hints OMPIO recognized/defaulted, set via open/set_info) is MISSING_INFLIGHT via branch pr/ompio-and-mpi-info-lets-be-friends (issue #13367); the MPI_File_set_info/get_info symbols themselves are present and return valid (minimal) results today. All 17 I/O error classes are defined.

findings.topic: MPI-IO subsystem (all MPI_File_* file management + data access) available by default via OMPIO

findings.evidence: ompi/mca/io/ompio/io_ompio_component.c:90,137 (priority_param=30, default-selected); config/ompi_configure_options.m4:245-252 (ompi_want_ompio=1 unless --disable-io-ompio); ompi/mca/io/ only base/ + ompio/ (no romio); ompi/file/file.c routes MPI_File_open through mca_io_base_file_select; default fs/ufs + fbtl/posix need no external lib
findings.adjudication_note: All three reviewers agree the entire MPI_File surface works on a default build; b3 marked 38 individual File ops 'config_gated' but per framework default-on-removable = SUPPORTED with a gate note, not 38 CONDITIONAL rows. Collapsed into ONE topic. The 38 verified flagged_symbols (open/close/delete/set_size/preallocate/get_size/get_group/get_amode/set_view/get_view/seek/get_position/get_byte_offset/get_type_extent/set_atomicity/get_atomicity/sync and all read/write families) all dispatch to real OMPIO backends, not stubs.
