findings.topic: MPI_File_set_view / get_view (displacement, etype, filetype, MPI_DISPLACEMENT_CURRENT in sequential mode)
findings.gate: io/ompio (default, removable via --disable-io-ompio)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/io/ompio/io_ompio_file_set_view.c:59-115 (validates etype/filetype, gates datarep, applies disp/etype/filetype; MPI_DISPLACEMENT_CURRENT->MPI_ERR_DISP unless MPI_MODE_SEQUENTIAL, lines 82-86); get_view returns disp/etype/filetype/datarep (lines 95-115)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous SUPPORTED for the view mechanism itself. Datarep acceptance is split out into separate native/external32/internal topics below.

findings.topic: 'native' data representation (no conversion)
findings.gate: io/ompio (default, removable via --disable-io-ompio)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/common/ompio/common_ompio_file_view.c:129-132 (else branch creates opal_local_arch convertor and sets OMPIO_DATAREP_NATIVE); accepted by the set_view gate at io_ompio_file_set_view.c:70-72
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous SUPPORTED.

findings.topic: 'external32' data representation (required of all implementations; portable big-endian IEEE / two's-complement conversion)
findings.gate: io/ompio (default, removable via --disable-io-ompio)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/common/ompio/common_ompio_file_view.c:122-128 (clones ompi_mpi_external32_convertor into f_file_convertor); ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_external32.c:111-117 (convertor created in datatype init); conversion applied in read/write when !OMPIO_DATAREP_NATIVE: common_ompio_file_read.c:92,447,638 and common_ompio_file_write.c:75,392,584
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous SUPPORTED. Verified the conversion is genuinely wired into both read and write data paths, not merely accepted at set_view.

findings.topic: 'internal' data representation (required named representation; standard permits implementing it as external32)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/io/ompio/io_ompio_file_set_view.c:70-72 (accepts ONLY native/NATIVE/external32/EXTERNAL32, else returns MPI_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_DATAREP -- this gate runs BEFORE mca_common_ompio_set_view); grep for 'internal' datarep handling in ompi/mca/io and ompi/mca/common/ompio returns zero matches
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT resolved against b3. b3 claimed (in caveats) 'internal' is silently treated as native via the common_ompio_file_view.c:121-131 else-branch. That is wrong: the set_view gate at io_ompio_file_set_view.c:70-72 rejects any string other than native/external32 with MPI_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_DATAREP and returns BEFORE the common backend is ever called -- so 'internal' never reaches the else-branch. b1/b2 are correct: 'internal' is a required named representation that is rejected outright. Not MISSING_INFLIGHT (neither in-flight branch adds it) and not NA (it is a required representation). A one-line alias internal->native would fix it but is absent.

findings.topic: set_view rejects unrecognized/unregistered datarep strings with MPI_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_DATAREP
findings.gate: io/ompio (default, removable via --disable-io-ompio)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/io/ompio/io_ompio_file_set_view.c:70-72 (any datarep != native/NATIVE/external32/EXTERNAL32 returns MPI_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_DATAREP)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT resolved against b3's 'set_view does not reject unregistered datarep strings' caveat (cited common_ompio_file_view.c:121-131). That caveat is bogus: the OMPIO set_view gate at io_ompio_file_set_view.c:70-72 explicitly rejects every unknown string with MPI_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_DATAREP before the common backend's else-branch is reached. Unknown datareps are NOT silently treated as native. b1's view (set_view rejects non-native/external32) is correct. Dropped b3's caveat as a misread of the call order.

findings.topic: MPI_Register_datarep: user-defined data representations (register read/write/extent converters; invoke them during access)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/io/ompio/io_ompio_component.c:363-370 (register_datarep is a hard stub: 'return OMPI_ERROR;'); OMPIO is the only io component so MPI_Register_datarep always fails; set_view independently rejects any non-native/external32 datarep (io_ompio_file_set_view.c:70-72) so a user datarep could never drive converters
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous non-functional. UNSUPPORTED (not MISSING_INFLIGHT -- neither PR #13280 (ABI) nor the ompio-info branch touches register_datarep). The MPI_Datarep_conversion_function / MPI_Datarep_extent_function typedefs and MPI_CONVERSION_FN_NULL are PRESENT in C and f08 (mpi.h.in:506-509, 1055-1056) but are dead because the registration backend always fails. b2 additionally notes a base-dispatch version guard (mca_type_major_version==2 never matches the v3 OMPIO component) that makes MPI_Register_datarep silently return MPI_SUCCESS without registering -- a second independent failure; either way the feature is absent.

findings.topic: MPI_File_set_info / get_info full MPI_Info hint round-trip (get_info returns all hints OMPIO recognized/defaulted, set via open/set_info/set_view)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/io/ompio/io_ompio_module.c:41-42 (module set_info/get_info hooks are NULL on main -> generic opal_infosubscribe fallback); ompi/mca/common/ompio/common_ompio_file_view.c registers only cb_nodes + collective_buffering into f_info (==s_info) so only those round-trip; in-flight branch pr/ompio-and-mpi-info-lets-be-friends (issue #13367) wires mca_io_ompio_file_set_info/get_info and adds comprehensive hint + default reporting
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT resolved by the framework's authoritative in-flight tiebreaker: the full MPI_Info-on-MPI_File round-trip BEHAVIOR is MISSING_INFLIGHT via issue #13367. The C/f08 SYMBOLS MPI_File_set_info/get_info ARE present and return valid (minimal) results today: get_info always yields a valid possibly-empty info from s_info, and 2 hints (cb_nodes, collective_buffering) do round-trip. On main, get_info does not list all supported/defaulted hints (cb_buffer_size, striping_factor/unit, fs hints) as 14.2.8 requires, and post-open set_info hints are not re-applied. b2's 'default' overstates (generic round-trip != spec-accurate active-hint exposure); b1's 'partial' is close but the framework classifies the gap as MISSING_INFLIGHT.

findings.topic: Reserved file info hints (access_style, collective_buffering, cb_buffer_size, cb_nodes, striping_factor/unit, etc.) recognized/honored
findings.gate: io/ompio (default); striping_* additionally consult fs/lustre when present
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/common/ompio/common_ompio_file_open.c (consults collective_buffering, cb_buffer_size, cb_nodes); ompi/mca/fs/lustre/fs_lustre_file_open.c:80 (striping_factor/striping_unit when Lustre present); unrecognized hints are ignored, which the standard permits
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT (partial-overlap) resolved to SUPPORTED. Ignoring hints an implementation does not understand is explicitly conformant (14.2.8). OMPIO consults the core collective-buffering hints (and Lustre striping hints when fs/lustre is built); the only true shortfall -- that recognized/defaulted hints are not all surfaced back via get_info -- is the same gap captured under the MISSING_INFLIGHT info round-trip topic above, not a separate conformance failure here. b1's PARTIAL conflated 'not surfaced via get_info' with 'not honored'; honoring is fine.

findings.topic: Atomic-mode consistency: MPI_File_set_atomicity / get_atomicity (default nonatomic; atomic mode enforced in access path)
findings.gate: io/ompio (default); atomic=TRUE additionally needs fbtl backend lock support (fbtl_check_atomicity) else MPI_ERR_IO
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/io/ompio/io_ompio_file_open.c:311-368 (collective bcast validation, fbtl_check_atomicity at :340, stores f_atomicity, get returns it); ompi/mca/common/ompio/common_ompio_file_open.c:465 (default f_atomicity=0, nonatomic); ompi/mca/fbtl/posix/fbtl_posix_pwritev.c:54-55 (F_WRLCK byte-range lock when f_atomicity set), preadv F_RDLCK
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous SUPPORTED. Atomicity is genuinely enforced via fcntl byte-range locks in the posix fbtl, default nonatomic matches the standard, and set_atomicity bcasts for collective [SAME] consistency. b1's 'conditional_on fs locking' is a per-filesystem runtime corner (returns MPI_ERR_IO when unsupported), not a build gate.

findings.topic: MPI_File_sync flushes data to the storage device; collective
findings.gate: io/ompio (default, removable via --disable-io-ompio)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/io/ompio/io_ompio_file_open.c:370-398 (collective coll_barrier then f_fs->fs_file_sync); default fs/ufs wires fs_file_sync=mca_fs_base_file_sync (ompi/mca/fs/ufs/fs_ufs.c:48) -> ompi/mca/fs/base/fs_base_file_sync.c:36 calls fsync(fh->fd)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous SUPPORTED. Real fsync to device, not a no-op; collective barrier present; fs/ufs has no configure gate.

findings.topic: MPI_File_get_type_extent returns datatype extent in the file's data representation
findings.gate: io/ompio (default, removable via --disable-io-ompio)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/io/ompio/io_ompio_file_open.c:302-308 (mca_io_ompio_file_get_type_extent calls opal_datatype_type_extent(&datatype->super, extent) and ignores fh->f_datarep)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT resolved to PARTIAL (b3's unique catch, verified). The implementation always returns the NATIVE extent regardless of the file's datarep, so for an external32 view with differently-sized representations it does not return the file-representation extent that 14.5.1 specifies. b1 marked it SUPPORTED, missing this. Narrow deviation: matters only for external32 views where representation sizes differ from native; for native views (the common case) it is correct.

findings.topic: Collective read byte-count (status.MPI_Count) reflects actual bytes read so EOF is detectable by short read
findings.gate: io/ompio (default, removable via --disable-io-ompio)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/common/ompio/common_ompio_file_read_all.c:139-141 (status->_ucount = max_data, the decoded requested byte count); ompi/mca/io/ompio/io_ompio_file_read.c:148-153 (status->_ucount = count * size, the requested byte count)
findings.adjudication_note: UNIQUE (b3 only), verified valid. For MPI_File_read_all / read_at_all and their split/nonblocking forms, status._ucount is set to the full requested byte count, not the bytes actually transferred, so a short read at end-of-file over-reports and the standard's 'detect EOF by amount read < requested' is broken for the collective read families. Data movement itself is correct; this is a status-reporting deviation only. Noncollective reads report real bytes. PARTIAL.

findings.topic: Per-file error handler defaults to MPI_ERRORS_RETURN; pre-handle errors use MPI_FILE_NULL
findings.evidence: ompi/file/file.c:251-263 (default file errhandler is MPI_ERRORS_RETURN via MPI_FILE_NULL: file->error_handler = ompi_mpi_file_null.file.error_handler, which initializes to &ompi_mpi_errors_return.eh)
findings.adjudication_note: b1+b2 agree SUPPORTED; b3 silent (silence is not disagreement). Verified the default file error handler is MPI_ERRORS_RETURN per 14.7, distinct from the MPI_ERRORS_ARE_FATAL default for communicators/windows.

findings.topic: Nonblocking I/O completion via MPI_Test/MPI_Wait on returned request handles
findings.gate: io/ompio (default, removable via --disable-io-ompio)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/common/ompio/common_ompio_request.c (OMPIO request objects integrate with the ompi_request progress/wait/test machinery)
findings.adjudication_note: UNIQUE (b1 only), valid. Nonblocking iread/iwrite return ompi_request_t handles completable by the standard MPI_Test/MPI_Wait family. SUPPORTED.

findings.topic: amode validation at open (exactly one of RDONLY/RDWR/WRONLY; reject RDONLY+CREATE/EXCL, RDWR+SEQUENTIAL)
findings.gate: io/ompio (default, removable via --disable-io-ompio)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/common/ompio/common_ompio_file_open.c:67-79 (exactly-one-of RDONLY/RDWR/WRONLY, RDONLY+CREATE/EXCL, RDWR+SEQUENTIAL all -> MPI_ERR_AMODE); mode constants in ompi/include/mpi.h.in:612-620
findings.adjudication_note: UNIQUE (b2 only), valid SUPPORTED. amode bit-vector validation present on every open. b2 noted (correctly) that the broader 'MPI_MODE_SEQUENTIAL restricts to shared-pointer access' aspect is not enforced here, but the cited amode-validation requirement is satisfied.

findings.topic: All I/O error classes (Table 14.5) defined
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:704-736 (all 17 classes: FILE, NOT_SAME, AMODE, UNSUPPORTED_DATAREP, UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION, NO_SUCH_FILE, FILE_EXISTS, BAD_FILE, ACCESS, NO_SPACE, QUOTA, READ_ONLY, FILE_IN_USE, DUP_DATAREP, CONVERSION, IO)
findings.adjudication_note: UNIQUE (b2 only), valid SUPPORTED. Note MPI_ERR_DUP_DATAREP is defined as a constant but never actually raised (register_datarep stub) -- the constant exists, which is what this topic requires.

findings.topic: Large-count (_c) variants of count-bearing I/O routines
findings.gate: io/ompio (default, removable via --disable-io-ompio)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/bindings/ompi_bindings/c.py (auto-generates _c variant from the single .c.in template when the prototype carries a COUNT type); _c variants funnel into the same MPI_Count-clean common_ompio backends
findings.adjudication_note: b2+b3 agree SUPPORTED. Each _c variant is exactly as conformant as its base routine (register_datarep_c inherits the same UNSUPPORTED stub behavior, captured under that topic).

findings.topic: Vendor filesystem optimization backends (Lustre / GPFS / DDN IME)
findings.evidence: ompi/mca/fs/lustre/configure.m4 (OMPI_CHECK_LUSTRE); default fs/ufs (ompi/mca/fs/ufs, no configure gate) + fbtl/posix cover generic Unix filesystems with no external lib
findings.adjudication_note: PARTIAL-OVERLAP. b1 and b3 marked the vendor backends CONDITIONAL, which is accurate FOR THE OPTIONAL TUNING but does not represent a Chapter-14 conformance gap: the standard's required I/O behavior is fully delivered by the always-built generic fs/ufs + fbtl/posix path. Lustre/GPFS/IME add performance tuning only, not required semantics. So the chapter requirement is SUPPORTED by default; the vendor backends are optional accelerators (noted as a gate on the tuning, not on conformance).

chapters.title: Tool Support
chapters.tldr: Open MPI's main branch substantially conforms to MPI-5.0 Chapter 15. The PMPI profiling interface (15.2) is fully and unconditionally SUPPORTED across all three language bindings (C, mpif-h, use-mpi-f08) via the dual profile/noprofile libtool build emitting weak MPI_* over real PMPI_* entry points, and MPI_Pcontrol is a conformant no-op. All 52 MPI_T procedures plus 3 callback typedefs are present with C bindings only (the standard defines no Fortran MPI_T bindings, so f08 absence is correct, not a gap). The cvar, pvar, enum, and category sub-interfaces are functionally backed by OPAL's mca_base_var/mca_base_pvar and are SUPPORTED, with two narrow deviations: MPI_T_pvar_readreset is an unimplemented stub (always MPI_T_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE) even though pvars are otherwise live, and MPI_T_category_get_events performs no cat_index validation. The MPI-4.0/5.0 MPI_T events sub-interface (events, event sources, callbacks, dropped-event handling, event read/copy/timestamp/source) is present as default-build symbols but functionally inert: MPI_T_event_get_num and MPI_T_source_get_num always return 0 and every index/handle query returns an error, with no mca_base_event infrastructure anywhere in the tree and no flag/component/library to enable delivery -> PARTIAL (enumeration of an empty space is permitted, but no event is ever delivered). Two MPI-5.0 error constants Table 15.7 mandates, MPI_T_ERR_NOT_ACCESSIBLE and MPI_T_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED, are genuinely absent from mpi.h.in (real header-level gaps, not in any in-flight PR).

findings.topic: PMPI name-shifted entry points (PMPI_ prefix) for every MPI/MPI_T procedure in every provided binding
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/pcontrol.c:27-32 (#pragma weak MPI_Pcontrol = PMPI_Pcontrol with #define fallback); ompi/mpi/tool/event_get_num.c:19-24 and every ompi/mpi/tool/*.c carry #pragma weak MPI_T_x = PMPI_T_x; ompi/mpi/tool/Makefile.am builds interface_profile_sources into both libmpi_mpit_profile_la (OMPI_BUILD_MPI_PROFILING=1) and libmpi_mpit_noprofile_la (=0); Fortran mpif-h profile/ and use-mpi-f08 profile variants exist
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. Verified the dual profile/noprofile build and weak-symbol-with-macro-fallback scheme produce PMPI_ entry points for C, mpif-h, use-mpi-f08, and all MPI_T routines. On platforms lacking weak symbols the #define MPI_x PMPI_x macro-rename path still yields PMPI_ symbols.

findings.topic: Profiling wrappers separable from the base library (a profiling library can be linked without name clashes)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/Makefile.am:107-110 (separate libmpi_mpit_profile.la / libmpi_mpit_noprofile.la archives); ompi/mpi/fortran/mpif-h/profile/ separate p*_f.c wrappers
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. Wrappers built as standalone libtool archives, satisfying separability; non-replaced functions link without name clash via the noprofile real-symbol library.

findings.topic: MPI_Pcontrol provided as a conformant no-op in the MPI library
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/pcontrol.c:37-53 (va_start/va_end then return MPI_SUCCESS, no side effects); f08 ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/pcontrol_f08.F90; mpif-h pcontrol_f.c. MPI_Pcontrol is the only Chapter-15 procedure with Fortran bindings.
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. Conformant no-op present in C, mpif-h, and f08.

findings.topic: MPI_Pcontrol init/finalize availability guard
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/pcontrol.c:41-43 (OMPI_ERR_INIT_FINALIZE under MPI_PARAM_CHECK)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2; verified correct. The init/finalize guard is applied when parameter checking is enabled. Valid and conformant.

findings.topic: MPI_T_init_thread / MPI_T_finalize: reference-counted, callable before MPI_Init and after MPI_Finalize, returns provided thread level
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/init_thread.c:40-73 (ompi_mpit_init_count refcount; opal_init_util initializes MCA independently of MPI_Init; ompi_mpi_thread_level returns provided level); ompi/mpi/tool/finalize.c balances init
findings.adjudication_note: b3's non_conformant flag is a minor low-impact caveat, not a real status drop: on the rare failure of opal_init_util or ompi_info_register_framework_params (init_thread.c:51-63) the routine returns MPI_T_ERR_INVALID rather than the more specific MPI_T_ERR_CANNOT_INIT. The happy path is fully conformant and MPI_T_ERR_CANNOT_INIT is itself a valid MPI_T return code; returning a less-specific-but-valid error on a hard-to-trigger failure does not make the interface non-conformant. SUPPORTED with a noted nit.

findings.topic: MPI_T enumeration types (MPI_T_enum) query: enum_get_info / enum_get_item
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/enum_get_info.c, enum_get_item.c (backed by mca_base_var_enum_t); MPI_T_ENUM_NULL present in mpi.h.in
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. Enumeration metadata is backed by the real mca_base_var_enum system.

findings.topic: MPI_T required datatype subset (Table 15.3) usable before MPI_Init
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/cvar_get_info.c / pvar_get_info.c return MPI_Datatype values; predefined datatypes are part of OMPI's always-present predefined-datatype set, available pre-MPI_Init via opal datatype init reachable from MPI_T_init_thread
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2; verified valid. The Table 15.3 datatype subset is part of the unconditionally-present predefined datatypes; no separate gate.

findings.topic: Control variables: enumerate / get_info / get_index / handle alloc-free / read / write honoring scope
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/cvar_get_num.c -> mca_base_var_get_count(); cvar_get_info.c, cvar_handle_alloc.c, cvar_read.c, cvar_write.c; CVAR_SET_NOT_NOW/NEVER and SCOPE_* constants present in mpi.h.in
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous. Functional cvar interface exposing real MCA control variables.

findings.topic: Performance variables: classes / get_info / sessions / handle alloc-free / start / stop / read / write / reset
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/pvar_get_num.c -> mca_base_pvar_get_count(); pvar_session_create.c, pvar_handle_alloc.c (mca_base_pvar), pvar_start.c/pvar_stop.c (mca_base_pvar), pvar_read.c:42 (mca_base_pvar_handle_read_value), pvar_reset.c:42-47 (mca_base_pvar_handle_reset), pvar_write.c, pvar_get_info.c; opal/mca/base/mca_base_pvar.c:681,797 implement read/reset
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict resolved to SUPPORTED for the core pvar surface. b1 marked the whole pvar topic PARTIAL solely because of MPI_T_pvar_readreset; that single stub is broken out as its own finding below. All enumeration, session, handle, start/stop, read, write, and (separate) reset operations are genuinely backed by mca_base_pvar. PVAR_CLASS_* and PVAR error classes (NO_WRITE/NO_STARTSTOP/NO_ATOMIC) are present in mpi.h.in.

findings.topic: MPI_T_pvar_readreset atomic read-and-reset
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/pvar_readreset.c:33-34 (comment '/* XXX -- TODO -- Implement me */', unconditionally returns MPI_T_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE after the init check); contrast pvar_read.c:42 and pvar_reset.c:42-47 which ARE functional; no mca_base_pvar read-and-reset primitive exists in opal/mca/base
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict resolved to PARTIAL. b2 lumped readreset into the working pvar interface, but the symbol is an unimplemented stub: it always fails with MPI_T_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE even for a valid started handle, so the standard's atomic read-and-reset is non-functional. b1 and b3 are correct. Because pvars ARE exposed in a default build and a valid handle is allocatable (unlike the events no-op), a conforming call that should read-and-reset atomically instead always fails -> present-but-deviates = PARTIAL. Not UNSUPPORTED because the symbol exists and the rest of the pvar interface works; the read and reset operations are individually available.

findings.topic: Variable categorization: category enumerate / get_info / get_cvars / get_pvars / get_categories / category_changed
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/category_get_num.c, category_get_info.c, category_get_cvars.c:44 (mca_base_var_group_get with OPAL_ERR_NOT_FOUND -> MPI_T_ERR_INVALID_INDEX), category_get_categories.c, category_changed.c (mca_base category system)
findings.adjudication_note: Unanimous for the cvar/pvar/category membership and change-counter query routines, which validate cat_index and are backed by the real mca_base_var_group system. (category_get_events validation deviation is a separate finding below.)

findings.topic: MPI_T_category_get_events: invalid cat_index validation
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/category_get_events.c:32-39 (does NO cat_index validation, returns MPI_SUCCESS for any cat_index); contrast sibling category_get_cvars.c:44-48 which maps a bad index to MPI_T_ERR_INVALID_INDEX. Standard defines cat_index range 0..num_cat-1 (for-claude/mpi-standard-5.0-apis.json:46181 desc 'in the range from 0 to num_cat-1')
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b3; verified correct. Unlike its siblings, MPI_T_category_get_events accepts any cat_index (including out-of-range) and returns MPI_SUCCESS, where the standard requires MPI_T_ERR_INVALID_INDEX for an invalid category index. The deviation is narrow because no events ever exist so indices[] is never written, but an invalid index that should be rejected is accepted -> PARTIAL (present-but-deviates).

findings.topic: MPI_T tool-info error constants MPI_T_ERR_NOT_ACCESSIBLE and MPI_T_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED defined
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in MPI_T_ERR block runs MPI_T_ERR_PVAR_NO_ATOMIC=67 (line 752) then MPI_ERR_RMA_RANGE=68 (line 753) - no slot for either constant; tree-wide grep for MPI_T_ERR_NOT_ACCESSIBLE and MPI_T_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED returns zero hits in *.c/*.h/*.in (excluding 3rd-party); pre-resolved by framework as GENUINELY MISSING
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict resolved to UNSUPPORTED. Pre-resolved fact: both MPI-5.0 Table 15.7 error constants are genuinely absent from every public Open MPI header. A return-code constant is a preprocessor #define that must exist in an installed header; no configure flag or MCA component can conjure it, so default/conditional are structurally impossible. Not in any in-flight PR (#13280 adds only MPI_ABI_VERSION/SUBVERSION/MPI_ERR_ABI; ompio-info branch adds no constants), so not MISSING_INFLIGHT. b1's PARTIAL undercounts; the constants are simply absent = UNSUPPORTED. Low real-world impact (the standard advises high-QoI implementations avoid returning NOT_ACCESSIBLE, and zero sources means NOT_SUPPORTED is currently unreachable), but the binary definition obligation is unmet.

findings.topic: MPI_T event sources: MPI_T_source_get_num / get_info / get_timestamp, ordering, ticks_per_second, max_ticks
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/source_get_num.c:36 (*num_source=0 unconditionally - the only num_source assignment in the tree); source_get_info.c and source_get_timestamp.c return MPI_T_ERR_INVALID_INDEX for any index; no source-registration framework anywhere in opal/mca/base, ompi/mca, configure, or Makefiles
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict resolved to PARTIAL. The source functions compile unconditionally into libmpi (default build, no flag) and are individually conformant for an empty space: the standard mandates no minimum source count, so num_source=0 and INVALID_INDEX for any index are correct vacuous results (b2/b3 correct on conformance-of-the-empty-space). BUT the source feature is functionally never provided - zero sources are ever registered and there is no flag/component/library path to register any - so source ordering, ticks_per_second, and max_ticks machinery is never exercised. Symbols present + permanently empty + no enable path = PARTIAL (b1 correct that it is functionally absent, though UNSUPPORTED overstates it since the empty-space behavior is standard-permitted). Aligned with the events sub-interface verdict.

findings.topic: MPI_T event types: enumerate (event_get_num/get_info/get_index) and event-registration handle alloc/free
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/tool/event_get_num.c:36 (*num_event=0 unconditionally); event_get_info.c and event_handle_alloc.c return MPI_T_ERR_INVALID_INDEX; event_get_index.c returns MPI_T_ERR_INVALID_NAME; event_handle_free.c returns MPI_T_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE; no mca_base_event infrastructure exists (mca_base_event appears ONLY in mpi.h.in/mpi.h comments, never as code)

Returning an empty event space (num_event=0) is standard-permitted, so the enumeration path is conformant-but-empty (b3's point). But there is no mca_base_event framework, no MCA component registers any event, and no configure flag/library enables event delivery - the marquee MPI-4.0/5.0 facility is permanently inert. b2 correctly noted there is NO conditional path (status missing_gap, i.e. no flag enables it); the framework taxonomy maps 'present but incomplete/not fully standard-conformant' to PARTIAL rather than UNSUPPORTED because the symbols exist and the empty-space behavior is conformant. Not MISSING_INFLIGHT: PR #13280 is the ABI chapter and adds zero event infrastructure.

Symbols present, behavior inert, no enable path -> PARTIAL, aligned with the events sub-interface verdict.

chapters.title: Deprecated Interfaces
chapters.tldr: Open MPI fully implements MPI-5.0 Chapter 16 (Deprecated Interfaces) by default on this checkout (HEAD 71d6dcf7f4). Deprecation != removal: every deprecated procedure, typedef, predefined function constant, and attribute key the chapter enumerates is present and SUPPORTED in a default build. The deprecated prototypes are declared unconditionally in ompi/include/mpi.h.in (the block beginning at line 3935), annotated with __mpi_interface_deprecated__ pointing at the modern replacement, and sit ABOVE the OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS gate at line 4080 that guards only the Chapter 17 removed symbols. b1 and b2 agree across the board (all SUPPORTED); b3 merged ch16+17 and left its behaviors array empty, addressing only a few non-procedure deprecations (typedefs, MPI_HOST, MPI_Sizeof) in caveats and (mis)labeling MPI_Sizeof config_gated. Per the framework's pre-resolved Fortran rule, MPI_Sizeof and the f08/mpif.h bindings are default-on and only removable via the optional --disable-mpi-fortran flag, so they are SUPPORTED with a gate note, not CONDITIONAL. The C-only (no-f08) absence for the attr/keyval routines and predefined function constants is exactly what the standard prescribes ('an mpi_f08 interface was never defined'), so it is conformant, not a gap. Neither in-flight item (ABI PR #13280, ompio-info #13367) touches deprecated interfaces.

findings.topic: Deprecated attribute/keyval routines (MPI_Attr_get/put/delete, MPI_Keyval_create/free) remain available and route through the same attribute engine as MPI_Comm_*_attr / MPI_Comm_*_keyval replacements
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:3939-3950 (MPI_Attr_delete/get/put unconditional, __mpi_interface_deprecated__ pointing to MPI_Comm_* replacements); MPI_Keyval_create/free declared in the same deprecated block; ompi/mpi/c/keyval_create.c.in, keyval_free.c.in, attr_put.c.in, attr_get.c.in, attr_delete.c.in (real bodies through the shared attribute backend)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 unanimous SUPPORTED; b3 silent (empty behaviors array). Verified the prototypes are unconditional (above the line-4080 OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS gate) and carry deprecation annotations naming the replacements. Deprecated != removed; MPI-5.0 still requires these names.

findings.topic: Predefined attribute-copy/delete function constants behave per standard: MPI_NULL_COPY_FN returns flag=0/SUCCESS; MPI_DUP_FN sets flag=1 and copies in->out, returns SUCCESS; MPI_NULL_DELETE_FN returns SUCCESS
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/attr_fn.c:128-148 (DUP_FN sets *flag=1, *out=in, returns MPI_SUCCESS; NULL_COPY_FN sets *flag=0/SUCCESS; NULL_DELETE_FN returns SUCCESS); ompi/include/mpi.h.in:4053-4078 (macros map MPI_*_FN to OMPI_C_MPI_*_FN, deprecation annotations)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 unanimous SUPPORTED with matching file:line evidence; b3 silent. Exact standard semantics.

findings.topic: Deprecated typedefs remain defined: MPI_Copy_function, MPI_Delete_function (used by MPI_Keyval_create), and the errhandler aliases MPI_Comm_errhandler_fn / MPI_File_errhandler_fn / MPI_Win_errhandler_fn (equivalent to the renamed *_function types)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:540-546 (the three *_errhandler_fn aliases as deprecated typedefs of *_function); mpi.h.in:4034,4037 (MPI_Copy_function/MPI_Delete_function typedefs)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 record SUPPORTED as findings; b3 addresses the same typedefs in its 'Deprecated typedefs and MPI_HOST attribute' caveat (status fully_conformant -> SUPPORTED). Plain typedef aliases with identical signatures; modern replacements also present.

findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/cancel.c.in (entry point); ompi/mca/pml/ob1/pml_ob1_sendreq.c:130-182 (send-cancel callback never grants; comment ~:173 'we dont cancel send requests by now' -> request completes normally); ompi/mca/pml/base/pml_base_frame.c:108-150 (warn_dep_cancel_send MCA var)

By the b3 normalization rule, config_gated when default-on-removable -> SUPPORTED. The standard defines no C binding, so the C-absence and the static-scan 'no f08 binding' note are by-design, not gaps; the f08 binding is generated at build time.

findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpif.h.in (file present and installed)

findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:3935-4078 (entire deprecated-prototypes block has no OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS guard); the gate begins only at mpi.h.in:4080 for the removed (Ch17) block
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 unanimous SUPPORTED. Verified directly: no Chapter 16 symbol sits inside the #if (!OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS) block. This is the structural confirmation that deprecation != removal in this tree.

chapters.title: Removed Interfaces
chapters.tldr: Chapter 17 enumerates MPI-1 bindings, datatypes, constants, a callback typedef, and the C++ bindings that were REMOVED in MPI-3.0. Per §17.1.1 these are explicitly NOT required: an implementation MAY provide them for backwards compatibility but need not. A default Open MPI build on this checkout is conformant by OMISSION: with no --enable-mpi1-compatibility (default OFF) and any modern C11/C++11 compiler, OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS=1 and the removed C names are #if'd out of mpi.h and redefined to a compile-time _Static_assert/static_assert 'was removed in MPI-3.0' diagnostic naming the MPI-2/3 replacement (mpi.h.in:332-368, 4080-4170). The framework's CONDITIONAL class is reserved for REQUIRED behavior reachable only via a non-default path; because removed interfaces are NOT required, the gated --enable-mpi1-compatibility exposure is an OPTIONAL back-compat extension, and the conformant state is the DEFAULT -> these topics are SUPPORTED (conformance-by-omission), with --enable-mpi1-compatibility recorded as a gate note for the optional restoration. This applies UNIFORMLY to the removed functions, MPI_LB/MPI_UB, MPI_COMBINER_*_INTEGER constants, MPI_Handler_function, and C++ bindings; b2's residual CONDITIONAL label on the COMBINER constants is corrected to SUPPORTED for the same reason. The C/Fortran symbol bodies are always compiled into libmpi for ABI; only header visibility is gated. One asymmetry (verified): the Fortran-side removed names MPI_LB/MPI_UB and MPI_COMBINER_*_INTEGER are defined UNCONDITIONALLY in mpif-handles.h/mpif-constants.h regardless of the flag -- conformant (the standard forbids requiring removed names, not defining extra ones). b1 and b2 cover this chapter; b3 merged ch16+17 and its flagged_symbols (in the ch16 bundle, all not_applicable -> SUPPORTED-by-omission) are b3's verdict for the removed-function topics here.

findings.topic: Removed MPI-1 C functions (MPI_Address, MPI_Errhandler_create/get/set, MPI_Type_extent/lb/ub/hindexed/hvector/struct) are correctly treated as removed by default: prototypes suppressed and any use yields a compile-time diagnostic naming the MPI-2 replacement; standard does not require them
findings.gate: --enable-mpi1-compatibility (configure flag, default OFF) restores the removed C prototypes for optional back-compat
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:4080-4157 (real prototypes under #if (!OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS), with __mpi_interface_removed__ encoding the exact Table 17.1 replacement names); mpi.h.in:4159-4170 (default: each name -> THIS_FUNCTION_WAS_REMOVED_IN_MPI30(name, replacement)); mpi.h.in:332-368,407-409 (OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS=1 by default with C11/C++11); config/ompi_configure_options.m4:182-191 (--enable-mpi1-compatibility default disabled -> OMPI_ENABLE_MPI1_COMPAT=0)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 marked the flag-restored behavior CONDITIONAL while marking the default non-exposure SUPPORTED; b2 (verification=adjusted) and b3 (not_applicable) both treat the default as conformant. ADJUDICATED to SUPPORTED: the framework's CONDITIONAL class is for REQUIRED behavior on a non-default path; §17.1.1 makes these explicitly NOT required, so the default (absence + diagnostic) IS the conformant state and the flag opts INTO an optional extension. The task instruction ('standard permits omitting removed interfaces, so not-present-by-default is conformant') overrides the framework's general listing of this flag under CONDITIONAL examples. Replacement mapping in __mpi_interface_removed__ matches Table 17.1 exactly (verified mpi.h.in:4104,4109,4122,4140,4154 etc.).

findings.topic: When --enable-mpi1-compatibility IS set, the removed C functions are usable and behave as their historical MPI-1 definitions; the symbol bodies are compiled into libmpi unconditionally for ABI regardless of the flag
findings.gate: --enable-mpi1-compatibility (configure flag, default OFF) exposes the prototypes; the symbol bodies are always linked
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/address.c (real param-checked body); ompi/mpi/c/Makefile.am:985-999 (address.c..type_ub.c added to interface_profile_sources UNCONDITIONALLY, no OMPI_ENABLE_MPI1_COMPAT AM_CONDITIONAL guard); in-file comment ~985-988 explains ABI motivation
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT: b1 split this into a CONDITIONAL row for the flag-restored behavior; b2/b3 fold it into default conformance. Resolved to SUPPORTED: this is the same requirement viewed from the optional-exposure side. The optional back-compat behavior is not a required-behavior gap; providing it under a flag is standard-permitted (§17.1.1 'may be provided ... but are not required'). Recording the flag as a gate note rather than as the basis for a CONDITIONAL status, consistent with the chapter-wide resolution.

findings.topic: Removed datatypes MPI_LB and MPI_UB are correctly treated as removed by default (replacement MPI_Type_create_resized present and unconditional); MPI_LB/MPI_UB user macros gated off, redefined to a static-assert naming the replacement
findings.gate: --enable-mpi1-compatibility (configure flag, default OFF) restores the C MPI_LB/MPI_UB macros
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:1259-1276 (MPI_LB/MPI_UB user macros only under !OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS; else THIS_SYMBOL_WAS_REMOVED_IN_MPI30(MPI_LB, MPI_Type_create_resized)); mpi.h.in:2493 (MPI_Type_create_resized declared unconditionally); ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_module.c:88-89 (ompi_mpi_lb/ub objects always exist for ABI)
findings.adjudication_note: b1/b2 SUPPORTED; b3 not_applicable (-> conformant-by-omission, same conclusion). Replacement present by default; removed names correctly diagnosed. Treated identically to the removed functions per the chapter-wide principle.

findings.topic: Removed constants MPI_COMBINER_HINDEXED_INTEGER / HVECTOR_INTEGER / STRUCT_INTEGER (no replacement) are correctly treated as removed by default; internal combiner recognition stays unconditional so Type_get_envelope/get_contents introspection of legacy-described types still works
findings.gate: --enable-mpi1-compatibility (configure flag, default OFF) restores the named C macros
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:809-850 (named C macros under #if (!OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS); else OMPI_WAS_*_INTEGER ABI placeholders; lines 845-848 static-assert removal); ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_args.c:154,173,194 (combiner values recognized unconditionally, no #if guard)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT: b2 kept these CONDITIONAL (verification=confirmed), b1 SUPPORTED, b3 not_applicable. ADJUDICATED to SUPPORTED for consistency with the chapter-wide principle: §17.1.4 removed these with no replacement and does NOT require them, so default non-exposure is the conformant state and the flag only opts into an optional, not-required restoration. b2's 'conditional' overstates the gap by the same inversion corrected for the removed functions. Internal recognition remains unconditional, so introspection of legacy types is robust by default.

findings.topic: Removed callback typedef MPI_Handler_function (replacement MPI_Comm_errhandler_function) is correctly absent by default; replacement present unconditionally
findings.gate: --enable-mpi1-compatibility (configure flag, default OFF) restores the typedef
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:4093 (typedef only inside the !OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT_DECLS block); replacement MPI_Comm_errhandler_function declared unconditionally elsewhere in mpi.h.in
findings.adjudication_note: b2's own verification verdict on this id was the (garbled) 'refuted', but its corrected reasoning and b3's flagged_symbols treatment both confirm: removed typedef absent by default, replacement present, flag re-adds the removed symbol -> conformant-by-omission = SUPPORTED. b1 did not break this out separately.

findings.topic: Fortran-side removed names (MPI_LB, MPI_UB, MPI_COMBINER_*_INTEGER) are defined UNCONDITIONALLY in the Fortran headers regardless of --enable-mpi1-compatibility (the one place removed symbols are present by default) -- conformant because the standard forbids requiring removed names, not defining extra ones
findings.gate: Fortran layer default-on, removable via --disable-mpi-fortran
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpif-handles.h:87,125,197,235 (MPI_LB/MPI_UB integer params, no OMPI_OMIT_MPI1_COMPAT guard, verified); ompi/include/mpif-constants.h:41,43,49,218,220,226 (MPI_COMBINER_*_INTEGER params, no guard, verified)

VERIFIED directly: grep shows no #if guard around these Fortran definitions, unlike the C side. Not a conformance failure -- defining removed names is permitted; only REQUIRING them is forbidden, and nothing requires them. SUPPORTED with a note.

findings.evidence: no ompi/mpi/cxx class-binding surface in the tree; no 'namespace MPI' declared; docs/building-apps/removed-mpi-constructs.rst:114-117
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 unanimous SUPPORTED; b3 silent. Nothing to provide and nothing in violation; reserved namespace requires no action.

findings.topic: mpi_f08 (an MPI-3+ construct) correctly contains NONE of the MPI-3.0-removed functions

chapters.title: Semantic Changes and Warnings
chapters.tldr: Chapter 18 introduces no new symbols; it records behavioral changes carried in from MPI-4.0/4.1 plus advice-to-users text. b1 and b2 cover it; b3 was silent on this chapter entirely (it merged 16+17 and never addressed 18) -- silence is not disagreement. Three of the four behavioral items are SUPPORTED by default: MPI_Comm_dup/idup no longer propagate input-communicator info hints (the *_with_info variants are the explicit carry path), and MPI_Wtime/MPI_Wtick (plus PMPI_ forms) and all handle-conversion functions are real OMPI_DECLSPEC functions, never macros. The one genuinely CONDITIONAL item is the MPI-4.0 default-error-communicator change: routing errors that involve no comm/win/file to MPI_COMM_SELF (instead of the legacy MPI_COMM_WORLD) is REQUIRED by MPI-5.0 and is compiled into every build, but is gated OFF by default -- the READONLY MCA parameter mpi_compat_mpi3 defaults to true (verified ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_params.c:86,366), so out of the box Open MPI raises such errors on MPI_COMM_WORLD. The conformant behavior is reachable with zero rebuild by launching with mpi_compat_mpi3=false, so it is CONDITIONAL (required behavior on a non-default path), not a gap. This is the exact inverse of the Chapter 17 gating: same mechanism (a non-default flag), opposite requiredness -> opposite status. The 30/31-character identifier-length relaxation is pure advice-to-users with no implementation obligation -> NA.

findings.topic: MPI_Comm_dup / MPI_Comm_idup do NOT propagate info hints from the input communicator to the output (MPI-4.0 semantic change); the explicit *_with_info variants are the prescribed path to carry info
findings.evidence: ompi/communicator/comm.c:1599 (ompi_comm_dup calls ompi_comm_dup_with_info(comm, NULL, ...)); comm.c:1647-1649 (info applied only when non-NULL, under if(info)); comm.c:1624 (attributes still copied, which is correct and unrelated to info); ompi/include/mpi.h.in:1633-1634 (*_with_info declared)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 unanimous SUPPORTED with matching file:line evidence; b3 silent on ch18. Plain dup/idup pass NULL info -> no source hints propagated. Conformant with the MPI-4.0 change.

findings.topic: Errors not involving a communicator, window, or file must be raised on MPI_COMM_SELF (changed from MPI_COMM_WORLD in MPI-4.0); MPI-5.0 mandates the MPI_COMM_SELF default
findings.gate: MCA runtime param mpi_compat_mpi3=false (READONLY, set at launch; default is true)
findings.evidence: ompi/errhandler/errhandler_invoke.c:54 (comm = ompi_mpi_compat_mpi3 ? &ompi_mpi_comm_world.comm : &ompi_mpi_comm_self.comm; verified); ompi/runtime/ompi_mpi_params.c:86 (bool ompi_mpi_compat_mpi3 = true) and :366-372 (re-set true, registered READONLY with help text: true=raise on MPI_COMM_WORLD/MPI-3, false=raise on MPI_COMM_SELF/MPI-4)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 unanimous CONDITIONAL; verified directly. The MPI-5.0-conformant routing to MPI_COMM_SELF is compiled in but gated OFF: mpi_compat_mpi3 defaults to true (no configure-derived override; the only routing point for the running-MPI state is line 54). Out-of-the-box, Open MPI is non-conformant on this point and raises no-handle errors on MPI_COMM_WORLD. Required behavior reachable only via a non-default runtime param = CONDITIONAL (not a gap: zero rebuild needed; not default: explicit opt-in needed). Neither in-flight PR touches this. Contrast Ch17, where the gated behavior is NOT required, so that gating yields SUPPORTED; here it IS required, so the gating yields CONDITIONAL.

findings.topic: MPI_Wtick / PMPI_Wtick / MPI_Wtime / PMPI_Wtime, and all handle-conversion functions (MPI_*_c2f / MPI_*_f2c), must NOT be implemented as macros (MPI-4.1 warning)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:2624-2625 (MPI_Wtick/MPI_Wtime as OMPI_DECLSPEC functions), 3798-3799 (PMPI_ forms), 1615/1636 (MPI_Comm_c2f/f2c); no function-like #define macro forms over these entry points (negative grep)
findings.adjudication_note: b1 and b2 unanimous SUPPORTED; b3 silent. All declared as real functions; no macro shadowing exists. Conformant.

findings.topic: The 30/31-character limit on MPI identifiers was removed (MPI-4.0 warning); a relaxation with no implementation obligation
findings.evidence: no source artifact; Open MPI never enforced a 30/31-char identifier limit and freely uses public identifiers longer than 31 chars (e.g. MPI_Comm_idup_with_info, mpi.h.in:1634)
findings.adjudication_note: CONFLICT in label only: b1 called it SUPPORTED but its own caveat says 'This is a relaxation, not an implementation requirement; no implementation constraint exists ... No single file:line implements it' -- which is exactly NA. b2 marked it NA. Resolved to NA per the framework's NA clause (advice-to-users / requirement that does not bind an implementation). The relaxation is trivially satisfied; there is nothing to implement.

chapters.title: Language Bindings
chapters.tldr: Open MPI's main branch conforms strongly to MPI-5.0 Chapter 19. All C language-binding machinery is present and unconditional: MPI_Aint/Count/Offset/Fint types, the full set of MPI_<Obj>_f2c/c2f handle converters (14 object classes), all six MPI_Status conversion routines (f2c/c2f/f082c/c2f08/f2f08/f082f), the C-side Fortran-status constants (MPI_F_STATUS_SIZE, zero-based MPI_F_SOURCE/TAG/ERROR, extern MPI_F_STATUS(ES)_IGNORE / MPI_F08_STATUS(ES)_IGNORE sentinels), large-count _c procedures and callbacks, and MPI_Aint_add/diff. All three Fortran support methods (mpi_f08 with BIND(C) %MPI_VAL derived-type handles, the mpi module, and deprecated mpif.h) are built by default whenever a Fortran compiler is present; --disable-mpi-fortran removes them (gate note only, still SUPPORTED). The headline correction across reviewers: MPI_SUBARRAYS_SUPPORTED and MPI_ASYNC_PROTECTS_NONBLOCKING are hard-coded .false. in mpif-config.h.in unconditionally -- the configure variable that would set .true. under TS-29113 reaches only Makefiles, never any Fortran source -- so the exposed values are .false. ALWAYS (not '.true. when TS detected'). That .false. is a standard-permitted honest under-claim, hence SUPPORTED. Genuinely below-SUPPORTED: extended Fortran KIND datatypes (MPI_REAL16/COMPLEX32/INTEGER16 ...) are CONDITIONAL on compiler representation support; the 6 MPI_Status_{get,set}_{source,tag,error} f08 bindings are genuinely UNSUPPORTED (C bindings present, no *_f08.F90); and the f08 standard-ABI compiler wrapper / ABI Fortran bindings are MISSING_INFLIGHT (PR #13280, really Chapter 20 surface).

findings.topic: C handle conversion MPI_<Obj>_f2c / _c2f for all opaque-object types (comm, datatype, errhandler, file, group, info, message, op, request, session, win), null->null / invalid->invalid
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:1615-2595 (prototypes); generated implementations ompi/mpi/c/{comm,type,errhandler,file,group,info,message,op,request,session,win}_{f2c,c2f}_generated.c (all present, verified by directory listing)
findings.adjudication_note: Correctly C-only (no f08 binding). All 14 object classes x {c2f,f2c} present and built unconditionally on the C side.

findings.topic: C status conversion: all six routines MPI_Status_f2c/c2f/f082c/c2f08/f2f08/f082f preserving hidden count/elements/cancellation info, with MPI_STATUS_IGNORE handling
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:2400-2405 (all six prototypes); generated impls ompi/mpi/c/status_{f2c,c2f,f082c,c2f08,f2f08,f082f}_generated.c
findings.adjudication_note: All six directions present in C and built unconditionally.

findings.topic: MPI_Status_f2f08 / MPI_Status_f082f callable from both the mpi_f08 and the mpi modules (excluded only from deprecated mpif.h, as the standard requires)
findings.gate: --disable-mpi-fortran removes the Fortran modules
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/status_f2f08_f08.F90 (mpi_f08); ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-ignore-tkr/mpi-ignore-tkr-status.h:15-34 (use mpi); C entry points always present at ompi/include/mpi.h.in:2403,2405
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict: b1 marked CONDITIONAL 'Fortran compiler present'. Per framework + pre-resolved fact, default-on-but-removable = SUPPORTED with gate note; the Fortran-compiler prerequisite is intrinsic (like a C compiler for C bindings), not a conditional trigger. b2's SUPPORTED is correct. Correctly excluded from mpif.h per spec.

findings.topic: C-side Fortran status constants: MPI_F_STATUS_SIZE, zero-based indices MPI_F_SOURCE/TAG/ERROR (one less than Fortran), and extern sentinels MPI_F_STATUS(ES)_IGNORE / MPI_F08_STATUS(ES)_IGNORE
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:602 (MPI_F_STATUS_SIZE), :603-605 (MPI_F_SOURCE=0/TAG=1/ERROR=2), :1246-1250 (extern MPI_Fint* sentinels)
findings.adjudication_note: Indices are 0/1/2 as required; sentinels are extern globals (not constant expressions), matching the standard's runtime-global wording.

findings.topic: mpi_f08 module: BIND(C) derived-type handles (TYPE(MPI_Comm) etc.) with single integer MPI_VAL component, TYPE(MPI_Status) with MPI_SOURCE/TAG/ERROR, .EQ./.NE. handle-comparison operator overloads, ISO_C_BINDING usage
findings.gate: --disable-mpi-fortran removes the mpi_f08 module; mpi_f08 additionally needs compiler BIND(C)/ISO_C_BINDING/ignore-TKR support (present in all modern Fortran compilers, attempted by default)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi/mpi-types.F90.in:45-86 (type, BIND(C) :: MPI_Comm { integer :: MPI_VAL } and the other 10 handle types), :27 (TYPE(MPI_Status)), :124 (.EQ. overload); ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/mod/mpi-f08-types.F90:24 (use ISO_C_BINDING), :25 (use mpi_types)
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict: b1 CONDITIONAL on Fortran compiler; corrected to SUPPORTED per default-on-removable rule. mpi_f08 is built by default; --disable-mpi-fortran removal is a gate note only.

findings.topic: All three Fortran support methods provided: USE mpi_f08, USE mpi, INCLUDE mpif.h (implementation must provide mpi_f08 and/or the mpi+mpif.h pair); built by default when a Fortran compiler is found
findings.gate: --disable-mpi-fortran removes all Fortran support
findings.evidence: config/ompi_setup_mpi_fortran.m4 builds mpif.h, use-mpi-ignore-tkr (use mpi), and use-mpi-f08 (mpi_f08); default enable_mpi_fortran (empty) sets OMPI_TRY_FORTRAN_BINDINGS to the full mpi_f08 level per config/ompi_configure_options.m4:125-130 (help text :107 'default: yes if Fortran compiler found')
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict: b1 CONDITIONAL, b2/b3 SUPPORTED. b2's 'adjusted->default' verdict is right: all three methods built by default; --disable-mpi-fortran is a removing flag, not a conditional trigger. SUPPORTED with gate note.

findings.topic: MPI_SUBARRAYS_SUPPORTED and MPI_ASYNC_PROTECTS_NONBLOCKING compile-time LOGICAL constants set per Fortran support method without over-claiming
findings.gate: --disable-mpi-fortran removes the constants (Fortran-only)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpif-config.h.in:81-86 hard-codes parameter(MPI_SUBARRAYS_SUPPORTED=.false.) and parameter(MPI_ASYNC_PROTECTS_NONBLOCKING=.false.) as literals (NOT @...@ substitutions); this header is included by all three methods (verified: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/mod/mpi-f08-types.F90:27 includes mpif-config.h). config/ompi_setup_mpi_fortran.m4:462,477-478 sets OMPI_MPI_SUBARRAYS_SUPPORTED=.true. under TS-29113 and AC_SUBSTs it (:480), but the variable lands ONLY in generated Makefiles (e.g. ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/mod/Makefile:1046), never in any Fortran source.
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict resolved against b1. b1 claimed CONDITIONAL/.true. when TS detected; that is factually refuted -- on this very checkout the Makefiles show .true. (TS WAS detected) yet the exposed Fortran constant in mpif-config.h is still .false.. The AC_SUBST var is dead w.r.t. the source declaration. So the user-visible value is .false. UNCONDITIONALLY (refining the task hint '.false. unless TS29113'). Per §19.1.6 a degraded ignore-TKR scheme legitimately reports .false. (honest under-claim, not over-claim); the constants are present and correctly valued => SUPPORTED. The dead configure variable is a code-quality wart, not a conformance gap.

findings.topic: f08 nonblocking/persistent choice-buffer bindings provided (assumed-type ignore-TKR TYPE(*),DIMENSION(*) + ASYNCHRONOUS attribute scheme; _f08ts specific procedures generated)
findings.gate: --disable-mpi-fortran removes the f08 bindings
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/*_ts.c.in templates and *_f08.F90 specific procedures; ompi/mpi/fortran/configure-fortran-output.h defines the ignore-TKR choice-buffer type used by the f08 bindings
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict over the MECHANISM (b1 calls it scheme-1B DIMENSION(..); b3 calls it scheme-1A ignore-TKR DIMENSION(*)+ASYNCHRONOUS). Given MPI_SUBARRAYS_SUPPORTED=.false. is the exposed value, b3's scheme-1A read is the consistent one. Either way the binding is PROVIDED and built by default => SUPPORTED; the mechanism distinction does not change conformance status. Collapsed into one topic.

findings.topic: MPI_F_SYNC_REG no-op register/memory-synchronization helper (Fortran-only), implemented so the compiler cannot optimize away the empty body
findings.gate: --disable-mpi-fortran removes it (Fortran-only)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/f_sync_reg_f08.F90:15; f_sync_reg_ts.c.in; pre-resolved fact: Fortran-only (no C variant)
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict: b1 CONDITIONAL on Fortran compiler. b2's 'adjusted->default' verdict is correct: present, real implementation, built by default; --disable-mpi-fortran is a removing flag. SUPPORTED with gate note.

findings.topic: MPI_Sizeof (deprecated, Fortran-only) generic numeric-size subroutine
findings.gate: --disable-mpi-fortran removes it (Fortran-only)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/sizeof_f08.f90 (generated per-type); ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-ignore-tkr/mpi-ignore-tkr-sizeof.f90; pre-resolved fact: Fortran-only
findings.adjudication_note: b2's 'adjusted->default' is correct: present, Fortran-only, built by default. SUPPORTED with gate note (deprecated but provided).

findings.topic: MPI_Type_create_f90_{real,complex,integer} return matching predefined unnamed datatypes (by p,r); erroneous/MPI_ERR_ARG for unsupported (p,r); MPI_Type_match_size switches on MPI_TYPECLASS_*
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/type_create_f90_real_generated.c:52-131 (and complex/integer counterparts); ompi/mpi/c/type_match_size_generated.c; ompi/mpi/c/type_create_f90_real.c.in
findings.adjudication_note: Real implementations (not stubs). Both-undefined p,r returns MPI_ERR_ARG; otherwise maps to existing predefined family member.

findings.topic: F90-created datatypes are predefined (un-freeable, need not be committed), same handle returned for same (XXX,p,r) via hash cache, MPI_TYPE_GET_ENVELOPE returns MPI_COMBINER_F90_* exposing p,r; external32 representation for F90 types incl. 16-byte double-extended
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/type_create_f90_real_generated.c:99-127 (hash-table cache keyed on (p,r), OMPI_DATATYPE_FLAG_PREDEFINED, set_args with MPI_COMBINER_F90_REAL); ompi/include/mpi.h.in:831-833 (MPI_COMBINER_F90_*); ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_external32.c:27-117 (external32 convertor encoding the 16-byte Double-Extended format)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2; valid. Implements the recommended hash-table reuse, predefined flag, special-combiner envelope, and external32 packing as the standard describes. SUPPORTED.

findings.topic: Large-count C procedures: _c-suffixed routines with MPI_Count/MPI_Aint/MPI_Offset count and displacement params; large-count callbacks (MPI_User_function_c, MPI_Datarep_conversion_function_c) and MPI_CONVERSION_FN_NULL_C; MPI_Op_create_c / MPI_Register_datarep_c directly callable
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:449-451 (MPI_Aint/Offset/Count typedefs), :509-1056 (large-count callbacks), :1460-1538 (_c prototypes), :2142 (MPI_Op_create_c); MPI_Aint_add/diff at :1443-1444
findings.adjudication_note: Large-count C API present and unconditional. The _c routines themselves belong to their home chapters; ch19 governs the binding rules, which are met.

findings.topic: Fortran large-count via mpi_f08 interface polymorphism (specific _c procedure with KIND=MPI_COUNT_KIND/ADDRESS_KIND/OFFSET_KIND); _c interfaces correctly absent from use mpi and mpif.h
findings.gate: --disable-mpi-fortran removes the f08 large-count interfaces
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/mod/mpi-f08-interfaces-generated.h (MPI_Send_c_f08ts, MPI_Type_create_struct_c_f08 etc.); ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/base/bigcount.h
findings.adjudication_note: Conflict: b1 CONDITIONAL on Fortran compiler. Corrected to SUPPORTED (default-on-removable). Polymorphic _c specific procedures exist for mpi_f08, correctly absent from use mpi / mpif.h per 19.2.

findings.topic: PMPI name-shift (profiling interface) for every routine in C and for Fortran (f08 + mpif.h) specific procedure names
findings.gate: Fortran PMPI removed by --disable-mpi-fortran; C PMPI always present
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:2789-3769 (C PMPI_* prototypes, e.g. PMPI_Status_c2f at :3574); ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/mod/pmpi-f08-interfaces.F90 (Fortran PMPI)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1, marked CONDITIONAL. The C PMPI layer is unconditional; the Fortran PMPI layer is default-on-removable. Net status SUPPORTED with gate note on the Fortran side.

findings.topic: Size-specific / extended Fortran KIND datatypes: MPI_REAL16, MPI_COMPLEX32, MPI_INTEGER16, MPI_LOGICAL1..16 etc. usable for communication
findings.gate: Fortran compiler must provide each specific representation (e.g. OMPI_HAVE_FORTRAN_REAL16); unsupported representations initialize to OMPI_DATATYPE_INIT_UNAVAILABLE
findings.evidence: ompi/datatype/ompi_datatype_module.c:168-234 (e.g. ompi_mpi_complex32, ompi_mpi_logical1..8 initialized via OMPI_DATATYPE_INIT_UNAVAILABLE macro by default, populated only when the compiler provides the representation)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1, status correct. Confirmed by pre-resolved fact: extended KINDs are CONDITIONAL on compiler representation support. Symbols always present but may be unavailable. This is the one genuinely CONDITIONAL topic in the chapter.

findings.topic: mpi_f08 bindings for the 6 status accessors MPI_Status_{get,set}_{source,tag,error}
findings.evidence: C bindings present: ompi/mpi/c/status_get_source_generated.c, status_set_source_generated.c (and tag/error). NO f08 binding: ompi/mpi/fortran/use-mpi-f08/ contains status_set_cancelled_f08.F90 and status_set_elements_x_f08.F90 but NO status_{get,set}_{source,tag,error}_f08.F90 (verified by directory listing)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b3 (it filed it as an awareness caveat since these procedures' home chapter is point-to-point, but the task instructs surfacing it here). Pre-resolved fact confirms the gap is genuine and NOT in-flight. The MPI-4.0/5.0 standard defines f08 bindings for these; their absence is a real f08-binding gap => UNSUPPORTED. C bindings exist and work.

findings.topic: f08 standard-ABI compiler wrapper (mpifort_abi) and ABI Fortran bindings
findings.evidence: docs/release-notes/mpi-5.0.rst:115 (documents absence); in-flight via PR #13280 (https://github.com/open-mpi/ompi/pull/13280)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1, marked UNSUPPORTED. Flipped per the authoritative in-flight tiebreaker: ALL ABI surface (Chapter 20, including the standard-ABI Fortran wrapper) is MISSING_INFLIGHT via PR #13280, never UNSUPPORTED. This is really Chapter 20 surface listed here only because b1 raised it.

findings.topic: win_f2c implemented as a plain handwritten file rather than a generated .c.in (cosmetic naming difference)
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/c/win_f2c.c (plain file with manual PMPI weak-symbol handling, vs the *_f2c_generated.c form used by sibling converters)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b3; valid cosmetic note. Behavior is identical and conformant; only the file naming differs from its siblings. SUPPORTED.

findings.topic: Interlanguage interoperability: init/finalize from any language affects all; opaque objects, MPI_Get_address, info contents, predefined reduce ops behave identically across languages; relaxed interlanguage type matching
findings.evidence: Single underlying library/runtime design: ompi/runtime/, ompi/datatype/ shared across all language layers; MPI_Get_address and predefined datatypes/ops are the same objects regardless of calling language
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2; valid and inherent to Open MPI's single-runtime architecture. MPI_BOTTOM / MPI_STATUS_IGNORE may legitimately differ in value between C and Fortran as the standard permits. SUPPORTED.

chapters.title: Application Binary Interface (ABI)
chapters.tldr: MPI-5.0 Chapter 20 defines an opt-in standard ABI: fixed integer/Huffman-coded handle and constant values, an 8-int (32-byte) C MPI_Status, fixed integer typedefs, version/info query functions, Fortran type/boolean registration functions, MPI_<Type>_toint/_fromint serializers for all 11 handle types (22 functions), the MPI_ABI_VERSION/MPI_ABI_SUBVERSION/MPI_ERR_ABI constants, and dedicated libmpi_abi / mpifort_abi libraries plus an mpicc_abi wrapper. The ENTIRE chapter is absent on main and is supplied by in-flight PR #13280 (https://github.com/open-mpi/ompi/pull/13280): verified zero hits for MPI_Abi_get/set, the 22 *_toint/*_fromint converters, and MPI_ABI_VERSION/SUBVERSION/ERR_ABI across ompi/, and no abi/toint/fromint binding files in ompi/mpi/c/. Every requirement is therefore MISSING_INFLIGHT (never UNSUPPORTED — in-flight, not abandoned). Open MPI's native ABI legitimately differs from the standard ABI (pointer-valued handles to ompi_*_t tags, a 24-byte LP64 status with size_t _ucount, MPI_STATUS_SIZE=6, error classes ending at MPI_ERR_ERRHANDLER=80/LASTCODE=92); the standard explicitly permits this parallel-ABI coexistence (20.1, 20.2), so those differences are context, not gaps. Draft generator stubs at ompi/mpi/bindings/ompi_bindings/c.py:262-264 (MPI_Abi_details/supported/version) use pre-standard names, are unwired, and satisfy no chapter requirement.

findings.topic: Standard-ABI header (mpi.h) declaring the full ABI surface; excludes pre-MPI-3.1 deprecated features
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in (single native header; no separate standard-ABI header; no MPI_ABI_* tags / *_toint / *_fromint / MPI_ABI_VERSION declared)
findings.adjudication_note: Token conflict (b1 UNSUPPORTED vs b2 MISSING_INFLIGHT); b1 itself carried in_flight=#13280 on every item but its rubric lacks a missing_inflight bucket. Resolved by the in-flight tiebreaker (canonical Ch20 case): the standard-ABI header is added by PR #13280, so MISSING_INFLIGHT, not UNSUPPORTED. Native default mpi.h.in is the implementation-specific ABI, which 20.1/20.2 permit to coexist.

findings.topic: MPI_Abi_get_version + MPI_ABI_VERSION / MPI_ABI_SUBVERSION macros (returns ABI major/minor, -1 if unsupported; thread-safe; callable any time)
findings.evidence: grep -rIiE 'MPI_Abi_(get|set)' ompi/ → 0 hits; grep -nE 'MPI_ABI_VERSION|MPI_ABI_SUBVERSION' ompi/include/mpi.h.in → 0 hits; no abi binding file in ompi/mpi/c/
findings.adjudication_note: b2/b3 MISSING_INFLIGHT vs b1 UNSUPPORTED (token only). Resolved by in-flight tiebreaker → MISSING_INFLIGHT. The draft generator stub MPI_Abi_version at ompi/mpi/bindings/ompi_bindings/c.py:264 uses pre-standard naming (no 'get'), is unwired, and does NOT make the conformant symbol present — the standardized function/macros are added by PR #13280.

findings.topic: MPI_Abi_get_info (info object with keys mpi_aint_size / mpi_count_size / mpi_offset_size; thread-safe; callable any time)
findings.evidence: no MPI_Abi_get_info in ompi/include/mpi.h.in or ompi/mpi/c/ (grep 0 hits); no binding file
findings.adjudication_note: Token conflict resolved by in-flight tiebreaker → MISSING_INFLIGHT. Predefined info-key values are asserted by PR #13280, not independently verifiable on this checkout; the symbol is verifiably absent.

findings.topic: Standard-ABI library naming (libmpi_abi as sole direct dependency of the app binary)
findings.evidence: ompi/Makefile.am builds libmpi only; no libmpi_abi target on main
findings.adjudication_note: Token conflict resolved by in-flight tiebreaker → MISSING_INFLIGHT. PR #13280 adds libmpi_abi (ompi/mpi/c/Makefile_abi.include).

findings.topic: mpicc_abi (standard-ABI compiler wrapper)
findings.evidence: ompi/tools/wrappers/ has only mpicc-wrapper-data.txt[.in]; no mpicc_abi wrapper data
findings.adjudication_note: Framed as advice-to-implementors but concretely part of PR #13280 (mpicc_abi-wrapper-data.txt.in). Kept MISSING_INFLIGHT (not NA) to keep the in-flight sweep consistent, since the artifact ships in the PR.

findings.topic: C MPI_Status standard-ABI layout (struct of exactly 8 ints: MPI_SOURCE, MPI_TAG, MPI_ERROR, int MPI_internal[5]; 32 bytes LP64)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:475-487 struct ompi_status_public_t = {int MPI_SOURCE,MPI_TAG,MPI_ERROR; int _cancelled; size_t _ucount} = 24 bytes LP64, NOT the 8-int/32-byte standard layout
findings.adjudication_note: Token conflict resolved by in-flight tiebreaker → MISSING_INFLIGHT. Native 24-byte status is a permitted parallel-ABI realization (20.1/20.2), not a violation; the 8-int standard-ABI status is supplied by PR #13280. Exact PR layout asserted, not verified on this checkout.

findings.topic: Opaque handles as incomplete-struct pointers with MPI_ABI_* tag names (all 11 handle types: Comm, Datatype, Group, Request, File, Win, Op, Info, Errhandler, Message, Session)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:452-468 native tags (typedef struct ompi_communicator_t *MPI_Comm, ompi_datatype_t *MPI_Datatype, ...); no MPI_ABI_Comm/MPI_ABI_Datatype tags present (grep 0 hits)
findings.adjudication_note: Native handles already use the incomplete-struct-pointer model (structurally ABI-compatible) but with native struct tags. The standard-ABI MPI_ABI_* tag names are absent and added by PR #13280 → MISSING_INFLIGHT (in-flight tiebreaker over b1's UNSUPPORTED).

findings.topic: Predefined handle constants as compile-time integer expressions cast to handle type (Huffman-coded values 1..4095; reserved range never used for user handles; integer 0 never valid)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:1282-1283 MPI_COMM_WORLD = OMPI_PREDEFINED_GLOBAL(...) (address of ompi_mpi_comm_world global), not a 1-4095 integer cast
findings.adjudication_note: Native predefined handles are link-time global-object pointers, not the standard ABI's compile-time Huffman integers — a permitted parallel-ABI difference. The standard fixed values are supplied by PR #13280 → MISSING_INFLIGHT. Full constant-value tables asserted, not independently verifiable here.

findings.topic: Standard-ABI integer constant scheme + MPI_ERR_ABI error class (group-unique values, unused values reserved to 16384; MPI_ANY_SOURCE etc. negative; MPI_ERR_ABI defined)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:767,772 native error classes end at MPI_ERR_ERRHANDLER=80, MPI_ERR_LASTCODE=92; grep -nE 'MPI_ERR_ABI' ompi/include/mpi.h.in → 0 hits
findings.adjudication_note: MPI_ERR_ABI absent; native error-class numbering is implementation-specific and need not follow the standard-ABI scheme (permitted parallel-ABI difference). Token conflict (b1 UNSUPPORTED) resolved by in-flight tiebreaker → MISSING_INFLIGHT; PR #13280 adds MPI_ERR_ABI and the reserved scheme.

findings.topic: Standard-ABI integer types (MPI_Aint=intptr_t, MPI_Offset=int64_t, MPI_Count=int64_t)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h native typedefs (ptrdiff_t / long long) are size-equivalent on LP64 but are the native-ABI forms, not the standard-ABI fixed intptr_t/int64_t typedefs
findings.adjudication_note: Native typedefs are configuration-dependent and size-equivalent but not the standard-ABI realization; the fixed forms are part of the standard-ABI header added by PR #13280 → MISSING_INFLIGHT (in-flight tiebreaker over b1's UNSUPPORTED).

findings.topic: Predefined datatype / op / handle constant values follow Huffman category-instance tables (Tables 20.1-20.11; fixed-size types encode log2(size) in bits 5:3)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in native datatypes/ops are pointers to ompi_predefined_datatype_t / ompi_predefined_op_t globals (OMPI_PREDEFINED_GLOBAL), no Huffman encoding
findings.adjudication_note: Merges b1's two Huffman-table items (datatypes vs ops/handles). Native predefined handles are global-object pointers, not Huffman integers — permitted parallel-ABI difference. Tables supplied by PR #13280 → MISSING_INFLIGHT. Table values asserted, not independently verifiable here.

findings.topic: Optional predefined datatype runtime detection (every predefined datatype has an ABI value; absent optional types detected when MPI_Type_size returns MPI_UNDEFINED)
findings.evidence: standard-ABI header (with all predefined datatype constant values present) is absent on main
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b2; valid. This runtime-detection behavior is meaningful only under the standard ABI where every predefined datatype constant is defined; that header is supplied by PR #13280 → MISSING_INFLIGHT.

findings.topic: MPI_Abi_set_fortran_info / MPI_Abi_get_fortran_info (convey Fortran type sizes & *_supported flags via predefined info keys; first SET wins, later SET calls return MPI_ERR_ABI)
findings.evidence: grep -rIiE 'MPI_Abi_(get|set)' ompi/ → 0 hits; no abi_*_fortran_info binding file in ompi/mpi/c/
findings.adjudication_note: Both functions absent on main. Token conflict (b1 UNSUPPORTED) resolved by in-flight tiebreaker → MISSING_INFLIGHT. PR #13280 adds them; b3 notes the Fortran ABI registration path is incomplete even in that PR. Key set and first-call-wins semantics asserted, not verified here.

findings.topic: MPI_Abi_set_fortran_booleans / MPI_Abi_get_fortran_booleans (convey LOGICAL size and .TRUE./.FALSE. literals with is_set flag; first SET wins, later return MPI_ERR_ABI)
findings.evidence: grep -rIiE 'MPI_Abi_(get|set)' ompi/ → 0 hits; no abi_*_fortran_booleans binding file in ompi/mpi/c/
findings.adjudication_note: Both functions absent on main. Token conflict resolved by in-flight tiebreaker → MISSING_INFLIGHT. PR #13280 adds them; Fortran ABI path incomplete in that PR. Semantics asserted, not verified here.

findings.topic: Standard-ABI Fortran modules (mpi, mpi_f08) and library mpifort_abi following 20.2.1 naming/versioning/dependency rules
findings.evidence: ompi/mpi/fortran/ builds native libmpi_mpifh and use-mpi-f08 only; no mpifort_abi target on main
findings.adjudication_note: Token conflict resolved by in-flight tiebreaker → MISSING_INFLIGHT. b2/b3 caveat: PR #13280's enumerated artifacts emphasize the C ABI (libmpi_abi, mpicc_abi) and the Fortran ABI (mpifort_abi + full type registration) is incomplete even in the PR; still classified MISSING_INFLIGHT since the chapter target is in-flight, not abandoned.

findings.topic: Handle serialization MPI_<Type>_toint / MPI_<Type>_fromint for all 11 handle types (22 functions): predefined handles map to fixed Annex A values, user handles map deterministically outside reserved range, do not depend on MPI_Fint; erroneous on invalid/freed handles
findings.evidence: grep -rIE 'MPI_(Comm|Type|Group|Request|File|Win|Op|Info|Errhandler|Message|Session)_(toint|fromint)' ompi/ → 0 hits; no *_toint/*_fromint binding files in ompi/mpi/c/
findings.adjudication_note: Collapses all 22 converters (b3's 22 flagged symbols + b1's item) into one topic. 11 handle types / 22 functions (Comm, Datatype, Group, Request, File, Win, Op, Info, Errhandler, Message, Session); b2's '12 classes / 24 functions' over-counts and was not propagated. All absent on main; C-only by design (depends only on C features, not MPI_Fint). Token conflict resolved by in-flight tiebreaker → MISSING_INFLIGHT; added by PR #13280.

findings.topic: Standard-ABI Fortran status layout (bind(C) type of 8 INTEGERs; MPI_STATUS_SIZE=8, MPI_F_STATUS_SIZE=8, MPI_F_SOURCE/TAG/ERROR=0/1/2)
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpif-config.h:53 MPI_STATUS_SIZE=6 (NOT 8); ompi/include/mpi.h.in:603-605 MPI_F_SOURCE/TAG/ERROR=0/1/2 (coincidentally match); no 8-INTEGER bind(C) standard-ABI status type present
findings.adjudication_note: b1 marked PARTIAL because the index constants (0/1/2) match while the size (6 vs 8) and 8-INTEGER bind(C) layout do not. Per the in-flight tiebreaker this does NOT become PARTIAL: the matching MPI_F_SOURCE/TAG/ERROR=0/1/2 is coincidental native behavior, not partial standard-ABI support, and the conforming 8-INTEGER layout + MPI_STATUS_SIZE=8 are supplied entirely by PR #13280 → MISSING_INFLIGHT.

findings.topic: ABI rule: MPI_F_STATUS_IGNORE / MPI_F08_STATUS_IGNORE sentinels NOT specified in the standard-ABI C header
findings.evidence: ompi/include/mpi.h.in:1246,1249 native MPI_F_STATUS_IGNORE / MPI_F08_STATUS_IGNORE ARE declared in the C header (opposite of the ABI rule)
findings.adjudication_note: Unique to b1; valid. Native behavior declares these sentinels in the C header — the opposite of the 20.4.4 ABI rule — but that is permitted native ABI, not a violation. The standard-ABI C header that omits them is part of PR #13280 → MISSING_INFLIGHT. (b1's original mpif-sentinels.h:54-56 citation was inaccurate per b1's own corrected caveat; that file holds the unrelated MPI_STATUS_IGNORE array sentinel.)
